title: Async panic reported complete
description:
Observed while running `make shatter` in `/home/ketan/project/zolem` on 2026-05-12. During exploration of `internal/fixture/watcher.go::StartWatcher`, the target process printed a nil-pointer panic from a spawned goroutine:

`panic: runtime error: invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference`
`zolem.dev/zolem/internal/fixture.watchLoop(... watcher.go:114 ...)`

Immediately afterward, Shatter wrote the `StartWatcher` artifact and emitted progress status `completed` for `StartWatcher`. The final report shows `StartWatcher` as completed with 92.86% coverage.

This is a correctness problem: asynchronous goroutine panics from explored executions should be captured as failures or at least surfaced as unsafe behavior, not classified as successful completion.
acceptance_criteria:
If an explored Go execution panics in any goroutine, the function result records a failure or explicit panic finding.
The progress stream and final report do not mark that function as completed/successful without surfacing the panic.
Add a Go frontend regression test where the function starts a goroutine that panics after return or shortly before return.

title: Rust 1.93 clippy breakage on shatter-core
description:
Rust 1.93.0 toolchain introduced new clippy lints that now error on shatter-core --all-targets:

- 17 errors: approximate_constants (f32/f64 PI literals in tests — should use std::f64::consts::PI)
- 1 error: overly_complex_bool_expr (test_runner.rs:699 — actual boolean logic simplification)
- 60 warnings (useless_vec, etc)

Files affected:
- shatter-core/src/export.rs (lines 968, 1085, 1179, 1188)
- shatter-core/src/float_probe.rs (line 329)
- shatter-core/src/mock_fixture.rs (lines 1002, 1008)
- shatter-core/src/orchestrator.rs (line 2253)
- shatter-core/src/pipeline.rs (line 1493)
- shatter-core/src/protocol.rs (line 2364)
- shatter-core/src/shrink.rs (line 883)
- shatter-core/src/sym_expr.rs (line 197)
- shatter-core/src/test_arbitraries.rs (lines 60, 76)
- shatter-core/src/test_runner.rs (line 699, logic bug)
- shatter-core/src/triage.rs (lines 801, 805)

This is blocking merge-gate verification for multiple ready branches.

Acceptance:
- cargo clippy -p shatter-core --all-targets is clean
- All existing tests pass
- npx task test-standard is green on main

title: TS numeric schema accepts floats
description:
# Summary
TypeScript batch analysis fails before exploration when the frontend emits floating-point numeric literals such as `Number.MAX_VALUE` but the orchestrator schema expects `i64`.

## Protocol-Visible Behavior
- Does this change the wire format or semantics of any protocol message? yes
- If yes, which commands or fields are affected: TypeScript frontend numeric literal or value fields deserialized by the orchestrator
- Is this change backward-compatible? yes

## Parity Impact
- [ ] `buildSymExpr` (instrumentor.ts) — update `buildSymExprWithFlow` to handle the same node type
- [ ] `buildSymExprWithFlow` (instrumentor.ts) — update `buildSymExpr` to handle the same node type
- [x] Random explorer (`shatter-core/src/explorer.rs`) — verify concolic orchestrator (`orchestrator.rs`) gets the same change
- [x] Concolic orchestrator (`shatter-core/src/orchestrator.rs`) — verify random explorer (`explorer.rs`) gets the same change
- [ ] CLI wiring in `main.rs` — verify both `--concolic` and default explorer paths receive the new config or flag

## Default Behavior
- Is new functionality enabled by default? yes
- If no, what flag or config option enables it:

## Required Test Updates
- [ ] Unit tests added or updated
- [ ] Property tests (proptest / fast-check / rapid) added for any new public function
- [ ] E2E concolic tests verified (`cargo test --test e2e_concolic`)
- [ ] Conformance tests verified (`npx task conformance`) — required if protocol-visible
- [ ] Walkthrough updated (`demo/walkthrough.sh`) if the compact demo narrative changes
- [ ] Gauntlet updated (`demo/gauntlet.sh`) or equivalent targeted coverage added for non-demo CLI changes

## Contract Updates
- Are runtime contracts (`#[requires]` / `#[ensures]`) needed? yes
- If yes, identify the trust boundary that justifies them (Z3 FFI / subprocess JSON / cross-collection index): subprocess JSON between TS frontend and Rust orchestrator
- Confirm all three qualifying criteria are met: trust boundary, type gap, silent corruption (see `shatter-core/CLAUDE.md`)

## Reproduction
- Scan a TS file that references `Number.MAX_VALUE`, such as lodash `src/toFinite.ts`.
- Observe: `invalid type: floating point ..., expected i64` during batch analysis.

## Acceptance
- The orchestrator accepts floating-point numeric values from the TS frontend.
- Previously failing files like `toFinite.ts` analyze successfully.
- Tests cover float-valued protocol payloads.

title: Go frontend emits args field
description:
# Summary
Go scans fail during batch analysis because the Go frontend omits the required `args` field in its response JSON, blocking all Go target analysis before any function is explored.

## Protocol-Visible Behavior
- Does this change the wire format or semantics of any protocol message? yes
- If yes, which commands or fields are affected: Go frontend batch-analysis response objects; required `args` field in function metadata or call records
- Is this change backward-compatible? yes

## Parity Impact
- [ ] `buildSymExpr` (instrumentor.ts) — update `buildSymExprWithFlow` to handle the same node type
- [ ] `buildSymExprWithFlow` (instrumentor.ts) — update `buildSymExpr` to handle the same node type
- [x] Random explorer (`shatter-core/src/explorer.rs`) — verify concolic orchestrator (`orchestrator.rs`) gets the same change
- [x] Concolic orchestrator (`shatter-core/src/orchestrator.rs`) — verify random explorer (`explorer.rs`) gets the same change
- [ ] CLI wiring in `main.rs` — verify both `--concolic` and default explorer paths receive the new config or flag

## Default Behavior
- Is new functionality enabled by default? yes
- If no, what flag or config option enables it:

## Required Test Updates
- [ ] Unit tests added or updated
- [ ] Property tests (proptest / fast-check / rapid) added for any new public function
- [ ] E2E concolic tests verified (`cargo test --test e2e_concolic`)
- [ ] Conformance tests verified (`npx task conformance`) — required if protocol-visible
- [ ] Walkthrough updated (`demo/walkthrough.sh`) if the compact demo narrative changes
- [ ] Gauntlet updated (`demo/gauntlet.sh`) or equivalent targeted coverage added for non-demo CLI changes

## Contract Updates
- Are runtime contracts (`#[requires]` / `#[ensures]`) needed? yes
- If yes, identify the trust boundary that justifies them (Z3 FFI / subprocess JSON / cross-collection index): subprocess JSON between Go frontend and Rust orchestrator
- Confirm all three qualifying criteria are met: trust boundary, type gap, silent corruption (see `shatter-core/CLAUDE.md`)

## Reproduction
- Scan a Go target such as `go-humanize` `comma.go` or `lo` `concurrency.go`.
- Observe: `failed to deserialize response: missing field \`args\`` during batch analysis.

## Acceptance
- Go frontend always emits `args`, including `[]` for zero-argument cases.
- Batch analysis succeeds for previously failing Go targets.
- Protocol or conformance coverage catches future missing-field regressions.

## Notes
This folds in the broader contract-divergence concern from PARSE-001. Track the concrete end-to-end failure here rather than as a separate duplicate issue.

title: Build concolic orchestrator (single-function exploration loop)
description: Implement the core concolic loop in shatter-core/src/orchestrator.rs: (1) generate initial inputs, (2) execute function via frontend, (3) collect branch decisions and symbolic constraints, (4) negate uncovered branch constraints, (5) solve for new inputs via Z3, (6) repeat until coverage plateau or budget exhausted. Uses a worklist of branch targets ordered by novelty. This is the heart of shatter's exploration engine.

title: Rust while-let instrumentation
description:
## Context

The Pickpackit image upload route still cannot execute after Axum `Multipart`
extractor classification/request synthesis work begins, because the Rust
instrumenter emits invalid Rust for an async `while let` condition.

This is a separate gap from multipart request construction: the generated Axum
harness reaches compilation, but the instrumented source fails because the
`while let Some(field) = ...` condition is transformed into a plain expression
that starts with `let`, and the loop body then references a pattern binding that
was never introduced.

## Reproduction

From a Shatter worktree, run the multipart harness regression test introduced
while working on `str-62pj`. At filing time, this regression test exists only
on the `str-62pj-axum-multipart-extractor` feature branch; the smaller
instrumentation regression for this issue should be added on this issue's own
branch before or alongside the fix.

```bash
SHATTER_BUILD_TIMEOUT=300 cargo test --manifest-path shatter-rust/Cargo.toml multipart -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture
```

Observed failing test:

```text
executor::tests::execute_axum_handler_sends_default_multipart_file_part
```

The temporary handler used by that test contains:

```rust
use axum::extract::Multipart;

pub async fn upload(mut multipart: Multipart) -> String {
    while let Some(field) = multipart.next_field().await.unwrap() {
        let name = field.name().unwrap_or("").to_string();
        let filename = field.file_name().unwrap_or("").to_string();
        let bytes = field.bytes().await.unwrap();
        return format!("{}:{}:{}", name, filename, bytes.len());
    }
    "empty".to_string()
}
```

The generated temporary crate fails with errors like:

```text
error: expected expression, found `let` statement
while { let __shatter_cond = let Some (field) = multipart.next_field().await.unwrap(); ... }

error[E0425]: cannot find value `field` in this scope
```

## Pickpackit Evidence

Pickpackit has the same shape in `api/src/handlers/item_images.rs`:

```rust
async fn read_file_part(multipart: &mut Multipart) -> ApiResult<Vec<u8>> {
    while let Some(field) = multipart
        .next_field()
        .await
        .map_err(|e| ApiError::BadRequest(format!("invalid multipart: {e}")))?
    {
        if field.name() == Some("file") {
            ...
        }
    }
    ...
}
```

That helper is called by `upload_item_image`, so the whole-corpus Pickpackit
handler scan cannot complete this route until `while let` instrumentation
preserves the pattern binding and emits compilable code.

## Desired Behavior

The Rust frontend should instrument `while let` conditions without converting
the `let` pattern into an invalid expression. The generated code should compile,
preserve the pattern binding inside the loop body, and still record loop/branch
execution consistently with ordinary `while` loops.

## Protocol-Visible Behavior

- Does this change protocol message shape? no
- Is cross-frontend protocol parity affected? no
- Is this Rust frontend behavior only? yes

## Scope

In scope:

- Fix Rust instrumentation for `while let` loop conditions.
- Add a regression test that fails on current invalid generated code and passes
  after the fix.
- Verify the multipart harness regression from `str-62pj` can compile past the
  `while let` handler shape.

Out of scope:

- Full multipart support or request construction changes; those remain covered
  by `str-62pj`.
- Reworking all loop instrumentation.
- Non-trivial Pickpackit feature-code changes.

## Acceptance Checks

- A focused Rust test covers an async or non-async function containing
  `while let Some(x) = ...` and proves Shatter emits compilable instrumented
  code with `x` visible in the loop body and a `loop_enter` event still emitted
  for the loop.
- `SHATTER_BUILD_TIMEOUT=300 cargo test --manifest-path shatter-rust/Cargo.toml multipart -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` no longer fails with `expected expression, found let statement` or missing `field` binding.
- The fix is limited to Rust frontend instrumentation/harness test code and
  does not modify Pickpackit feature behavior.

title: Go overlay go.mod update fails
description:
Title: Go overlay go.mod update fails

Type: bug
Priority: 1
Labels: go,kapow-validation,overlay

## Problem

Broad Shatter scans of Kapow Go modules fail many otherwise-discovered functions during launcher build with:

```text
go: updates to go.mod needed, but go.mod is part of the overlay specified with -overlay
```

This is a Shatter-owned Go launcher/build failure. It prevents Kapow whole-corpus validation from approaching the requested 90% execution target and turns large parts of the corpus into `InstrumentationFailed` results before behavior exploration can run.

## Current Evidence

After building `/home/ketan/project/shatter/target/release/shatter`, run from the target module root:

```bash
SHATTER_ARTIFACT_DIR=/tmp/kapow-tools-kapow-artifacts-20260531 \
SHATTER_HARNESS_CACHE=/tmp/kapow-tools-kapow-harness-20260531 \
SHATTER_GO_WORKSPACE_ROOT=/tmp/kapow-tools-kapow-workspace-20260531 \
/home/ketan/project/shatter/target/release/shatter scan . \
  --project-dir /home/ketan/project/kapow/tools/kapow \
  --language go --all --no-cache --parallelism 8 \
  --request-timeout 180 --timeout-total 1800 \
  --exclude '**/.shatter/**' --exclude '**/.shatter-cache/**' \
  --exclude '**/.shatter-launchers/**' --exclude '**/shatter-artifacts/**' \
  --exclude '**/*.pb.go' --exclude '**/*_gen.go' \
  --exclude '**/zz_generated*.go' --exclude '**/generated.go' \
  --output /tmp/kapow-tools-kapow-scan-20260531.md \
  --output /tmp/kapow-tools-kapow-scan-20260531.json \
  --timing summary --timing-output /tmp/kapow-tools-kapow-timing-20260531.json \
  --timing-format json --log-level warn
```

Observed result in `/tmp/kapow-tools-kapow-scan-20260531.json`:

- `total_discovered_functions`: 1130
- `completed_functions`: 342
- `failed_functions`: 628
- `unsupported_functions`: 160
- The dominant failure text appears about 430 times in the failed reasons:
  `go: updates to go.mod needed, but go.mod is part of the overlay specified with -overlay`

A smaller module reproduces the same failure class:

```bash
SHATTER_ARTIFACT_DIR=/tmp/kapow-tools-dbverify-artifacts-20260531 \
SHATTER_HARNESS_CACHE=/tmp/kapow-tools-dbverify-harness-20260531 \
SHATTER_GO_WORKSPACE_ROOT=/tmp/kapow-tools-dbverify-workspace-20260531 \
/home/ketan/project/shatter/target/release/shatter scan . \
  --project-dir /home/ketan/project/kapow/tools/dbverify \
  --language go --all --no-cache --parallelism 4 \
  --request-timeout 120 --timeout-total 600 \
  --exclude '**/.shatter/**' --exclude '**/.shatter-cache/**' \
  --exclude '**/.shatter-launchers/**' --exclude '**/shatter-artifacts/**' \
  --output /tmp/kapow-tools-dbverify-scan.json
```

Observed result from the module batch summary:

- `total_discovered_functions`: 87
- `completed_functions`: 2
- `failed_functions`: 82
- Representative failed function: `/home/ketan/project/kapow/tools/dbverify/content.go::ensureOnPath`
- Representative failed reason:

```text
error: exploration error: unexpected response from frontend: execute error (InstrumentationFailed): build failed: build: compilation failed for <hash>: launcher: go build: exit status 1
go: updates to go.mod needed, but go.mod is part of the overlay specified with -overlay
```

## Current Code Facts

- `shatter-go/launcher/launcher.go` builds launchers with `go build -mod=mod -buildvcs=false` and appends `-overlay <overlayPath>` when a wrapper or augmented `go.mod` overlay is needed.
- The same file's `buildCombinedOverlay` overlays the target module's `go.mod` with an augmented copy when `UseHarnessLoop` is enabled.
- `writeAugmentedGoMod` currently adds `require shatter-harness v0.0.0`, adds a `replace shatter-harness => <HarnessRuntimeDir>`, and bumps the `go` directive high enough for the harness module. It does not ensure that the overlaid module graph is otherwise tidy for modules whose original `go.mod` needs updates under `-mod=mod`.
- The failure happens before function execution, so it is not caused by Kapow feature logic.

## Desired Behavior

Shatter should build Go launchers without asking the Go tool to update an overlaid `go.mod`, or should generate an augmented module overlay that is already acceptable to `go build`. Kapow modules that currently fail with this `go.mod` overlay error should proceed to execution or fail for more specific function-level reasons.

## Acceptance Checks

- Add a focused regression fixture or test that reproduces a launcher build where `go build -mod=mod -overlay <go.mod overlay>` would try to update the overlaid `go.mod`.
- The fix makes that fixture pass without modifying the target module's real `go.mod` or `go.sum`.
- A targeted Kapow scan of `tools/dbverify` no longer has the `go: updates to go.mod needed, but go.mod is part of the overlay specified with -overlay` failure class.
- A targeted Kapow scan of `tools/kapow` has zero occurrences of the failure text `go: updates to go.mod needed, but go.mod is part of the overlay specified with -overlay`; any remaining build failures must have different, more specific root causes.

## In Scope

- Go launcher build arguments and overlay generation.
- Augmented `go.mod` / module graph handling in generated Shatter workspace files.
- Tests in `shatter-go` that cover the failure without depending on Kapow.
- Targeted Kapow validation runs after the fix.

## Out of Scope

- Changing Kapow feature code.
- Solving unrelated execution timeouts or unsupported opaque parameter types.
- Solving the separate target-working-directory side effect where executed functions write fuzz output into the target repo.
- Solving the separate API generated enum corruption failures around `internal/search/field_name_gen.go`.

title: Go scan sandbox fan-out hang
description:
Title: Go scan hangs on sandbox fan-out

Type: bug
Priority: 1
Labels: go,sandbox,scan

## Problem

A whole-corpus Go scan of Zolem can stop making progress while Shatter and Go
frontend subprocesses remain alive. Per-function/request timeouts do not cleanly
finish the scan, and stale Docker sandbox containers remain running until the
operator interrupts the run.

## Reproduction

From `/home/ketan/project/shatter` on main commit
`fdd809731ff2e84ed9ff9ca3aad1059e1cb957a8`, after building
`shatter-cli` release, run:

```bash
SHATTER_SANDBOX_BACKEND=docker \
SHATTER_SANDBOX_DOCKER_IMAGE=golang:1.26-bookworm \
SHATTER_ARTIFACT_DIR=/tmp/zolem-shatter-artifacts-main-proof2 \
SHATTER_HARNESS_CACHE=/tmp/zolem-shatter-harness-main-proof2 \
SHATTER_GO_WORKSPACE_ROOT=/tmp/shatter-go-workspaces-main-proof2 \
/tmp/shatter-target-zolem-main-proof/release/shatter scan \
  --project-dir /home/ketan/project/zolem \
  --language go \
  --all \
  --no-cache \
  --timeout-total 7200 \
  --timeout-per-fn 180 \
  --timeout-explore 180 \
  --request-timeout 180 \
  --build-timeout 180 \
  --exec-timeout 30 \
  --output /tmp/zolem-main-proof2.md \
  --output /tmp/zolem-main-proof2.json \
  .
```

## Observed Evidence

- The scan discovered 360 functions, selected 326, and skipped 34 unsupported
  signatures.
- Artifacts were written through index 00281; `sleepContext` and
  `loadOpenAPIDocument` both wrote artifacts in this run.
- The artifact directory contained 280 per-function JSON files:
  `/tmp/zolem-shatter-artifacts-main-proof2/scan-results/f567f4913aa8e0851e9373cffbe6e3254468c7e6aab90ea2a4aa9856c329dc4a/functions`.
- No aggregate outputs were written at `/tmp/zolem-main-proof2.json` or
  `/tmp/zolem-main-proof2.md`.
- Artifact timestamps stopped advancing for roughly 40 minutes while the
  top-level `shatter scan` process, Go frontend subprocesses, and 13 Docker
  sandbox containers remained alive.
- `pstree -ap <shatter-pid>` showed the top-level scan waiting under multiple
  `go-frontend-b8a...` children with Docker sandbox children.
- Manual interruption and container cleanup were required.

## Expected Behavior

When a Go function, frontend request, build, or sandbox execution exceeds the
configured timeout, Shatter should record the function as failed/skipped,
terminate associated frontend/sandbox subprocesses, continue the scan, and
eventually write aggregate Markdown/JSON outputs. `--timeout-total` should also
abort the run and clean child processes instead of leaving a silent live scan.

## Acceptance Criteria

- A reproduction run with Docker sandboxing and explicit timeouts exits without
  manual intervention, even if tail functions hang or exceed timeout.
- Timed-out functions are represented in the final scan JSON/Markdown failure
  accounting.
- No Docker sandbox containers or orphan Go frontend/harness subprocesses remain
  after timeout or scan completion.

## In Scope

- Timeout propagation and cleanup for Go frontend requests and Docker sandbox
  executions.
- Scan finalization when a worker/frontend child is stuck.
- A focused regression test or harness-level test that proves cleanup/final
  accounting for a hung Go execution path.

## Out of Scope

- Improving Zolem coverage or adding new type factories.
- Changing Zolem feature code.
- Broad redesign of the scan scheduler beyond what is needed to make timeout
  and cleanup behavior reliable.
acceptance_criteria: No Docker sandbox containers or orphan Go frontend/harness subprocesses remain after timeout or completion
notes: Additional evidence from a second run: reran from /home/ketan/project/zolem with --parallelism 1 and fresh proof3 temp directories. The run again selected 326/360 functions and skipped the same 34 unsupported signatures. It wrote 276 per-function artifacts and reached internal/specs/openapi.go::loadOpenAPIDocument, but no aggregate /tmp/zolem-main-proof3.json or .md was written. The top-level command exited with signal 9 before --timeout-total 7200, after the same late OpenAPI/spec region. No Docker containers were left behind in this second run; the remaining visible go-frontend processes belonged to a separate Kapow workspace and were left untouched.

title: Pickpackit resource helper skips
description:
Title: Pickpackit resource helper skips
Type: task
Labels: rust,pickpackit,scan

## Context

The whole-corpus Pickpackit handler scan includes public SQL helper functions in handler modules as exported scan targets. Two of these helpers take `&PgPool` and are not standalone request entrypoints, so Shatter reports them as JSON-harness failures even though the route handlers that call them execute.

## Reproduction

From the Pickpackit validation worktree:

```bash
cd /home/ketan/.local/share/worktrees/pickpackit/shatter-str-l5ru-validation/api
PATH=/tmp/shatter-target-pickpackit-root/release:/tmp/shatter-target-pickpackit-rust/release:$PATH \
DATABASE_URL=postgres://pickpackit:pickpackit@localhost:55432/pickpackit \
SHATTER_RUST_SRC=/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-rust \
SHATTER_RUNTIME_PATH=/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-rust-runtime \
SHATTER_HARNESS_CACHE=/tmp/shatter-harness-pickpackit-full-main-1 \
SHATTER_BUILD_TIMEOUT=300 CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 \
shatter scan src/handlers --project-dir . --require-rust \
  --timeout-total 3600 --timeout-per-fn 300 --timeout-explore 240 \
  --request-timeout 300 --exec-timeout 240 --build-timeout 300 \
  --max-iterations 1 --progress --color never \
  -o /tmp/shatter-pickpackit-full-handlers-main-1.json
```

Observed failures:

```text
src/handlers/legs.rs::fetch_legs_for_workspace
unsupported: crate_bridge: function fetch_legs_for_workspace is not JSON-harness compatible: function parameters are not JSON-harness compatible and may not implement DeserializeOwned

src/handlers/tasks.rs::fetch_tasks_for_workspace
unsupported: crate_bridge: function fetch_tasks_for_workspace is not JSON-harness compatible: function parameters are not JSON-harness compatible and may not implement DeserializeOwned
```

## Current Code Facts

- Pickpackit validation config path: `/home/ketan/.local/share/worktrees/pickpackit/shatter-str-l5ru-validation/api/.shatter/config.yaml`.
- Shatter config supports per-function `skip: true` through `FunctionConfig.skip` in `shatter-core/src/config.rs`; the scan path checks `resolved.skip` in `shatter-cli/src/commands/explore.rs` and logs `Skipping ... (skip=true in config)`.
- Existing Pickpackit validation config uses scan-style function keys under `functions:`, for example `"**/bundles.rs::get_bundle"`.
- `src/handlers/legs.rs::fetch_legs_for_workspace` signature:

```rust
pub async fn fetch_legs_for_workspace(
    pool: &PgPool,
    workspace_id: Uuid,
) -> sqlx::Result<Vec<TripLeg>>
```

- The route handler `list_workspace_legs` calls `fetch_legs_for_workspace(&state.pool, workspace_id)` and completed in the same full scan.
- `src/handlers/tasks.rs::fetch_tasks_for_workspace` signature:

```rust
pub async fn fetch_tasks_for_workspace(
    pool: &PgPool,
    workspace_id: Uuid,
) -> ApiResult<Vec<TripTask>>
```

- The route handler `list_workspace_tasks` calls `fetch_tasks_for_workspace(&state.pool, workspace_id)` and completed in the same full scan.
- These helpers are public because application code reuses them, but they are resource-bound helper functions rather than independently callable request entrypoints.

## Desired Behavior

The Pickpackit validation setup should avoid counting these resource-bound public helpers as failed feature-code entrypoints. A justified Shatter config skip is acceptable for this issue if it records that the code is covered indirectly by the corresponding route handlers. A Shatter-side classification improvement is also acceptable if it is narrow and well tested.

## Protocol-Visible Behavior

- Does this change the wire format or semantics of any protocol message? no
- If yes, which commands or fields are affected: N/A
- Is this change backward-compatible? N/A

## Parity Impact

- [ ] `buildSymExpr` (instrumentor.ts) - N/A
- [ ] `buildSymExprWithFlow` (instrumentor.ts) - N/A
- [ ] Random explorer (`shatter-core/src/explorer.rs`) - N/A
- [ ] Concolic orchestrator (`shatter-core/src/orchestrator.rs`) - N/A
- [ ] CLI wiring in `main.rs` - verify no new CLI/config option is required, or wire both paths if one is added

## Default Behavior

- Is new functionality enabled by default? N/A for config-only skip; yes if implemented in Shatter classification
- If no, what flag or config option enables it: Pickpackit `.shatter/config.yaml` skip entries if config-only

## Required Test Updates

- [ ] Config-only path: run a whole Pickpackit handler scan showing these helpers are skipped/excluded with documented reasons, not failed
- [ ] Shatter-code path: unit tests added or updated for the scan classification change
- [ ] E2E concolic tests verified where relevant
- [ ] Conformance tests verified if protocol-visible behavior changes

## Contract Updates

- Are runtime contracts needed? no
- If yes, identify the trust boundary that justifies them: N/A
- Confirm all three qualifying criteria are met: N/A

## Acceptance Checks

- `fetch_legs_for_workspace` and `fetch_tasks_for_workspace` are no longer counted as failed functions in the whole Pickpackit `src/handlers` scan.
- Any skip/exclusion is documented with a reason tying each helper to its covering route handler.
- No Pickpackit feature code is deleted or behaviorally changed.

## Out of Scope

- Broad public/private API refactors in Pickpackit.
- Hiding arbitrary exported functions without justification.
- General support for constructing live `&PgPool` parameters for direct crate-bridge function calls.

title: Axum multipart extractor support
description:
Title: Axum multipart extractor support
Type: feature
Labels: rust,axum,pickpackit,input-generation

## Context

The whole-corpus Pickpackit handler scan now reaches most exported handler code, but the image upload route still fails before execution because Shatter's Rust Axum adapter reports `Multipart` as an unsupported extractor.

## Reproduction

From the Pickpackit validation worktree:

```bash
cd /home/ketan/.local/share/worktrees/pickpackit/shatter-str-l5ru-validation/api
PATH=/tmp/shatter-target-pickpackit-root/release:/tmp/shatter-target-pickpackit-rust/release:$PATH \
DATABASE_URL=postgres://pickpackit:pickpackit@localhost:55432/pickpackit \
SHATTER_RUST_SRC=/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-rust \
SHATTER_RUNTIME_PATH=/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-rust-runtime \
SHATTER_HARNESS_CACHE=/tmp/shatter-harness-pickpackit-full-main-1 \
SHATTER_BUILD_TIMEOUT=300 CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 \
shatter scan src/handlers --project-dir . --require-rust \
  --timeout-total 3600 --timeout-per-fn 300 --timeout-explore 240 \
  --request-timeout 300 --exec-timeout 240 --build-timeout 300 \
  --max-iterations 1 --progress --color never \
  -o /tmp/shatter-pickpackit-full-handlers-main-1.json
```

Observed summary:

```text
Scanning 75 exported functions
Scan complete: 71 completed, 4 failed, 0 unsupported, 0 skipped
```

The multipart-specific failure is:

```text
src/handlers/item_images.rs::upload_item_image
unsupported: axum handler has unsupported extractor types: Multipart
```

## Current Code Facts

- Shatter's Axum extractor recognizer includes `"Multipart"` in `AXUM_EXTRACTOR_TYPES` at `shatter-rust/src/adapters.rs`, but `execute_with_adapter` rejects mappings whose kind is `Unsupported` with the message `axum handler has unsupported extractor types: ...`.
- The existing adapter test coverage in `shatter-rust/src/adapters.rs` currently asserts that `Multipart` is reported as unsupported, so this issue should update that expectation or add replacement coverage.
- Pickpackit route signature:

```rust
pub async fn upload_item_image(
    State(state): State<AppState>,
    current: CurrentAccount,
    Path((workspace_id, item_id)): Path<(Uuid, Uuid)>,
    mut multipart: Multipart,
) -> ApiResult<Json<Item>>
```

- The handler expects multipart/form-data with a `file` part, processes the uploaded bytes through Pickpackit's image pipeline, stores WebP renditions, updates the item row, and enqueues the previous image prefix for cleanup.
- Other routes in `src/handlers/item_images.rs`, including `delete_item_image`, are now executable by Shatter.

## Desired Behavior

Shatter's Rust Axum harness should support enough multipart request construction to execute handlers that use `axum::extract::Multipart`, including a generated or recipe-provided file part. A minimal image upload payload is acceptable if it exercises the handler without requiring external feature-code changes in Pickpackit.

## Protocol-Visible Behavior

- Does this change the wire format or semantics of any protocol message? no
- If yes, which commands or fields are affected: N/A
- Is this change backward-compatible? N/A

## Parity Impact

- [ ] `buildSymExpr` (instrumentor.ts) - N/A
- [ ] `buildSymExprWithFlow` (instrumentor.ts) - N/A
- [ ] Random explorer (`shatter-core/src/explorer.rs`) - N/A
- [ ] Concolic orchestrator (`shatter-core/src/orchestrator.rs`) - N/A
- [ ] CLI wiring in `main.rs` - verify no new CLI/config option is required, or wire both paths if one is added

## Default Behavior

- Is new functionality enabled by default? yes
- If no, what flag or config option enables it: N/A

## Required Test Updates

- [ ] Unit tests added or updated for Rust Axum extractor classification/request building
- [ ] Property tests added if a new public helper is introduced
- [ ] E2E concolic tests verified where relevant
- [ ] Conformance tests verified if protocol-visible behavior changes
- [ ] Gauntlet or equivalent targeted coverage added for this route shape

## Contract Updates

- Are runtime contracts needed? no
- If yes, identify the trust boundary that justifies them: N/A
- Confirm all three qualifying criteria are met: N/A

## Acceptance Checks

- A focused scan or harness test for an Axum handler with `Multipart` completes instead of failing extractor classification.
- Pickpackit `src/handlers/item_images.rs::upload_item_image` executes in the validation worktree without non-trivial Pickpackit feature-code changes.
- The whole `src/handlers` Pickpackit scan no longer has a multipart-extractor failure.

## Out of Scope

- Full browser-equivalent multipart fuzzing.
- Changes to Pickpackit image upload feature behavior.
- Supporting every possible multipart field schema in this issue.

title: Rust direct native params
description:
Title: Rust direct native params
Type: feature
Labels: rust,pickpackit,input-generation

## Context

The whole-corpus Pickpackit handler scan fails on the `session` route because Shatter classifies it as a direct Rust function and requires JSON-deserializable parameters. The route takes Pickpackit's `CurrentAccount` auth extractor directly, and the validation config already has a native generator for `CurrentAccount`.

## Reproduction

From the Pickpackit validation worktree:

```bash
cd /home/ketan/.local/share/worktrees/pickpackit/shatter-str-l5ru-validation/api
PATH=/tmp/shatter-target-pickpackit-root/release:/tmp/shatter-target-pickpackit-rust/release:$PATH \
DATABASE_URL=postgres://pickpackit:pickpackit@localhost:55432/pickpackit \
SHATTER_RUST_SRC=/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-rust \
SHATTER_RUNTIME_PATH=/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-rust-runtime \
SHATTER_HARNESS_CACHE=/tmp/shatter-harness-pickpackit-full-main-1 \
SHATTER_BUILD_TIMEOUT=300 CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 \
shatter scan src/handlers --project-dir . --require-rust \
  --timeout-total 3600 --timeout-per-fn 300 --timeout-explore 240 \
  --request-timeout 300 --exec-timeout 240 --build-timeout 300 \
  --max-iterations 1 --progress --color never \
  -o /tmp/shatter-pickpackit-full-handlers-main-1.json
```

Observed failure:

```text
src/handlers/session.rs::session
unsupported: crate_bridge: function session is not JSON-harness compatible: function parameters are not JSON-harness compatible and may not implement DeserializeOwned
```

## Current Code Facts

- Pickpackit validation config path: `/home/ketan/.local/share/worktrees/pickpackit/shatter-str-l5ru-validation/api/.shatter/config.yaml`.
- Relevant config defaults:

```yaml
defaults:
  generators:
    State: generators/pickpackit.rs
    CurrentAccount: generators/pickpackit.rs
  param_generators:
    current: generators/pickpackit.rs
```

- Shatter resolves param/type generator config into `ExploreConfig.value_sources` in `shatter-cli/src/commands/explore.rs`, but direct JSON-harness compatibility can still reject non-Deserialize params before a native generator constructs them.
- Pickpackit route source:

```rust
use axum::Json;
use crate::{auth::CurrentAccount, domain::Account, error::ApiResult};

pub async fn session(current: CurrentAccount) -> ApiResult<Json<Account>> {
    Ok(Json(Account::from(current)))
}
```

- Pickpackit validation config already registers native generators for `CurrentAccount` and the `current` parameter through `.shatter/config.yaml`.
- Axum handlers with explicit `State`, `Path`, and `Json` extractors can now use native generators, but direct Rust function execution still rejects non-JSON params before native generation can construct them.

## Desired Behavior

Shatter should be able to execute direct Rust functions whose parameters are supplied by configured native generators, or otherwise classify this common auth-extractor-only Axum route shape into the Rust Axum harness path. The fix should make `session(CurrentAccount)` executable without changing Pickpackit feature code.

## Protocol-Visible Behavior

- Does this change the wire format or semantics of any protocol message? no
- If yes, which commands or fields are affected: N/A
- Is this change backward-compatible? N/A

## Parity Impact

- [ ] `buildSymExpr` (instrumentor.ts) - N/A
- [ ] `buildSymExprWithFlow` (instrumentor.ts) - N/A
- [ ] Random explorer (`shatter-core/src/explorer.rs`) - N/A
- [ ] Concolic orchestrator (`shatter-core/src/orchestrator.rs`) - N/A
- [ ] CLI wiring in `main.rs` - verify no new CLI/config option is required, or wire both paths if one is added

## Default Behavior

- Is new functionality enabled by default? yes
- If no, what flag or config option enables it: N/A

## Required Test Updates

- [ ] Unit tests added or updated for native generator dispatch in the chosen execution path
- [ ] Property tests added if a new public helper is introduced
- [ ] E2E concolic tests verified where relevant
- [ ] Conformance tests verified if protocol-visible behavior changes
- [ ] Targeted Pickpackit validation scan confirms `session` completes

## Contract Updates

- Are runtime contracts needed? no
- If yes, identify the trust boundary that justifies them: N/A
- Confirm all three qualifying criteria are met: N/A

## Acceptance Checks

- A focused test covers a Rust function or route with a non-Deserialize parameter supplied by native generation.
- Pickpackit `src/handlers/session.rs::session` completes in the validation worktree without non-trivial Pickpackit feature-code changes.
- The whole `src/handlers` Pickpackit scan no longer reports a JSON-harness compatibility failure for `session`.

## Out of Scope

- General automatic construction of arbitrary non-JSON Rust types without configured native generators.
- Pickpackit auth or session behavior changes.
- Protocol format changes.

title: Go interface{} candidates
description:
Title: Go interface{} candidates
Tracker: Beads
Repo: shatter
Type: feature
Priority: 1
Labels: go,input-generation,zolem,scan

## Problem

Zolem whole-corpus Shatter execution is below the requested 90% executable
corpus threshold because the Go frontend still treats empty interface values as
opaque. A fresh non-cached Zolem scan on 2026-05-31 completed successfully, but
left 45 unsupported functions:

```json
{
  "total_discovered_functions": 360,
  "attempted_functions": 315,
  "completed_functions": 314,
  "failed_functions": 1,
  "skipped_functions_count": 45,
  "unsupported_functions": 45,
  "completed_with_behavior": 304,
  "completed_error_only": 10
}
```

That is 315/360 attempted (87.5%) and 314/360 completed (87.2%). The largest
single Shatter-owned unsupported family is `interface` / `any`, appearing in 14
unsupported Zolem functions. Supporting those would push the Zolem scan above
90% attempted coverage before addressing remaining project-specific constructor
types.

## Smallest Known Reproduction

From current `~/project/shatter` main and `~/project/zolem` main, run:

```bash
SHATTER_SANDBOX_BACKEND=docker \
SHATTER_SANDBOX_DOCKER_IMAGE=golang:1.26-bookworm \
SHATTER_ARTIFACT_DIR=/tmp/zolem-shatter-artifacts-full-fresh \
SHATTER_HARNESS_CACHE=/tmp/zolem-shatter-harness-full-fresh \
SHATTER_GO_WORKSPACE_ROOT=/tmp/shatter-go-workspaces-full-fresh \
~/project/shatter/target/release/shatter scan \
  --project-dir ~/project/zolem \
  --language go \
  --all \
  --no-cache \
  --progress \
  --output /tmp/zolem-full-fresh.md \
  --output /tmp/zolem-full-fresh.json \
  .
```

Inspect unsupported functions:

```bash
jq -r '.codebase.skipped_functions[]? | select(.reason | test("interface|any")) | [.function_name, .reason] | @tsv' /tmp/zolem-full-fresh.json
```

Current affected Zolem examples include:

- `decodeRequestJSON`: `param "v" -> interface`
- `loadSpecs`: validator schema internals containing `[] -> interface`
- `(adminClient).getJSON`: `param "out" -> interface`
- `(adminClient).putJSON`: `param "in" -> interface`; `param "out" -> interface`
- `(adminClient).doJSON`: `param "out" -> interface`
- `(*WatcherConfig).logf`: variadic/log args containing `[] -> interface`
- `marshalResponseEvents`: event `_value` fields containing `interface`
- `LoadProviderSchema`, `lookupRequestSchema`, and `normalizeSchemaRef`: OpenAPI/JSON-schema structures containing `interface`

## Desired Behavior

The Go frontend should treat `interface{}` / `any` as a bounded dynamically
typed value surface instead of an opaque blocker. It should generate a small,
deterministic candidate set of JSON-like concrete values where the expected type
is empty interface:

- `nil`
- booleans
- representative strings
- representative numbers
- `[]any` with a small element set
- `map[string]any` with a small key/value set

Where the surrounding expected shape narrows the value, Shatter should prefer
that narrower shape. For example, fields inside JSON/OpenAPI schema maps should
favor JSON-compatible maps/slices/scalars, and pointer/output parameters should
receive addressable concrete values when the function is expected to unmarshal
or assign into them.

The candidate set must be bounded to avoid exploding path counts.

## Current Code Facts

- The Zolem scan already exercises whole-corpus Go execution with Docker sandboxing.
- Shatter now supports exact Go runtime values through `.shatter/config.yaml`
  `go_runtime_values`; Zolem uses that for `fixture.CompiledModule`.
- `parse.Node` is no longer a current Zolem skip in dry-run evidence, so this issue should not bundle template parse-node support.
- The remaining `interface` problem is Shatter-owned generic Go input generation, not Zolem feature-code behavior.

## Acceptance Checks

- Add focused Go frontend coverage for functions that take `interface{}` / `any` directly and nested inside `map[string]any`, `[]any`, or JSON-like structs.
- Empty interface values are no longer classified as unsupported solely because the type is `interface{}` / `any`.
- Candidate generation remains bounded and deterministic.
- Rebuild Shatter and rerun a fresh Zolem dry-run. The unsupported count drops by at least 9 functions, enough for the Zolem attempted corpus to exceed 90%.
- Rerun a fresh Zolem whole-corpus scan. It completes without increasing runtime failures materially; record attempted/completed counts and remaining unsupported buckets.
- Go frontend tests and the relevant Shatter quality gates pass.

## In Scope

- Go frontend type classification and value generation for empty interface.
- Nested JSON-like values inside maps, slices, structs, and pointer/output parameters where the expected type is empty interface.
- Zolem validation evidence after the fix.

## Out Of Scope

- Zolem feature-code changes.
- Project-specific constructors for `main.startupDeps`, `main.adminClient`, `main.rootOptions`, `fixture.Selector`, `main.Recorder`, or provider generator interfaces.
- First-party `parse.Node` work unless a new regression appears while implementing this issue.
- Unbounded arbitrary concrete-type guessing.
close_reason: Landed on main in fc66c902. Empty interface/any values now generate bounded candidates and no longer pre-skip through aggregate maps.

title: Go config runtime values
description:
Title: Go config runtime values
Tracker: Beads
Repo: shatter
Type: feature
Priority: 1
Labels: go,planner,zolem,config

## Problem

Current Shatter `main` can synthesize exact first-party Wazero values, but a whole-corpus dry-run against `~/project/zolem` still skips project-owned wrapper types whose valid value must be created by project code.

Current measurement from Shatter `main` commit `abf06766`:

```bash
SHATTER_SANDBOX_BACKEND=docker \
SHATTER_SANDBOX_DOCKER_IMAGE=golang:1.26-bookworm \
SHATTER_ARTIFACT_DIR=/tmp/zolem-shatter-artifacts-current-dry2 \
SHATTER_HARNESS_CACHE=/tmp/zolem-shatter-harness-current-dry2 \
SHATTER_GO_WORKSPACE_ROOT=/tmp/shatter-go-workspaces-current-dry2 \
/tmp/shatter-zolem-current-target/release/shatter scan \
  --project-dir /home/ketan/project/zolem \
  --language go \
  --all \
  --dry-run \
  --no-cache \
  --output /tmp/zolem-current-dry2.md \
  --output /tmp/zolem-current-dry2.json \
  .
```

Observed result:

- 360 total functions discovered.
- 302 selected functions in the dry-run plan.
- 58 functions skipped for unsupported/unexecutable parameter types.
- 0 skipped reasons mention `wazero.Runtime`.
- 0 skipped reasons mention `wazero.CompiledModule`.
- 18 skipped functions mention Zolem's `fixture.CompiledModule`.
- 14 skipped functions mention `interface` or `any`.
- 0 skipped reasons mention `parse.Node`.

Representative `fixture.CompiledModule` skips:

- direct value params: `(*Runner).Score`, `newWASMSelector`
- nested through `Fixture.Module *CompiledModule`: `RenderBody`, `ValidateTemplate`, `templateContext`, `renderSeed`
- nested through fixture slices/selectors/watchers: `NewMatcher`, `(*Matcher).Swap`, `StartWatcher`, `watchLoop`, `reload`

## Current Code Facts

- Shatter's built-in Go runtime-value registry lives in `shatter-go/runtimeval/registry.go`.
- `shatter-go/protocol/analyzer.go` has a hard-coded `synthesizableStdlibTypes` map. Despite the name, it now includes selected external runtime values such as `wazero.Runtime` and `wazero.CompiledModule`.
- The analyzer uses that hard-coded map to emit `TypeInfo{Kind: "unknown", Label: <registry key>}` for exact synthesizable types, which lets dry-run executability checks proceed.
- `shatter-go/planner/runtime_values.go` and `shatter-go/wrapper/wrapper.go` consume runtime-value entries by exact Go type spelling and paste Go-source expressions into generated wrappers with required imports.
- `shatter-go/config/loader.go` currently parses per-function `generators`, but those map parameter names to existing built-in runtime-value registry keys. They do not let a project declare a type-wide Go expression/import factory for a project-owned type.
- `shatter-go/wrapper/wrapper.go::applyRuntimeValueBindings` currently consults only the built-in runtime-value registry.
- `shatter-go/planner/composite.go::synthesizeFieldValue` already knows how to use built-in runtime-value expressions for nested non-pointer fields, and uses `nil` for registered pointer runtime-value fields.
- Zolem module path is `zolem.dev/zolem`.
- Zolem's `internal/fixture/wasm.go` defines:
  - `type CompiledModule struct { compiled wazero.CompiledModule }`
  - `func NewRunner() *Runner`
  - `func (r *Runner) CompileWASM(ctx context.Context, wasmBytes []byte) (CompiledModule, error)`
- Zolem's `fixture.CompiledModule` is intentionally not directly constructible outside its package via a meaningful struct literal because its only field is unexported.

## Desired Behavior

Go projects can declare exact type-wide runtime values in `.shatter/config.yaml` for project-owned types that are not suitable for Shatter's built-in registry.

This issue is the v1 slice with a fixed schema and bounded semantics:

```yaml
go_runtime_values:
  "fixture.CompiledModule":
    expression: |
      func() fixture.CompiledModule {
        # Go expression body shown only as shape; real config uses Go syntax.
      }()
    imports:
      - context
      - zolem.dev/zolem/internal/fixture
```

Schema contract:

- `go_runtime_values` is a top-level map.
- Each key is the exact Go source type spelling used by Shatter for parameters and TypeInfo labels, such as `fixture.CompiledModule`.
- Pointer keys are optional. For this slice, if `T` is configured, `*T` fields may be treated as synthesizable via `nil` even when `*T` is not separately configured.
- `expression` is a single Go expression of the configured type. Multi-statement setup must be written as an immediately invoked function expression. No implicit local variables such as `ctx` are provided.
- `(T, error)` helper semantics are out of scope. A configured expression handles its own failures, for example by panicking inside an IIFE.
- `imports` is a list of Go import paths only. No aliases in this slice. Imports are deduplicated with existing wrapper imports and referenced by their default package names.
- Matching is exact by source spelling. Package-alias normalization, module-path keys, generics, and assignability-based matching are out of scope.

For a configured exact type, Shatter should:

- treat direct values of that type as synthesizable during analysis/dry-run executability checks,
- treat pointers to that type as synthesizable in composite fields by emitting `nil`,
- use the configured Go-source expression for direct parameters,
- use the configured Go-source expression for nested non-pointer composite fields where built-in runtime-value expressions are already supported,
- add configured imports to generated wrapper code, and
- keep configured runtime values separate from the built-in first-party runtime-value registry.

## Proposed Zolem Validation Config

Use an uncommitted or committed `.shatter/config.yaml` validation config in Zolem, depending on implementation needs, with a `fixture.CompiledModule` entry whose expression compiles a tiny deterministic WASM module through `fixture.NewRunner().CompileWASM(...)`.

The expression may mirror the already-landed built-in `wazero.CompiledModule` bytes and lifecycle behavior. If it allocates a runner without closing it, document scan-process lifetime behavior in the issue result; teardown hooks are out of scope.

## In Scope

- Parse `go_runtime_values` from `.shatter/config.yaml`.
- Apply configured exact type values during Go analysis so configured values are not reported as opaque/no-constructor.
- Apply configured exact type values during wrapper generation for direct params.
- Extend the existing composite field path only as needed for configured exact values and pointers to configured values.
- Add focused Shatter Go tests for config parsing, analyzer direct-type canonicalization, wrapper direct-param substitution, and one nested composite case.
- Validate against Zolem by rerunning the whole-corpus dry-run with a documented `fixture.CompiledModule` config.

## Out of Scope

- Hard-coding Zolem package names into Shatter's built-in runtime-value registry.
- Adding production feature-code constructors to Zolem solely for Shatter.
- General automatic method-factory-chain discovery.
- General `interface{}` / `any` narrowing.
- `parse.Node`; current evidence shows 0 `parse.Node` skips.
- Import aliases, generic type matching, module-path type keys, and assignability-based matching.
- Full lifecycle teardown for generated runtime values.

## Acceptance Checks

- A config parser test proves `go_runtime_values` round-trips exact type key, expression, and path-only imports.
- An analyzer regression proves a configured exact type with all-unexported fields is reported as synthesizable rather than `static_opacity: "no_constructor"`.
- A wrapper regression proves a direct parameter of a configured exact type is initialized from the configured expression rather than JSON input, with configured imports emitted.
- A composite regression proves a nested field of a configured exact type uses the configured expression, and a pointer field to that configured type is allowed as `nil`.
- Existing Go checks pass: `go test ./...` and `go vet ./...` in `shatter-go`.
- A Zolem whole-corpus dry-run is rerun with a documented `fixture.CompiledModule` config.
- The rerun reports fewer `fixture.CompiledModule` skipped functions than the current count of 18, or records precise evidence for any remaining exact-type gaps.
acceptance_criteria: Configurable exact Go runtime values parse from .shatter/config.yaml, affect analyzer/planner/wrapper/composite paths, go test ./... and go vet ./... pass in shatter-go, and Zolem dry-run with fixture.CompiledModule config reduces that skip bucket below 18.

title: Pickpackit bundle entity seeds
description:
The Pickpackit `api/src/handlers/bundles.rs` validation slice now avoids the earlier blanket authorization failure: all 9 bundle handlers complete behaviorally, and `list_bundles` reaches HTTP 200 with generated authenticated workspace input. However, 7 of 9 handlers still stop at HTTP 404 because generated path values do not include bundle IDs and bundle-entry IDs that belong to the seeded workspace.

This blocks the broader Pickpackit validation goal because Shatter reaches authenticated handler behavior, but does not yet exercise domain branches behind bundle and bundle-entry lookup.

## Target Repository And Edit Scope

Primary repo/tracker: Shatter, using Beads issue tracking.

Likely edit scope:

- Pickpackit validation scaffolding under `api/.shatter/`, especially `api/.shatter/generators/pickpackit.rs`.
- Shatter native replay recipe or Axum request recipe plumbing only if validation scaffolding cannot express shared bundle/entity IDs through the existing `__shatter_replay.recipe.axum` request fields and generator recipes.

Do not make non-trivial Pickpackit production feature-code changes. In particular, do not change `api/src/handlers/bundles.rs` lookup or authorization semantics.

## Setup From Fresh Checkouts

Pickpackit prerequisites:

```bash
cd ~/project/pickpackit
docker compose up -d postgres
DATABASE_URL=postgres://pickpackit:pickpackit@localhost:55432/pickpackit make api-migrate
```

Build Shatter release binaries:

```bash
cd ~/project/shatter
CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/shatter-target-pickpackit-root CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 cargo build --release -p shatter-cli
CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/shatter-target-pickpackit-rust CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 cargo build --release --manifest-path shatter-rust/Cargo.toml
```

Then build the Pickpackit custom Rust frontend from `~/project/pickpackit/api`:

```bash
PATH=/tmp/shatter-target-pickpackit-root/release:/tmp/shatter-target-pickpackit-rust/release:$PATH \
SHATTER_RUST_SRC=~/project/shatter/shatter-rust \
SHATTER_RUNTIME_PATH=~/project/shatter/shatter-rust-runtime \
CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 \
shatter build-frontend rust --config .shatter -o .shatter-cache/bin --color never
```

## Smallest Reproduction

From `~/project/pickpackit/api` after the setup above:

```bash
PATH=/tmp/shatter-target-pickpackit-root/release:/tmp/shatter-target-pickpackit-rust/release:$PATH \
DATABASE_URL=postgres://pickpackit:pickpackit@localhost:55432/pickpackit \
SHATTER_RUST_SRC=~/project/shatter/shatter-rust \
SHATTER_RUNTIME_PATH=~/project/shatter/shatter-rust-runtime \
SHATTER_HARNESS_CACHE=/tmp/shatter-harness-pickpackit-bundles-entities \
SHATTER_BUILD_TIMEOUT=300 CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 \
shatter scan src/handlers --include "**/bundles.rs" --project-dir . --require-rust \
  --timeout-total 1800 --timeout-per-fn 300 --timeout-explore 240 \
  --request-timeout 300 --exec-timeout 240 --build-timeout 300 \
  --max-iterations 1 --progress --color never \
  -o /tmp/shatter-pickpackit-bundles-entities.json
```

Current observed status distribution from the `str-8bz8` validation run:

```text
apply_bundle          404  behavioral
create_bundle         400  behavioral
create_bundle_entry   404  behavioral
delete_bundle         404  behavioral
delete_bundle_entry   404  behavioral
get_bundle            404  behavioral
list_bundles          200  behavioral
update_bundle         404  behavioral
update_bundle_entry   404  behavioral
```

Summary shape:

```json
{
  "functions": 9,
  "outcomes": [{"outcome": "behavioral", "count": 9}],
  "bad_strings": 0,
  "http_statuses": [
    {"status": 200, "count": 1},
    {"status": 400, "count": 1},
    {"status": 404, "count": 7}
  ],
  "thrown_errors": 0
}
```

Helpful check command:

```bash
jq -r '.functions[] | "\(.qualified_id | split("::")[-1])\t\(([.discovered_inputs[]?.return_value.status?] | unique) | join(","))\t\(.completion_outcome)"' /tmp/shatter-pickpackit-bundles-entities.json
```

## Current Code Facts

Relevant Pickpackit handler facts in `api/src/handlers/bundles.rs`:

- `list_bundles(State<AppState>, CurrentAccount, Path<Uuid>, Query<ListBundlesQuery>)` needs an authorized workspace ID and currently reaches HTTP 200 with the seeded workspace path.
- `create_bundle(State<AppState>, CurrentAccount, Path<Uuid>, Json<CreateBundleRequest>)` needs the same authorized workspace ID and currently reaches HTTP 400, which is beyond authorization and acceptable for this issue's baseline.
- `get_bundle` and `update_bundle` take `Path<(Uuid, Uuid)>`, where the first ID is workspace ID and the second is a bundle ID in that workspace.
- `create_bundle_entry`, `update_bundle_entry`, and `delete_bundle_entry` require a coherent bundle ID, and the update/delete entry handlers also need a coherent bundle-entry ID.

Relevant validation scaffolding facts:

- `api/.shatter/config.yaml` maps `State` and `CurrentAccount` type generators, and maps parameter generator `current`, to `generators/pickpackit.rs`.
- `api/.shatter/generators/pickpackit.rs::CurrentAccount` can seed an account, workspace, and owner membership and return an Axum recipe path for the workspace route.
- That scaffolding does not yet seed a bundle row or bundle-entry row, and does not yet expose those IDs to Axum path/body recipes.

Relevant schema and seed-query facts:

- `api/migrations/0001_initial_schema.sql` defines `bundles(id, workspace_id, owner_person_id, label, priority, glyph, notes, archived_at, created_at)` with `priority` constrained to `required`, `important`, or `optional`.
- The same migration defines `bundle_entries(id, bundle_id, entry_kind, item_id, qty_override, task_bucket, task_text, sort_order)`.
- A valid task entry can be inserted without an item row by setting `entry_kind = 'task'`, `item_id = NULL`, `qty_override = NULL`, and non-null `task_bucket` plus `task_text`.
- Existing Pickpackit tests use:
  - `INSERT INTO bundles (workspace_id, owner_person_id, label, priority) VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4) RETURNING id`
  - `INSERT INTO bundle_entries (bundle_id, entry_kind, task_bucket, task_text) VALUES ($1, 'task', $2, $3) RETURNING id`

## Desired Behavior

The bundle validation scaffolding should produce at least one coherent authenticated request for bundle/entity handlers where:

- The `CurrentAccount` account owns the workspace used in the route path.
- The generated bundle ID belongs to that workspace.
- The generated bundle-entry ID, where needed, belongs to that bundle.
- Body/query defaults are valid enough to reach domain logic rather than failing early only because related entity IDs do not exist.

## Acceptance Checks

- Running the reproduction scan against `src/handlers --include "**/bundles.rs"` completes all 9 handlers with 0 failed, 0 unsupported, and 0 skipped.
- `list_bundles` still reaches HTTP 200 and `create_bundle` still reaches a non-403 path.
- At least one of `get_bundle`, `update_bundle`, `delete_bundle`, `apply_bundle`, `create_bundle_entry`, `update_bundle_entry`, or `delete_bundle_entry` reaches a non-404 path using generated/scaffolded IDs.
- The scan JSON contains zero occurrences of `pool timed out`, `Cannot start a runtime`, `requires a Tokio context`, and `Broken pipe`.
- Any remaining 404-only bundle handlers are listed with the specific missing shared input value or recipe capability needed next.

## Out Of Scope

- Changing Pickpackit production authorization or lookup semantics.
- Broad whole-corpus 90% validation.
- Non-trivial Pickpackit feature-code modifications.
- Fixing unrelated handler families outside `bundles.rs`.

title: wazero.CompiledModule factory
description:
Title: wazero.CompiledModule factory
Tracker: Beads
Repo: shatter
Type: feature
Priority: 1
Labels: go,planner,zolem,wazero

## Problem

Whole-corpus Shatter dry-runs against `~/project/zolem` still skip WASM-backed Zolem paths after exact `wazero.Runtime` support landed. The next Wazero-specific blocker is `github.com/tetratelabs/wazero.CompiledModule`, which Shatter currently treats as opaque and cannot synthesize for direct parameters or nested struct fields.

Latest measured dry-run after Shatter merge `96bd92330914855d55c82c518de758e9b7171ea9`:

```bash
SHATTER_SANDBOX_BACKEND=docker \
SHATTER_SANDBOX_DOCKER_IMAGE=golang:1.26-bookworm \
SHATTER_ARTIFACT_DIR=/tmp/zolem-shatter-artifacts-str-ibba-dry3 \
SHATTER_HARNESS_CACHE=/tmp/zolem-shatter-harness-str-ibba-dry3 \
SHATTER_GO_WORKSPACE_ROOT=/tmp/shatter-go-workspaces-str-ibba-dry3 \
/tmp/shatter-str-ibba-target/release/shatter scan \
  --project-dir /home/ketan/project/zolem \
  --language go \
  --all \
  --dry-run \
  --no-cache \
  --output /tmp/zolem-str-ibba-dry3.md \
  --output /tmp/zolem-str-ibba-dry3.json \
  .
```

Observed buckets from `/tmp/zolem-str-ibba-dry3.json`:

- 66 total skipped functions.
- 0 skips mention `wazero.Runtime`.
- 12 skips mention `wazero.CompiledModule`.
- 18 skip reasons mention `fixture.CompiledModule`.
- 14 skips mention `interface` or `any`.
- 0 skips mention `parse.Node`.

Representative remaining `wazero.CompiledModule` skips:

- `buildLocalHandler`: matcher/wasmGenerator fields named `compiled` resolve to `wazero.CompiledModule`.
- Provider `NewHandler` variants: matcher/wasmGenerator fields named `compiled` resolve to `wazero.CompiledModule`.
- `renderFixtureBody`, `serveFixture`, and `renderFixtureBodyBytes`: fixture module fields resolve to `wazero.CompiledModule`.
- `validateCompiledModule`: direct `compiled wazero.CompiledModule` parameter.

## Current Code Facts

- Shatter's Go runtime-value registry lives in `shatter-go/runtimeval/registry.go`.
- `shatter-go/runtimeval/registry.go` now includes first-party `wazero.Runtime` using `wazero.NewRuntime(context.Background())`.
- `shatter-go/protocol/analyzer.go` maps exact package/type pairs through `synthesizableStdlibTypes`; `github.com/tetratelabs/wazero`.`Runtime` maps to the registry key `wazero.Runtime`.
- `shatter-go/protocol/analyzer.go` returns `TypeInfo{Kind: "unknown", Label: <registry key>}` for synthesizable runtime values, allowing executability checks to proceed.
- `shatter-go/planner/composite.go` already emits registered non-pointer runtime-value expressions for nested struct fields whose `TypeInfo` is `Kind: "unknown"` and `Label` matches a registry key.
- `shatter-go/wrapper/wrapper.go` uses the same runtime-value registry for direct parameter bindings, so exact direct params can be initialized from generated Go source expressions rather than JSON input.
- Shatter currently depends on `github.com/tetratelabs/wazero v1.9.0` in `shatter-go/go.mod`.
- In wazero v1.9.0, `wazero.CompiledModule` is an interface returned by `Runtime.CompileModule(ctx, binary []byte)`.
- Wazero docs in the module source state that closing the runtime closes any `CompiledModule` it compiled. Shatter does not yet have runtime-value teardown support; existing `wazero.Runtime` support owns values for the scan process lifetime.
- Zolem also has its own `fixture.CompiledModule` wrapper type in `internal/fixture/wasm.go`; this issue is not expected to make every `fixture.CompiledModule` skip disappear.
- Zolem has tiny valid WASM binary fixtures in tests such as `internal/fixture/wasm_test.go`; Shatter can use an equivalent minimal binary in generated expressions without depending on Zolem feature code.

## Desired Behavior

Shatter treats exact `github.com/tetratelabs/wazero.CompiledModule` as a first-party Go runtime value/type factory. Direct parameters and nested composite fields of that exact type should receive a valid compiled WASM module built from a tiny deterministic WASM binary.

The generated expression should be self-contained Go source that:

- constructs a `wazero.Runtime`,
- compiles a small valid WASM binary with `CompileModule`,
- panics only if compilation unexpectedly fails, and
- documents scan-process ownership until runtime-value teardown exists.

## Proposed Scope

- Add a first-party runtime-value registry entry for `wazero.CompiledModule`.
- Add analyzer canonicalization for exact `github.com/tetratelabs/wazero.CompiledModule`.
- Reuse existing composite-field runtime-value support so nested fields can emit the compiled-module expression and imports.
- Add focused tests covering direct parameter planning/wrapper substitution and nested composite fields.
- Rerun the Zolem whole-corpus dry-run and compare the `wazero.CompiledModule` skip bucket against the current count of 12.

## In Scope

- Exact `wazero.CompiledModule` runtime-value support in Shatter.
- Tiny deterministic WASM bytes embedded in the generated expression or a helper expression string.
- Lifecycle note matching current runtime-value ownership limitations.
- Regression tests in Shatter Go planner/protocol/wrapper layers as appropriate.

## Out of Scope

- Synthesizing Zolem's `fixture.CompiledModule` wrapper type directly.
- General project-internal native generator module redesign.
- General `interface{}` / `any` candidate narrowing.
- `parse.Node`; current Zolem evidence shows 0 `parse.Node` skips.
- Zolem feature-code changes.

## Acceptance Checks

- Unit tests prove `wazero.CompiledModule` is registered and resolves to a runtime-value expression with required imports.
- A Go analyzer regression proves a direct `wazero.CompiledModule` parameter receives `TypeName: "wazero.CompiledModule"` and a nested struct field preserves `Label: "wazero.CompiledModule"`.
- A Go wrapper regression proves a target with a direct `wazero.CompiledModule` parameter is initialized from the generated expression rather than JSON input.
- Existing relevant Shatter Go checks pass: `go test ./...` and `go vet ./...` in `shatter-go`.
- A Zolem whole-corpus dry-run is rerun after the change.
- The rerun reports fewer `wazero.CompiledModule` skipped functions than the current count of 12, or records precise follow-up evidence explaining why exact-type support does not yet reach nested Zolem fields.
acceptance_criteria: wazero.CompiledModule is a first-party Go runtime value; go test ./... and go vet ./... pass in shatter-go; Zolem whole-corpus dry-run reports fewer wazero.CompiledModule skips than 12 or records precise nested follow-up evidence.

title: Pickpackit bundle auth seeds
description:
The Pickpackit `api/src/handlers/bundles.rs` validation slice now executes without Shatter harness/runtime failures, but generated inputs do not share the seeded authenticated workspace IDs. Every bundle handler explored in the sample stops at Pickpackit authorization and returns HTTP 403 `{"error":"forbidden"}`.

This blocks the broader Pickpackit validation goal because Shatter is reaching handler entry behavior, but not the domain branches behind workspace authorization.

## Target Repository And Edit Scope

Primary repo/tracker: Shatter, using Beads issue tracking.

Likely edit scope for this issue:

- Pickpackit validation scaffolding under `api/.shatter/`, especially `api/.shatter/generators/pickpackit.rs` and `api/.shatter/config.yaml`.
- Shatter input generation or native replay recipe plumbing only if validation scaffolding cannot express shared generated IDs.

Do not make non-trivial changes to Pickpackit production feature code. In particular, do not change `api/src/handlers/bundles.rs` authorization semantics.

## Setup From Fresh Checkouts

Pickpackit prerequisites:

```bash
cd ~/project/pickpackit
docker compose up -d postgres
DATABASE_URL=postgres://pickpackit:pickpackit@localhost:55432/pickpackit make api-migrate
```

Pickpackit docs confirm local Postgres is exposed on host port `55432`, and `Makefile` target `api-migrate` runs `cargo run --manifest-path api/Cargo.toml --bin pickpackit-migrate`.

Shatter prerequisites:

```bash
cd ~/project/shatter
CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/shatter-target-pickpackit-root CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 cargo build --release -p shatter-cli
CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/shatter-target-pickpackit-rust CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 cargo build --release --manifest-path shatter-rust/Cargo.toml
```

Then build the Pickpackit custom Rust frontend from `~/project/pickpackit/api`:

```bash
PATH=/tmp/shatter-target-pickpackit-root/release:/tmp/shatter-target-pickpackit-rust/release:$PATH \
SHATTER_RUST_SRC=~/project/shatter/shatter-rust \
SHATTER_RUNTIME_PATH=~/project/shatter/shatter-rust-runtime \
CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 \
shatter build-frontend rust --config .shatter -o .shatter-cache/bin --color never
```

## Smallest Reproduction

From `~/project/pickpackit/api` after the setup above:

```bash
PATH=/tmp/shatter-target-pickpackit-root/release:/tmp/shatter-target-pickpackit-rust/release:$PATH \
DATABASE_URL=postgres://pickpackit:pickpackit@localhost:55432/pickpackit \
SHATTER_RUST_SRC=~/project/shatter/shatter-rust \
SHATTER_RUNTIME_PATH=~/project/shatter/shatter-rust-runtime \
SHATTER_HARNESS_CACHE=/tmp/shatter-harness-pickpackit-bundles-auth \
SHATTER_BUILD_TIMEOUT=300 CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 \
shatter scan src/handlers --include "**/bundles.rs" --project-dir . --require-rust \
  --timeout-total 1800 --timeout-per-fn 300 --timeout-explore 240 \
  --request-timeout 300 --exec-timeout 240 --build-timeout 300 \
  --max-iterations 1 --progress --color never \
  -o /tmp/shatter-pickpackit-bundles-auth.json
```

Observed with the current validation branch:

- 9 completed functions, 0 failed, 0 unsupported, 0 skipped.
- `completion_outcome` is `behavioral` for all 9 functions.
- No strings in the JSON match `pool timed out`, `Cannot start a runtime`, `requires a Tokio context`, or `Broken pipe`.
- All 9 discovered HTTP outcomes have status `403` and body `{"error":"forbidden"}`.

Helpful check command:

```bash
jq '{functions: (.functions | length), outcomes: ([.functions[].completion_outcome] | group_by(.) | map({outcome: .[0], count: length})), bad_strings: ([.. | strings | select(test("pool timed out|Cannot start a runtime|requires a Tokio context|Broken pipe"))] | length), http_statuses: ([.functions[].discovered_inputs[]?.return_value.status?] | group_by(.) | map({status: .[0], count: length})), thrown_errors: ([.functions[].discovered_inputs[]?.thrown_error? | select(. != null)] | length)}' /tmp/shatter-pickpackit-bundles-auth.json
```

Current output shape:

```json
{
  "functions": 9,
  "outcomes": [{"outcome": "behavioral", "count": 9}],
  "bad_strings": 0,
  "http_statuses": [{"status": 403, "count": 9}],
  "thrown_errors": 0
}
```

## Current Code Facts

Relevant Pickpackit handler facts in `api/src/handlers/bundles.rs`:

- `list_bundles(State<AppState>, CurrentAccount, Path<Uuid>, Query<ListBundlesQuery>)` calls `ensure_workspace_access_for(&state.pool, current.id, workspace_id)` before query logic.
- `create_bundle(State<AppState>, CurrentAccount, Path<Uuid>, Json<CreateBundleRequest>)` calls `require_workspace_role(&state.pool, workspace_id, current.id, WRITE_ROLES)` before payload validation and insert logic.
- `get_bundle` and `update_bundle` take `Path<(Uuid, Uuid)>`, where the first ID must be an authorized workspace ID and the second ID is a bundle ID in that workspace.

Relevant validation scaffolding facts:

- `api/.shatter/config.yaml` maps `State` and `CurrentAccount` type generators, and maps parameter generator `current`, to `generators/pickpackit.rs`.
- `api/.shatter/generators/pickpackit.rs::seed_account_with_workspace()` inserts an `accounts` row, a `workspaces` row, and a `workspace_members` owner row.
- `CurrentAccount` returns only `CurrentAccount::test(account_id)`. The seeded `workspace_id` is not currently exposed to path/body/query generation.
- The observed `apply_bundle` inputs show native replay values for `State` and `current`, followed by `null` route/body values, then a 403 response.

## Desired Behavior

Pickpackit bundle handler validation should generate at least one coherent authenticated request where:

- `CurrentAccount` is an account seeded as a member/owner of a workspace.
- The generated path workspace ID is that same seeded workspace ID.
- For handlers that require a bundle ID, the generated bundle ID belongs to that same workspace.
- Body/query defaults are valid enough to reach domain logic rather than authorization failure.

The first useful slice can target one low-risk handler, preferably `list_bundles` or `create_bundle`, before expanding to all bundle handlers.

## Acceptance Checks

- Running the reproduction scan against `src/handlers --include "**/bundles.rs"` completes all 9 handlers with 0 failed, 0 unsupported, and 0 skipped.
- At least one of `list_bundles` or `create_bundle` reaches a non-403 path using generated/scaffolded inputs.
- The scan JSON still contains zero occurrences of `pool timed out`, `Cannot start a runtime`, `requires a Tokio context`, and `Broken pipe`.
- Any remaining 403-only bundle handlers are listed with the specific missing shared input value or recipe capability needed next.

## Out Of Scope

- Changing Pickpackit production authorization semantics.
- Broad whole-corpus 90% validation.
- Non-trivial Pickpackit feature-code modifications.
- Fixing unrelated handler families outside `bundles.rs`.

title: wazero.Runtime type factory
description:
Title: wazero.Runtime type factory
Tracker: Beads
Repo: shatter
Type: feature
Priority: 1
Labels: go,planner,zolem,wazero

## Problem

Whole-corpus Shatter dry-runs against `~/project/zolem` still skip Zolem's WASM-backed fixture and provider paths because Shatter cannot synthesize `github.com/tetratelabs/wazero.Runtime` values. This blocks progress toward executing 90%+ of Zolem feature code without modifying Zolem feature behavior.

Latest measured dry-run from current Shatter main against Zolem:

```bash
SHATTER_SANDBOX_BACKEND=docker \
SHATTER_SANDBOX_DOCKER_IMAGE=golang:1.26-bookworm \
SHATTER_ARTIFACT_DIR=/tmp/zolem-shatter-artifacts-czo5-dry3 \
SHATTER_HARNESS_CACHE=/tmp/zolem-shatter-harness-czo5-dry3 \
/tmp/shatter-czo5-target/release/shatter scan \
  --project-dir /home/ketan/project/zolem \
  --language go \
  --all \
  --dry-run \
  --no-cache \
  --output /tmp/zolem-czo5-dry3.md \
  --output /tmp/zolem-czo5-dry3.json \
  .
```

Observed result:

- 53 source files discovered.
- 205 unexported functions in scope.
- 70 skipped functions.
- 13 skipped functions mention `wazero.Runtime`.
- 14 skipped functions mention `interface{}` or `any`.
- 0 skipped functions mention `parse.Node`.

Representative `wazero.Runtime` skipped targets:

- `loadLocalFixtures`: `param "runner" -> inner -> field "rt" -> wazero.Runtime`
- `loadFixtures`: `param "runner" -> inner -> field "rt" -> wazero.Runtime`
- `(*Loader).WithRunner`: `param "r" -> inner -> field "rt" -> wazero.Runtime`
- `NewMatcher`, `NewLegacySelector`, `newWASMSelector`: `runner -> rt`; `newWASMSelector` also needs `fixture.CompiledModule`
- `StartWatcher`, `watchLoop`, `reload`: config values contain nested matcher/runner runtime fields
- provider `NewHandler` variants: matcher or wasm generator values contain nested runtime fields

## Current Code Facts

- Shatter's Go runtime-value registry lives in `shatter-go/runtimeval/registry.go`.
- The current registry includes first-party entries such as `context.Context`, `http.ResponseWriter`, `*http.Request`, `io.Reader`, `io.Writer`, `io.ReadCloser`, `time.Time`, `http.Header`, and `*template.Template`.
- `shatter-go/wrapper/wrapper.go` calls the shared runtime-value registry while generating wrapper code, so direct parameters with registered types are initialized from Go source expressions instead of JSON unmarshalling.
- `shatter-go/planner/runtime_values.go` exposes registered runtime values to invocation planning as `ValuePlanKindRuntimeValue`.
- `shatter-go/protocol/analyzer.go` has canonical-package spelling logic so imported types such as `template.Template` can be reported with registry keys consumed by the planner.
- Shatter Go tests already cover first-party runtime-value behavior in planner, protocol, and wrapper tests.
- `shatter-go/go.mod` currently has indirect `github.com/tetratelabs/wazero v1.9.0`.
- Zolem uses `github.com/tetratelabs/wazero v1.11.0` and constructs runtimes in paths such as `internal/fixture/wasm.go` and `internal/wasmgen/generator.go`.
- Zolem has a Zolem-side issue, `zolem-xw3`, for supplying wazero-backed Shatter generators, but it is blocked because Shatter's current custom Go frontend generator build isolates configured generators into a separate module and cannot import Zolem internal packages.

## Desired Behavior

Shatter treats `github.com/tetratelabs/wazero.Runtime` as a first-party Go runtime value/type factory so wrappers and invocation plans can supply a valid runtime where the expected type is exactly `wazero.Runtime`.

The implementation should use Shatter's existing terminology and mechanisms where possible: `runtime_value`, runtime-value registry entries, native generators, or generator config. The phrase "type factory" is acceptable as issue shorthand, but user-facing/code-facing naming should stay aligned with existing Shatter concepts unless a broader naming change is intentionally scoped.

## Proposed Scope

- Add first-party Go runtime-value support for `wazero.Runtime`.
- Add required import handling for the generated expression.
- Add focused tests proving direct `wazero.Runtime` params produce runtime-value plans and wrapper expressions.
- Verify whether existing nested construction paths can use the registry when a struct field or constructor parameter expects `wazero.Runtime`; if not, leave the nested-generalization as a follow-up issue with concrete evidence.
- Rerun the Zolem whole-corpus dry-run and compare the `wazero.Runtime` skip bucket against the current count of 13.

## In Scope

- `wazero.Runtime` as a first-party Shatter Go runtime value.
- Deterministic construction using a wazero constructor, for example `wazero.NewRuntime(context.Background())`.
- Lifecycle risk assessment for generated runtime values. If teardown cannot be wired into wrapper cleanup in this slice, document the bounded scan-process ownership behavior and file or link follow-up work for cleanup support.
- Regression tests in Shatter's Go planner/protocol/wrapper layers as appropriate.

## Out of Scope

- Synthesizing `wazero.CompiledModule`.
- Generating arbitrary WASM modules or fixture selectors.
- Zolem feature-code changes.
- Full custom generator-module redesign for Zolem internal package imports.
- General `interface{}` / `any` candidate narrowing.
- `parse.Node` work; current Zolem evidence shows `parse.Node` skips are already zero after first-party template handling.

## Acceptance Checks

- Unit tests prove `wazero.Runtime` is registered and resolves to a runtime-value expression with required imports.
- A Go wrapper regression proves a target with a direct `wazero.Runtime` parameter is initialized from a generated runtime expression rather than JSON input.
- Existing relevant Shatter Go checks pass: `go test ./...` and `go vet ./...` in `shatter-go`.
- A Zolem whole-corpus dry-run is rerun after the change.
- The rerun reports fewer `wazero.Runtime` skipped functions than the current count of 13, or the issue records precise follow-up evidence explaining why exact-type support does not yet reach nested Zolem fields.
acceptance_criteria: wazero.Runtime is a first-party Go runtime value; go test ./... and go vet ./... pass in shatter-go; Zolem whole-corpus dry-run reports fewer wazero.Runtime skips than 13 or records precise nested follow-up evidence.

title: Pickpackit DB handlers timeout
description:
Title: Pickpackit DB handlers timeout

Type: bug
Priority: 1
Labels: rust,axum,pickpackit,native-replay,state,behavior-map

## Symptom

After route generation and coverage-line capture fixes, many Pickpackit Axum
handlers that do not require request bodies still return status 500 before
reaching meaningful branches. The observed response body reports that the
SQLx/Postgres pool timed out while waiting for an open connection.

Observed in sampled output:

```text
/tmp/shatter-pickpackit-str-ycil-bundles-coverage-line-hit-1iter.json
```

The sampled `delete_bundle` handler now records function-entry coverage, which
proves handler entry can be captured, but execution stops at the database
access path with a pool timeout. Other DB-backed no-body handlers similarly
fail before exploring application branches.

Literal sampled payloads from that artifact:

```json
{"function_name":"delete_bundle","coverage_pct":6.25,"status":500,"body":{"error":"pool timed out while waiting for an open connection"},"lines":[275]}
```
