```json
{"function_name":"get_bundle","coverage_pct":0.0,"status":500,"body":{"error":"pool timed out while waiting for an open connection"},"lines":[]}
{"function_name":"list_bundles","coverage_pct":0.0,"status":500,"body":{"error":"pool timed out while waiting for an open connection"},"lines":[]}
```

## Smallest Known Reproduction

From the Pickpackit API validation worktree used by `str-l5ru`:

```text
/home/ketan/.local/share/worktrees/pickpackit/shatter-str-l5ru-validation/api
```

The repro assumes that validation worktree already exists and its local
Postgres service is reachable at:

```text
postgres://pickpackit:pickpackit@localhost:55432/pickpackit
```

Run:

```bash
env PATH=/home/ketan/.local/share/worktrees/shatter/str-ycil-rust-handler-coverage-zero/target/release:/home/ketan/.local/share/worktrees/shatter/str-ycil-rust-handler-coverage-zero/shatter-rust/target/release:$PATH \
  DATABASE_URL=postgres://pickpackit:pickpackit@localhost:55432/pickpackit \
  SHATTER_RUST_SRC=/home/ketan/.local/share/worktrees/shatter/str-ycil-rust-handler-coverage-zero/shatter-rust \
  SHATTER_RUNTIME_PATH=/home/ketan/.local/share/worktrees/shatter/str-ycil-rust-handler-coverage-zero/shatter-rust-runtime \
  SHATTER_HARNESS_CACHE=/tmp/shatter-harness-db-pool-repro \
  shatter scan src/handlers --include "**/bundles.rs" \
    --project-dir . --require-rust \
    --timeout-total 1800 --timeout-per-fn 300 --timeout-explore 240 \
    --request-timeout 300 --exec-timeout 240 --build-timeout 300 \
    --max-iterations 1 --progress --color never \
    -o /tmp/shatter-pickpackit-db-pool-repro.json
```

Inspect DB-backed no-body handlers:

```bash
jq '.functions[] | select(.function_name | test("delete_bundle|get_bundle|list_bundles")) | {function_name, coverage_pct, status: .discovered_inputs[0].return_value.status, body: .discovered_inputs[0].return_value.body, lines: .discovered_inputs[0].lines_executed}' /tmp/shatter-pickpackit-db-pool-repro.json
```

## Current Code Facts

- Pickpackit handlers use Axum state/native values for application state and
  database access.
- `shatter-rust/src/executor.rs` contains native replay support for Axum
  `State` and custom extensions, including regression coverage in
  `execute_axum_handler_replays_native_state_and_extension_values`.
- The current sampled failure is not a route mismatch: the route reaches
  handler entry for at least `delete_bundle`, then fails while waiting for a
  database connection.

## Desired Behavior

First narrow whether the timeout is caused by Shatter's Axum native-state
replay/execution strategy or by the external Pickpackit database environment.
If Shatter is constructing, replaying, or scheduling state in a way that starves
the pool, fix that path so DB-backed handlers can execute past handler entry.
If the environment is missing or live DB-backed state cannot be exercised
safely, report a precise unsupported/skip reason instead of recording a generic
500 behavior-map result as if it were application behavior.

## Acceptance Checks

- Add a focused diagnostic test or integration fixture that distinguishes
  Shatter-induced Axum state replay/pool starvation from an unavailable external
  database environment.
- If the cause is in Shatter, fix it and re-run sampled Pickpackit `bundles.rs`
  no-body DB handlers, verifying they execute past handler entry without the
  literal error `pool timed out while waiting for an open connection`.
- If the cause is external environment or intrinsically unsupported live DB
  state, classify the handler with a precise justified unsupported/skip reason
  instead of a generic 500 behavior-map result.
- Do not modify Pickpackit feature code except trivial validation scaffolding if
  absolutely necessary.

## Scope

In scope: Shatter Rust/Axum state replay, execution scheduling, and error
classification for DB-backed handlers.

Out of scope: broad database seeding strategy, semantic branch exploration,
JSON body defaults, or Pickpackit feature-code rewrites.
acceptance_criteria: Diagnostic distinguishes Shatter pool starvation from external DB environment; sampled DB handlers no longer emit pool timeout if Shatter is at fault, or receive precise unsupported/skip classification otherwise.

title: Axum Json bodies default null
description:
Title: Axum Json bodies default null

Type: bug
Priority: 1
Labels: rust,axum,pickpackit,input-generation,behavior-map

## Symptom

Pickpackit Axum handlers that take `Json<T>` complete at the routing layer but
do not enter useful handler code because Shatter sends a null or empty JSON
body. Axum rejects the request before the handler body can deserialize the
payload, so coverage remains 0% and behavior maps contain only request-parse
failures.

Observed in the sampled Pickpackit bundles scan after the Rust coverage-line
fix:

```text
/tmp/shatter-pickpackit-str-ycil-bundles-coverage-line-hit-1iter.json
```

For `src/handlers/bundles.rs`, handlers such as `apply_bundle`,
`create_bundle`, and `create_bundle_entry` report status 400 with an error like
`Failed to parse request body as JSON: EOF`, and no executed handler lines.

## Smallest Known Reproduction

From the Pickpackit API validation worktree:

```bash
env PATH=/home/ketan/.local/share/worktrees/shatter/str-ycil-rust-handler-coverage-zero/target/release:/home/ketan/.local/share/worktrees/shatter/str-ycil-rust-handler-coverage-zero/shatter-rust/target/release:$PATH \
  DATABASE_URL=postgres://pickpackit:pickpackit@localhost:55432/pickpackit \
  SHATTER_RUST_SRC=/home/ketan/.local/share/worktrees/shatter/str-ycil-rust-handler-coverage-zero/shatter-rust \
  SHATTER_RUNTIME_PATH=/home/ketan/.local/share/worktrees/shatter/str-ycil-rust-handler-coverage-zero/shatter-rust-runtime \
  SHATTER_HARNESS_CACHE=/tmp/shatter-harness-json-body-repro \
  shatter scan src/handlers --include "**/bundles.rs" \
    --project-dir . --require-rust \
    --timeout-total 1800 --timeout-per-fn 300 --timeout-explore 240 \
    --request-timeout 300 --exec-timeout 240 --build-timeout 300 \
    --max-iterations 1 --progress --color never \
    -o /tmp/shatter-pickpackit-json-body-repro.json
```

Inspect body-taking handlers:

```bash
jq '.functions[] | select(.function_name | test("apply_bundle|create_bundle")) | {function_name, status: .discovered_inputs[0].return_value.status, body: .discovered_inputs[0].return_value.body, lines: .discovered_inputs[0].lines_executed}' /tmp/shatter-pickpackit-json-body-repro.json
```

## Current Code Facts

- `shatter-rust/src/executor.rs` generates Axum harnesses and request bodies
  for `Json<T>` extractors.
- Existing unit coverage includes `generate_axum_harness_json_body_defaults_to_post`,
  but the Pickpackit repro shows the generated request body is not a valid
  default payload for concrete application DTOs.
- This issue is distinct from route matching and coverage-line capture:
  non-body `bundles.rs` handlers can now report at least function-entry
  coverage, while body-taking handlers are rejected before handler entry.

## Desired Behavior

For supported `Json<T>` extractor inputs, Shatter should generate a
syntactically valid JSON body that Axum can deserialize far enough to enter the
handler. Default payloads may be simple zero/empty values, but they should not
be `null` or empty when `T` requires an object.

## Acceptance Checks

- Add a focused Rust frontend regression test for an Axum handler with
  `Json<LocalStruct>` showing the generated request reaches handler entry
  instead of returning a JSON EOF/deserialization-before-entry failure.
- Re-run the sampled Pickpackit `bundles.rs` scan and verify `apply_bundle`,
  `create_bundle`, or `create_bundle_entry` no longer fail solely because the
  request body is empty/null.
- Preserve existing behavior for no-body handlers and non-Axum Rust functions.

## Scope

In scope: Rust Axum harness input/default generation for `Json<T>` and
behavior-map evidence for body-taking handlers.

Out of scope: solving database availability, improving semantic branch
exploration, or changing Pickpackit feature code.
acceptance_criteria: Axum Json<LocalStruct> default body reaches handler entry; sampled Pickpackit bundles Json handlers no longer fail solely from empty/null body; existing no-body/non-Axum behavior preserved.

title: CurrentAccount direct input
description:
Title: CurrentAccount direct input

Type: feature
Priority: 1
Labels: rust,axum,custom-extractor,pickpackit,scan

## Symptom

After the crate-bridge target-cache fix, a whole Pickpackit `src/handlers` scan
with `--max-iterations 1` gets past disk exhaustion, but the `session` endpoint
still fails because Shatter treats its `CurrentAccount` parameter as generic
JSON-harness input.

Observed in `/tmp/shatter-pickpackit-str-qsrb-handlers-full-1iter-nodebug.json`:

```text
/home/ketan/.local/share/worktrees/pickpackit/shatter-str-l5ru-validation/api/src/handlers/session.rs::session:
error: exploration error: unsupported: crate_bridge: function `session` is not JSON-harness compatible: function parameters are not JSON-harness compatible and may not implement DeserializeOwned
```

Resolved Pickpackit signature:

```rust
use axum::Json;

use crate::{auth::CurrentAccount, domain::Account, error::ApiResult};

pub async fn session(current: CurrentAccount) -> ApiResult<Json<Account>> {
    Ok(Json(Account::from(current)))
}
```

This is an Axum-style custom extractor endpoint with only a custom extractor
parameter. It is not a generic JSON-harness function.

## Smallest Known Reproduction

From the Pickpackit API validation worktree:

```bash
env PATH=/home/ketan/project/shatter/target/release:/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-rust/target/release:$PATH \
  DATABASE_URL=postgres://pickpackit:pickpackit@localhost:55432/pickpackit \
  SHATTER_RUST_SRC=/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-rust \
  SHATTER_RUNTIME_PATH=/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-rust-runtime \
  shatter scan src/handlers/session.rs \
    --project-dir . \
    --require-rust \
    --timeout-total 1800 --timeout-per-fn 300 --timeout-explore 240 \
    --request-timeout 300 --exec-timeout 240 --build-timeout 300 \
    --max-iterations 1 \
    --progress --color never \
    -o /tmp/shatter-pickpackit-session-current-account-1iter.json
```

The known failing symbol is `session`.

## Current Code Facts

- `shatter-rust/src/adapters.rs` and `shatter-rust/src/executor.rs` contain the
  Axum adapter and harness-generation paths.
- Earlier Pickpackit work made Shatter resolve custom generator value sources
  for `CurrentAccount` in Axum handlers, but this direct `session` endpoint is
  still routed to generic crate-bridge JSON compatibility and rejected.
- The function return type is `ApiResult<Json<Account>>`, so successful
  execution should be representable through existing Axum response capture.

## Desired Behavior

Shatter should classify and execute a function whose parameters are only
custom Axum extractor/runtime values, such as Pickpackit `CurrentAccount`,
instead of requiring those params to implement JSON deserialization. The
`session` endpoint should execute through an adapter-owned or generator-backed
harness and produce a behavior-map artifact.

## Acceptance Checks

- Add a focused Rust frontend test for an Axum-style handler with only a custom
  extractor parameter and no path/query/json/form/state extractor.
- The Pickpackit `session.rs::session` scan no longer fails with
  `not JSON-harness compatible`.
- Existing custom extractor support for handlers with other Axum extractors
  continues to pass.
- Genuinely unsupported custom params still produce clear unsupported errors.
- `cargo test --manifest-path shatter-rust/Cargo.toml`, Rust frontend clippy,
  `task smoke`, and `task e2e` pass.

## Scope

In scope:

- Rust Axum adapter classification and harness selection for custom-extractor
  only handlers.
- Reuse of existing custom generator/runtime value mechanisms for
  `CurrentAccount`.
- Focused Pickpackit validation for `session`.

Out of scope:

- Pickpackit feature-code changes.
- SQLx `PgPool` helper support.
- Multipart extractor support.
- Coverage-reporting improvements after the endpoint executes.
acceptance_criteria: Custom-extractor-only Axum handlers route through adapter/generator support; session no longer fails JSON-harness compatibility; gates pass.

title: SQLx PgPool helper input
description:
Title: SQLx PgPool helper input

Type: feature
Priority: 1
Labels: rust,sqlx,pickpackit,scan

## Symptom

After the crate-bridge target-cache fix, a whole Pickpackit `src/handlers` scan
with `--max-iterations 1` gets past disk exhaustion, but two direct helper
functions in handler files still fail because Shatter treats their `&PgPool`
parameter as generic JSON-harness input.

Observed in `/tmp/shatter-pickpackit-str-qsrb-handlers-full-1iter-nodebug.json`:

```text
/home/ketan/.local/share/worktrees/pickpackit/shatter-str-l5ru-validation/api/src/handlers/legs.rs::fetch_legs_for_workspace:
error: exploration error: unsupported: crate_bridge: function `fetch_legs_for_workspace` is not JSON-harness compatible: function parameters are not JSON-harness compatible and may not implement DeserializeOwned

/home/ketan/.local/share/worktrees/pickpackit/shatter-str-l5ru-validation/api/src/handlers/tasks.rs::fetch_tasks_for_workspace:
error: exploration error: unsupported: crate_bridge: function `fetch_tasks_for_workspace` is not JSON-harness compatible: function parameters are not JSON-harness compatible and may not implement DeserializeOwned
```

Resolved handler signatures from Pickpackit:

```rust
pub async fn fetch_legs_for_workspace(
    pool: &PgPool,
    workspace_id: Uuid,
) -> sqlx::Result<Vec<TripLeg>>

pub async fn fetch_tasks_for_workspace(
    pool: &PgPool,
    workspace_id: Uuid,
) -> ApiResult<Vec<TripTask>>
```

These are not Axum handler functions. They are feature-code SQL helper functions
inside Pickpackit handler modules.

## Smallest Known Reproduction

From the Pickpackit API validation worktree:

```bash
env PATH=/home/ketan/project/shatter/target/release:/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-rust/target/release:$PATH \
  DATABASE_URL=postgres://pickpackit:pickpackit@localhost:55432/pickpackit \
  SHATTER_RUST_SRC=/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-rust \
  SHATTER_RUNTIME_PATH=/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-rust-runtime \
  shatter scan src/handlers/legs.rs src/handlers/tasks.rs \
    --project-dir . \
    --require-rust \
    --timeout-total 1800 --timeout-per-fn 300 --timeout-explore 240 \
    --request-timeout 300 --exec-timeout 240 --build-timeout 300 \
    --max-iterations 1 \
    --progress --color never \
    -o /tmp/shatter-pickpackit-sqlx-pgpool-1iter.json
```

The known failing symbols are `fetch_legs_for_workspace` and
`fetch_tasks_for_workspace`.

## Current Code Facts

- `shatter-rust/src/executor.rs` rejects non-JSON-compatible params in the
  generic crate-bridge path.
- Pickpackit validation already provides `DATABASE_URL`, and other Pickpackit
  Axum handlers execute through `AppState`/database-backed paths.
- `Uuid` is already a JSON-compatible/generated input type in existing handler
  scans; the incompatible parameter here is `&PgPool`.

## Desired Behavior

Shatter should be able to construct or supply a SQLx `PgPool` runtime value from
the existing validation environment for direct Rust helper functions that accept
`&PgPool`, then explore the remaining JSON-compatible parameters normally. The
two known Pickpackit helper functions should execute and produce behavior-map
artifacts.

If general SQLx support needs staged rollout, this issue should support
`sqlx::PgPool` / `sqlx::postgres::PgPool` first and return a clear unsupported
error for other pool types.

## Acceptance Checks

- Add a focused Rust frontend test for a crate-bridge function accepting
  `&PgPool` plus a JSON-compatible parameter.
- The harness constructs the pool from `DATABASE_URL` or an explicit Shatter
  runtime configuration source and passes it by reference.
- Pickpackit `fetch_legs_for_workspace` and `fetch_tasks_for_workspace` no
  longer fail with `not JSON-harness compatible`.
- Genuinely unsupported external reference params still produce clear
  unsupported errors.
- `cargo test --manifest-path shatter-rust/Cargo.toml`, Rust frontend clippy,
  `task smoke`, and `task e2e` pass.

## Scope

In scope:

- Rust crate-bridge runtime-value construction for `sqlx::PgPool`.
- Tests for direct helper functions that take `&PgPool`.
- Focused Pickpackit validation for the two known helper functions.

Out of scope:

- Pickpackit feature-code changes.
- Arbitrary external reference construction.
- General support for every SQLx database backend.
- Coverage-reporting improvements after the functions execute.
acceptance_criteria: Crate-bridge can supply sqlx PgPool from runtime config/DATABASE_URL; fetch_legs_for_workspace and fetch_tasks_for_workspace no longer fail JSON-harness compatibility; gates pass.

title: Rust handler coverage zero
description:
Title: Rust handler coverage zero

Type: bug
Priority: 1
Labels: rust,coverage,behavior-map,pickpackit,scan

## Symptom

After the crate-bridge target-cache fix, Shatter can execute most Pickpackit
handlers, but the scan table reports `0%` coverage for every completed handler.
This blocks the overall Pickpackit validation goal because successful execution
is not producing useful coverage/behavior-map evidence.

Observed in `/tmp/shatter-pickpackit-str-qsrb-handlers-full-1iter-nodebug.json`:

```text
Scan complete: 71 completed, 4 failed, 0 unsupported, 0 skipped
```

The same run lists each completed handler with one explored path and `0%`
coverage, for example:

```text
/home/ketan/.local/share/worktrees/pickpackit/shatter-str-l5ru-validation/api/src/handlers/bundles.rs::create_bundle | 1 | 0%
/home/ketan/.local/share/worktrees/pickpackit/shatter-str-l5ru-validation/api/src/handlers/items.rs::create_item | 1 | 0%
/home/ketan/.local/share/worktrees/pickpackit/shatter-str-l5ru-validation/api/src/handlers/workspaces.rs::workspaces | 1 | 0%
```

This is distinct from the four remaining unsupported-handler failures: the
functions above completed, but their reported coverage is still zero.

## Smallest Known Reproduction

From the Pickpackit API validation worktree:

```bash
env PATH=/home/ketan/project/shatter/target/release:/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-rust/target/release:$PATH \
  DATABASE_URL=postgres://pickpackit:pickpackit@localhost:55432/pickpackit \
  SHATTER_RUST_SRC=/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-rust \
  SHATTER_RUNTIME_PATH=/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-rust-runtime \
  shatter scan src/handlers/bundles.rs \
    --project-dir . \
    --require-rust \
    --timeout-total 1800 --timeout-per-fn 300 --timeout-explore 240 \
    --request-timeout 300 --exec-timeout 240 --build-timeout 300 \
    --max-iterations 1 \
    --progress --color never \
    -o /tmp/shatter-pickpackit-bundles-coverage-1iter.json
```

In the latest known run, all nine `bundles.rs` handlers completed but each
reported `0%` coverage.

## Current Code Facts

- `shatter-rust/src/instrument.rs` contains Rust branch/condition
  instrumentation.
- `shatter-rust/src/executor.rs` contains the generated harness execution paths
  used by crate-backed and crate-bridge Rust handlers.
- The CLI scan table receives completed-path counts, but the coverage summary
  for Pickpackit Rust handlers is not reflecting executed lines/branches.

## Desired Behavior

Completed Rust handler executions should produce non-zero, meaningful coverage
and behavior-map evidence when the handler body or generated adapter path is
actually executed. For sampled Pickpackit handler files, the scan output should
distinguish real low coverage from missing coverage capture.

## Acceptance Checks

- Add a focused regression test showing a crate-backed or crate-bridge Rust
  handler execution reports coverage hits instead of `0%` when code runs.
- Re-run a sampled Pickpackit handler file such as `src/handlers/bundles.rs`
  and verify completed handlers no longer all report `0%` coverage.
- Preserve behavior-map artifact shape and existing coverage reporting for
  non-Rust frontends.
- `cargo test --manifest-path shatter-rust/Cargo.toml`, Rust frontend clippy,
  `task smoke`, and `task e2e` pass.

## Scope

In scope:

- Rust frontend instrumentation, execution-result coverage capture, and scan
  report plumbing needed for crate-backed/crate-bridge handlers.
- Focused Pickpackit sampled validation.

Out of scope:

- Increasing branch exploration quality or seed diversity beyond fixing missing
  coverage capture.
- Pickpackit feature-code changes.
- Adding support for the four currently failing unsupported handlers.
acceptance_criteria: A crate-backed or crate-bridge Rust handler reports coverage hits when code runs; sampled Pickpackit handlers no longer all report 0%; Rust frontend tests/clippy plus task smoke/e2e pass.

title: Axum Multipart extractor
description:
Title: Axum Multipart extractor

Type: feature
Priority: 1
Labels: rust,axum,pickpackit,scan

## Symptom

After the crate-bridge target-cache fix, Shatter can run a whole Pickpackit
`src/handlers` scan with `--max-iterations 1` without exhausting disk, but one
handler still fails because the Rust Axum adapter rejects `Multipart`.

Observed in `/tmp/shatter-pickpackit-str-qsrb-handlers-full-1iter-nodebug.json`:

```text
Scan complete: 71 completed, 4 failed, 0 unsupported, 0 skipped

/home/ketan/.local/share/worktrees/pickpackit/shatter-str-l5ru-validation/api/src/handlers/item_images.rs::upload_item_image:
error: exploration error: unsupported: axum handler has unsupported extractor types: Multipart
```

This is a Shatter adapter/extractor support gap, not a Pickpackit feature-code
failure.

## Smallest Known Reproduction

From the Pickpackit API validation worktree:

```bash
env PATH=/home/ketan/project/shatter/target/release:/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-rust/target/release:$PATH \
  DATABASE_URL=postgres://pickpackit:pickpackit@localhost:55432/pickpackit \
  SHATTER_RUST_SRC=/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-rust \
  SHATTER_RUNTIME_PATH=/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-rust-runtime \
  shatter scan src/handlers/item_images.rs \
    --project-dir . \
    --require-rust \
    --timeout-total 1800 --timeout-per-fn 300 --timeout-explore 240 \
    --request-timeout 300 --exec-timeout 240 --build-timeout 300 \
    --max-iterations 1 \
    --progress --color never \
    -o /tmp/shatter-pickpackit-item-images-1iter.json
```

The failing symbol is `upload_item_image`.

## Current Code Facts

- `shatter-rust/src/adapters.rs` classifies supported Axum extractors.
- Existing supported extractor categories include path/query/json/form/state-style extractors, but the current scan reports `Multipart` as unsupported.
- `shatter-rust/src/executor.rs` contains the Axum harness and crate-backed Axum driver generation used by Pickpackit handlers.

## Desired Behavior

Shatter should execute Axum handlers with a `Multipart` extractor well enough to
include Pickpackit `upload_item_image` in behavior-map generation. If full
multipart body synthesis is too broad for one issue, a deterministic minimal
multipart input that reaches handler validation/error branches is acceptable as
the first end-to-end support slice.

## Acceptance Checks

- Add focused Rust frontend tests proving `Multipart` is recognized and routed
  through the Axum adapter instead of being rejected during analysis/execution.
- Add or update an executable harness test that exercises an Axum handler with a
  `Multipart` parameter.
- The Pickpackit `item_images.rs::upload_item_image` scan no longer fails with
  `unsupported extractor types: Multipart`.
- `cargo test --manifest-path shatter-rust/Cargo.toml`, Rust frontend clippy,
  `task smoke`, and `task e2e` pass.

## Scope

In scope:

- Axum `Multipart` extractor recognition and harness input construction.
- Minimal multipart request synthesis sufficient for Pickpackit scanning.
- Tests for the new adapter behavior.

Out of scope:

- Pickpackit feature-code changes.
- Full multipart fuzzing or rich file-content generation beyond what is needed
  for handler execution.
- Branch coverage strategy for handlers that already execute.
acceptance_criteria: Multipart is accepted by the Axum adapter, upload_item_image no longer fails with unsupported extractor types: Multipart, and Rust frontend tests/clippy plus task smoke/e2e pass.

title: Crate bridge target bloat
description:
Title: Crate bridge target bloat

Type: bug
Priority: 1
Labels: rust,crate-bridge,scan,pickpackit

## Symptom

After `str-hc0j` fixed the missing `shatter-crate-bridge` feature activation, a
whole-corpus Pickpackit handler scan no longer collapses with
`shatter_rust_runtime` or broken-pipe failures. It still cannot finish the whole
feature corpus because crate-bridge harness builds exhaust disk while compiling
many independent Cargo target directories.

Observed scan result from the Pickpackit validation API worktree:

```text
Scan complete: 12 completed, 63 failed, 0 unsupported, 0 skipped
```

The saved report `/tmp/shatter-pickpackit-str-hc0j-handlers-full-1iter.json`
contains 124 occurrences of `No space left on device`. Representative failure
snippets include:

```text
error: failed to write to .../.shatter/cache/harness/rust/crate-bridge/f960294eb500c021/target/debug/deps/...: No space left on device (os error 28)
```

and:

```text
error: failed to write .../.shatter/cache/harness/rust/crate-bridge/603f282fcdb9b637/target/debug/.fingerprint/.../invoked.timestamp
Caused by:
  No space left on device (os error 28)
```

The failures are in Shatter-generated crate-bridge harness target directories,
not in Pickpackit feature code.

## Smallest Known Reproduction

From `/home/ketan/.local/share/worktrees/pickpackit/shatter-str-l5ru-validation/api`
after building Shatter from `main`:

```bash
env PATH=/home/ketan/project/shatter/target/release:/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-rust/target/release:$PATH \
  DATABASE_URL=postgres://pickpackit:pickpackit@localhost:55432/pickpackit \
  SHATTER_RUST_SRC=/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-rust \
  SHATTER_RUNTIME_PATH=/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-rust-runtime \
  shatter scan src/handlers \
    --project-dir . \
    --require-rust \
    --parallelism 4 --parallelism-min 1 --parallelism-max 4 \
    --timeout-total 7200 --timeout-per-fn 300 --timeout-explore 240 \
    --request-timeout 300 --exec-timeout 240 --build-timeout 300 \
    --max-iterations 1 \
    --progress --color never \
    -o /tmp/shatter-pickpackit-main-handlers-full-1iter.json
```

Clear the generated cache before rerunning if prior failed builds consumed disk:

```bash
rm -rf .shatter/cache/harness/rust/crate-bridge
```

## Current Code Facts

- `shatter-rust/src/executor.rs::stable_crate_bridge_dir` creates one stable
  cache directory per crate-bridge harness under
  `SHATTER_HARNESS_CACHE/rust/crate-bridge/<key>` or a temp directory.
- `shatter-rust/src/executor.rs::build_and_spawn_crate_bridge_harness` currently
  sets `target_dir = harness_dir.join("target")` and passes that path as
  `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` to `cargo check` and `cargo build`.
- As a result, a whole-corpus scan of a large crate can compile the same
  dependency graph into many independent `crate-bridge/<key>/target` trees.
- `standalone_target_dir()` already centralizes standalone harness target output
  under `SHATTER_HARNESS_CACHE/rust/standalone/target`, so there is an existing
  local pattern for sharing Cargo metadata across generated harness builds.

## Desired Behavior

Crate-bridge harness compilation should keep disk use bounded enough for a
whole Pickpackit `src/handlers` scan to run across the corpus. Reusing Cargo
build artifacts across crate-bridge harnesses, pruning failed build output, or
another bounded target-cache strategy is acceptable if it preserves correctness
and does not require Pickpackit feature-code changes.

## Acceptance Checks

- Add a regression test or focused unit coverage showing crate-bridge harness
  builds no longer allocate a separate full Cargo target directory under every
  per-function harness cache key. For this issue, "bounded" means repeated
  whole-corpus crate-bridge harness builds share or prune Cargo build artifacts
  enough to avoid one dependency graph copy per harness key.
- The Pickpackit whole `src/handlers` scan with `--max-iterations 1` gets past
  the `No space left on device` build failure mode.
- Existing crate-bridge and Rust frontend tests pass.
- `task smoke` and `task e2e` pass.

## Scope

In scope:

- Crate-bridge target directory selection, cleanup, or cache-bounding behavior.
- Tests around generated harness target/cache paths.
- Validation against the Pickpackit whole handler corpus.

Out of scope:

- Pickpackit feature-code changes.
- Seed strategy or branch-coverage improvements after harness builds complete.
- Reworking non-crate-bridge harness modes unless directly required to preserve
  existing behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Crate-bridge harness builds do not allocate one full Cargo target tree per harness key; Pickpackit whole src/handlers scan with max-iterations 1 gets past No space left on device; existing crate-bridge/Rust frontend tests, task smoke, and task e2e pass.

title: Crate bridge feature dep omitted
description:
Title: Crate bridge feature dep omitted

Type: bug
Priority: 1
Labels: rust,crate-bridge,scan,pickpackit

## Symptom

After the Axum crate-backed harness fixes landed on `main`, a whole-corpus
Pickpackit handlers scan still fails almost every non-trivial handler. The
top-level scan symptom is noisy:

```text
Scan complete: 1 completed, 74 failed, 0 unsupported, 0 skipped
... frontend error: failed to write to frontend stdin: Broken pipe (os error 32)
```

The first concrete failure artifact shows the real cause: the crate-bridge
shadow crate is instrumented with `shatter_rust_runtime::branch_hit(...)`, but
the generated driver depends on the shadow crate without enabling its
`shatter-crate-bridge` feature. As a result, Cargo compiles the instrumented
shadow crate without the optional runtime dependency visible, and compilation
fails with `E0433`.

## Smallest Known Reproduction

From the Pickpackit validation API worktree:

```bash
cd /home/ketan/.local/share/worktrees/pickpackit/shatter-str-l5ru-validation/api

env PATH=/home/ketan/project/shatter/target/release:/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-rust/target/release:$PATH \
  DATABASE_URL=postgres://pickpackit:pickpackit@localhost:55432/pickpackit \
  RUST_BACKTRACE=1 \
  SHATTER_RUST_SRC=/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-rust \
  SHATTER_RUNTIME_PATH=/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-rust-runtime \
  shatter scan src/handlers \
    --project-dir . \
    --require-rust \
    --include '**/bundles.rs' \
    --parallelism 1 --parallelism-min 1 --parallelism-max 1 \
    --timeout-total 900 --timeout-per-fn 300 --timeout-explore 240 \
    --request-timeout 300 --exec-timeout 240 --build-timeout 300 \
    --max-iterations 1 \
    --progress --color never \
    -o /tmp/shatter-pickpackit-main-bundles-serial-1.json
```

Observed artifact:

```text
shatter-artifacts/scan-results/4bc304bc96e41deb46522f99b6a6e3c09bf52e558a58725b5f22182495911f31/functions/00001_home_ketan_.local_share_worktrees_pickpackit_shatter-str-l5ru-validation_api_src_handlers_bundles.rs__apply_bundle.json
```

The artifact's failure reason begins:

```text
execute error (CompilationError): ... error[E0433]: failed to resolve: use of unresolved module or unlinked crate `shatter_rust_runtime`
```

The broader whole-corpus evidence is:

```text
/tmp/shatter-pickpackit-main-handlers-full.json
Scan complete: 1 completed, 74 failed, 0 unsupported, 0 skipped
```

## Current Code Facts

- `shatter-rust/src/executor.rs::inject_crate_bridge_feature` mutates the
  staged user crate `Cargo.toml` to add:
  - `shatter-rust-runtime = { path = "...", optional = true }`
  - `[features] shatter-crate-bridge = ["dep:shatter-rust-runtime"]`
- The generated shadow crate confirms that feature exists:
  `.shatter/cache/harness/rust/crate-bridge/4a463a0acae2d4bd/crate-shadow/Cargo.toml`
  contains `shatter-crate-bridge = ["dep:shatter-rust-runtime"]`.
- The generated driver manifest omits the feature activation:
  `.shatter/cache/harness/rust/crate-bridge/4a463a0acae2d4bd/Cargo.toml`
  currently contains:
  `pickpackit-api = { path = ".../crate-shadow" }`
  instead of:
  `pickpackit-api = { path = ".../crate-shadow", features = ["shatter-crate-bridge"] }`.
- Instrumented shadow files call `shatter_rust_runtime::branch_hit(...)` and
  `shatter_rust_runtime::loop_enter(...)`, so the feature must be active
  whenever the driver compiles the shadow crate.

## Desired Behavior

Crate-bridge driver generation must enable the staged crate's
`shatter-crate-bridge` feature when compiling instrumented shadow crates, so
instrumented Pickpackit handlers compile and execute instead of surfacing
`E0433` or downstream broken-pipe errors.

## Acceptance Checks

- Add a regression test showing the generated crate-bridge driver manifest
  activates `features = ["shatter-crate-bridge"]` for the staged crate
  dependency.
- The narrow Pickpackit `bundles.rs` scan gets past this `E0433:
  shatter_rust_runtime` compilation failure.
- The whole Pickpackit `src/handlers` scan no longer collapses into 74
  broken-pipe failures from this missing feature activation.
- Touched Rust frontend quality gates pass.

## Scope

In scope:

- Crate-bridge driver Cargo.toml generation.
- Tests around staged crate feature activation.
- Improving the surfaced error only if needed to verify the fix.

Out of scope:

- Pickpackit feature-code changes.
- New Pickpackit data generators or seed strategy.
- Fixes for later unsupported extractors or branch-coverage gaps after this
  compile blocker is removed.
acceptance_criteria: Generated crate-bridge driver manifest enables shatter-crate-bridge feature for the staged crate; Pickpackit bundles.rs scan gets past E0433 shatter_rust_runtime; whole Pickpackit handlers scan no longer collapses into missing-feature broken-pipe failures; touched Rust frontend gates pass.

title: Axum harness subprocess exits
description:
Title: Axum harness subprocess exits on update_workspace

Type: bug
Priority: 1
Labels: rust,harness,scan,pickpackit

## Symptom

After Shatter resolves Pickpackit Axum handlers with custom generators and uses
the public re-export path for `update_workspace`, the narrow `workspaces.rs`
scan progresses past compile time but fails during execution:

```text
Scan complete: 1 completed, 1 failed, 0 unsupported, 0 skipped
update_workspace: error: exploration error: unexpected response from frontend:
execute error (InternalError): output parse error: harness subprocess terminated unexpectedly
```

The successful sibling function in the same scan is `workspaces`, which returns
an Axum response with HTTP status 500 under the current seeded inputs. The
failure is specific to the `update_workspace` execution path after the harness
starts.

## Smallest Known Reproduction

From the Pickpackit validation API worktree:

```bash
cd /home/ketan/.local/share/worktrees/pickpackit/shatter-str-l5ru-validation/api
docker compose -f ../docker-compose.yml up -d postgres
```

Use a Shatter build that includes `str-p6w3` or later, rebuild the custom Rust
frontend, then run:

```bash
env PATH=<shatter-branch>/target/release:<shatter-branch>/shatter-rust/target/release:$PATH \
  SHATTER_RUST_SRC=<shatter-branch>/shatter-rust \
  shatter build-frontend rust --config .shatter -o .shatter-cache/bin --color never

env PATH=<shatter-branch>/target/release:<shatter-branch>/shatter-rust/target/release:$PATH \
  DATABASE_URL=postgres://pickpackit:pickpackit@localhost:55432/pickpackit \
  SHATTER_RUST_SRC=<shatter-branch>/shatter-rust \
  SHATTER_RUNTIME_PATH=<shatter-branch>/shatter-rust-runtime \
  shatter scan src/handlers \
    --project-dir . \
    --require-rust \
    --include '**/workspaces.rs' \
    --parallelism 1 --parallelism-min 1 --parallelism-max 1 \
    --timeout-total 420 --timeout-per-fn 240 --timeout-explore 180 \
    --request-timeout 240 --exec-timeout 180 --build-timeout 240 \
    --max-iterations 1 \
    --progress --color never \
    -o /tmp/shatter-pickpackit-scan-workspaces.json
```

Observed on 2026-05-30 against `str-p6w3-private-module-harness`:

- `update_workspace` starts first and writes a scan artifact, then fails after
  about 97 seconds with `harness subprocess terminated unexpectedly`.
- `workspaces` then completes after the scan continues.
- The report at `/tmp/shatter-pickpackit-str-p6w3-scan-workspaces.json` records
  `attempted_functions: 2`, `completed_functions: 1`, `failed_functions: 1`,
  and no unsupported or skipped functions.

## Current Code Facts

- `shatter-rust/src/executor.rs` owns Axum crate-backed harness generation,
  native replay wiring, harness process spawning, and JSON response parsing.
- `execute_axum_handler_crate_backed` now computes the crate module path,
  resolves a public invocation target when the source module re-exports the
  handler, instruments the source, builds a harness, and calls
  `harness.execute(inputs, timeout_ms)`.
- The error is no longer `module ... is private`; the harness compiles and the
  failure is reported as an `InternalError` while parsing frontend output after
  the harness subprocess exits.
- Pickpackit `api/src/handlers/workspaces.rs::update_workspace` takes
  `State<AppState>`, `CurrentAccount`, `Path<uuid::Uuid>`, and
  `Json<UpdateWorkspaceRequest>`. The validation `.shatter/config.yaml` maps
  `State`, `CurrentAccount`, and parameter `current` to native generators.

## Desired Behavior

The Axum crate-backed harness should not terminate without a well-formed
execution result. If the handler panics or returns an application error, Shatter
should report it as a structured `thrown_error` or Axum HTTP response artifact
instead of an output parse failure.

## Acceptance Checks

- Add a regression that reproduces a crate-backed Axum handler process exit or
  panic during execution and verifies Shatter returns a well-formed execution
  result rather than an output parse failure.
- The narrow Pickpackit `workspaces.rs` scan no longer fails
  `update_workspace` with `harness subprocess terminated unexpectedly`.
- Existing Rust frontend/core quality gates for the touched area pass.

## Scope

In scope:

- Axum crate-backed harness execution/error handling.
- Harness subprocess output parsing and structured error recovery.
- Native generator replay interactions only if they are the direct cause of
  this subprocess termination.

Out of scope:

- Pickpackit feature-code changes.
- Improving branch coverage or seed quality after the handler returns a
  well-formed result.
- Multipart or other unrelated unsupported extractor work.
acceptance_criteria: Regression returns a well-formed execution result when a crate-backed Axum handler subprocess exits or panics; Pickpackit workspaces.rs scan no longer fails update_workspace with harness subprocess terminated unexpectedly; touched Rust/core gates pass.

title: Rust harness uses private module path
description:
Title: Rust harness uses private module path

Type: bug
Priority: 1
Labels: rust,harness,scan,pickpackit

## Symptom

After scan resolves Pickpackit Axum handlers with custom generators, `scan` can execute `workspaces` but fails `update_workspace` during Rust crate-bridge harness compilation.

The generated harness calls:

```rust
pickpackit_api::handlers::workspaces::update_workspace
```

That module is private in Pickpackit:

```text
error[E0603]: module `workspaces` is private
  --> src/main.rs:78:91
   |
78 |         let app = Router::new().route("/test/:p0", routing::any(pickpackit_api::handlers::workspaces::update_workspace));
   |                                                                                           ^^^^^^^^^^ private module
note: the module `workspaces` is defined here
  --> .../crate-shadow/src/handlers/mod.rs:25:1
   |
25 | mod workspaces;
   | ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
help: consider importing this function instead
   |
78 -         let app = Router::new().route("/test/:p0", routing::any(pickpackit_api::handlers::workspaces::update_workspace));
78 +         let app = Router::new().route("/test/:p0", routing::any(pickpackit_api::handlers::update_workspace));
```

## Smallest Known Reproduction

From the Pickpackit API worktree with `.shatter/config.yaml` defining native generators for `State`, `CurrentAccount`, and param `current`, run:

```bash
env PATH=<shatter-branch>/target/release:<shatter-branch>/shatter-rust/target/release:$PATH \
  DATABASE_URL=postgres://pickpackit:pickpackit@localhost:55432/pickpackit \
  SHATTER_RUST_SRC=<shatter-branch>/shatter-rust \
  SHATTER_RUNTIME_PATH=<shatter-branch>/shatter-rust-runtime \
  shatter scan src/handlers \
    --project-dir . \
    --require-rust \
    --include '**/workspaces.rs' \
    --parallelism 1 --parallelism-min 1 --parallelism-max 1 \
    --timeout-total 420 --timeout-per-fn 240 --timeout-explore 180 \
    --request-timeout 240 --exec-timeout 180 --build-timeout 240 \
    --max-iterations 1 \
    --progress --color never \
    -o /tmp/shatter-pickpackit-scan-workspaces.json
```

Observed result:

- `workspaces` completes.
- `update_workspace` fails with `CompilationError` because the harness uses the private module path.

## Desired Behavior

When a Rust function is publicly re-exported from a module, generated crate-bridge Axum harness code should invoke the public reachable path instead of the private source-module path.

For this Pickpackit case, the harness should use:

```rust
pickpackit_api::handlers::update_workspace
```

## Current Code Facts

- `shatter-rust/src/adapters.rs` defines `FileContext { use_paths, has_tokio_macro }`, Axum adapter recognition, and invocation model selection for Rust targets.
- `shatter-rust/src/handler.rs` populates adapter hints and invocation models during analyze, caches analyses by canonical file path and function name, and recomputes the analysis in execute if no cached entry exists.
- `shatter-rust/src/executor.rs` owns crate-bridge harness generation and contains the existing Axum extractor and crate-bridge wrapper regression tests around `generate_crate_bridge_wrapper`.
- Existing public re-export metadata appears in the Rust analysis/harness path only indirectly today; the follow-up should determine whether to reuse existing re-export handling or add explicit public invocation path metadata.

## Acceptance Checks

- Add a regression test covering a Rust handler whose source module is private but whose function is publicly re-exported.
- The generated crate-bridge Axum harness uses the public re-export path for execution.
- The narrow Pickpackit `workspaces.rs` scan no longer fails `update_workspace` with `module ... is private`.
- Existing Rust/core quality gates for the touched area pass.

## Scope

In scope:

- Rust frontend or harness path selection for crate-bridge execution.
- Re-export metadata propagation if needed to select a public invocation path.

Out of scope:

- Pickpackit feature-code changes.
- Multipart support or other unsupported extractor work.
- Increasing Pickpackit branch coverage beyond unblocking this compile failure.
acceptance_criteria: Regression covers private source module with public re-export; crate-bridge Axum harness invokes the public path; Pickpackit workspaces.rs scan no longer fails update_workspace with private module compile error; touched Rust/core gates pass.

title: Scan Axum custom generators
description:
Title: Scan uses Axum unsupported path despite custom generators
Tracker: Beads
Repo: shatter
Type: bug
Priority: 1
Labels: rust,axum,scan,pickpackit,generators

# Scan uses Axum unsupported path despite custom generators

## Problem

After `str-40s2` landed, targeted `shatter explore` can execute a Pickpackit Axum handler with configured Rust native generators for `State<AppState>` and `CurrentAccount`. The same handler still fails immediately under `shatter scan`, reporting `CurrentAccount` as an unsupported extractor instead of using the custom generator-backed Axum execution path.

This blocks the Pickpackit whole-handler-corpus goal because the scan command is the path used to run the full `api/src/handlers` corpus. A full handler scan on 2026-05-30 completed only `health` and failed 74/75 attempted handlers, mostly with:

```text
unsupported: axum handler has unsupported extractor types: CurrentAccount
```

## Reproduction

Use Shatter `main` at commit `3971419a` or later and the Pickpackit validation worktree:

```bash
cd /home/ketan/.local/share/worktrees/pickpackit/shatter-str-l5ru-validation/api
docker compose -f ../docker-compose.yml up -d postgres
```

The validation worktree has build/usage-only Shatter config under `api/.shatter/`:

- `.shatter/config.yaml` maps `State`, `CurrentAccount`, and parameter `current` to `generators/pickpackit.rs`.
- `.shatter/generators/pickpackit.rs` creates `AppState` and `CurrentAccount` live values against the local Postgres test database.

Build Shatter and the custom Rust frontend:

```bash
cd /home/ketan/project/shatter
cargo build --release --bin shatter
cargo build --release --manifest-path shatter-rust/Cargo.toml

cd /home/ketan/.local/share/worktrees/pickpackit/shatter-str-l5ru-validation/api
PATH=/home/ketan/project/shatter/target/release:/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-rust/target/release:$PATH \
SHATTER_RUST_SRC=/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-rust \
  shatter build-frontend rust --config .shatter -o .shatter-cache/bin --color never
```

Targeted explore succeeds:

```bash
PATH=/home/ketan/project/shatter/target/release:/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-rust/target/release:$PATH \
DATABASE_URL=postgres://pickpackit:pickpackit@localhost:55432/pickpackit \
SHATTER_RUST_SRC=/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-rust \
SHATTER_RUNTIME_PATH=/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-rust-runtime \
  shatter explore src/handlers/workspaces.rs:workspaces \
    --project-dir . --clean --no-cache --require-rust \
    --max-iterations 1 --per-function-timeout 240 --timeout-explore 180 \
    --request-timeout 240 --exec-timeout 180 --build-timeout 240 \
    --color never -o /tmp/shatter-pickpackit-main-workspaces-postscan.json
```

Observed result:

```text
[progress] completed 1/1: workspaces (128.4s)
[batch 1/1] workspaces: 1 iters, 1 paths, 0/1 branches, 129.3s (ok)
[info] Wrote explore artifact for workspaces -> ./shatter-artifacts/explore-results/src_handlers_workspaces.rs/00022_workspaces.json
```

The equivalent scan path fails immediately, even with `--project-dir .`:

```bash
PATH=/home/ketan/project/shatter/target/release:/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-rust/target/release:$PATH \
DATABASE_URL=postgres://pickpackit:pickpackit@localhost:55432/pickpackit \
SHATTER_RUST_SRC=/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-rust \
SHATTER_RUNTIME_PATH=/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-rust-runtime \
  shatter scan src/handlers \
    --project-dir . --require-rust --include '**/workspaces.rs' \
    --parallelism 1 --parallelism-min 1 --parallelism-max 1 \
    --timeout-total 420 --timeout-per-fn 240 --timeout-explore 180 \
    --request-timeout 240 --exec-timeout 180 --build-timeout 240 \
    --max-iterations 1 --progress --color never \
    -o /tmp/shatter-pickpackit-main-scan-workspaces-projectdir.json
```

Observed result:

```text
Scan complete: 0 completed, 2 failed, 0 unsupported, 0 skipped
workspaces: unsupported: axum handler has unsupported extractor types: CurrentAccount
```

## Current Code Facts

- `shatter-cli/src/commands/build_frontend.rs` can build a custom Rust frontend from `.shatter/config.yaml` generator mappings.
- `shatter-rust/src/executor.rs` and `shatter-rust/src/handler.rs` now support crate-backed Axum native generator replay for targeted explore.
- `shatter-cli/src/commands/explore.rs` successfully uses the project/custom frontend context for the targeted command above.
- `shatter-cli/src/commands/scan.rs` should use the same project/custom frontend context for each explored Rust function, but currently the scan path reaches the generic unsupported-extractor classification before native generator-backed Axum execution.

## Desired Behavior

`shatter scan` should preserve the same Rust project config, custom frontend binary, generator mappings, and execution profile used by targeted `shatter explore` for the same file/function. A scan of `src/handlers/workspaces.rs` with `--project-dir .` and the Pickpackit `.shatter` config should execute `workspaces` instead of rejecting `CurrentAccount` as unsupported.

## In Scope

- Fix scan-to-explore plumbing for Rust custom frontend/project config so native generator-backed Axum handlers work under scan.
- Add a Shatter regression that reproduces scan/explore parity for an Axum handler with a custom request-part extractor satisfied by a native generator.
- Validate against Pickpackit `src/handlers/workspaces.rs` with the command above.

## Out of Scope

- Adding support for `Multipart`.
- Improving Pickpackit seed quality or branch coverage after the handler executes.
- Changing Pickpackit feature code.
- Claiming the full Pickpackit corpus reaches 90% after this single fix.

## Acceptance Checks

- A focused Shatter test fails before the fix and passes after, proving scan uses the same custom Rust generator-backed Axum path as targeted explore.
- The Pickpackit scan reproduction for `src/handlers/workspaces.rs` no longer reports `CurrentAccount` as an unsupported extractor.
- Existing Rust frontend, core, CLI, smoke, and E2E checks pass for the changed scope.
acceptance_criteria: Scan uses the same custom Rust generator-backed Axum path as targeted explore; Pickpackit workspaces scan no longer reports CurrentAccount unsupported; relevant Rust/core/CLI/smoke/E2E checks pass.

title: Rust native generator replay
description:
Title: Rust native generator replay
Tracker: Beads
Repo: shatter
Type: feature
Priority: 1
Labels: rust,generators,harness,pickpackit

# Rust native generator replay

## Problem

Shatter's Rust native generator API can produce live values, but those values are stored only inside the Rust frontend process. Rust function execution commonly happens in compiled subprocess harnesses. A generated live value represented as `{"__shatter_native": true, "handle": "h_0001"}` cannot be downcast or used inside the harness process because the harness has a different address space.

This blocks `str-40s2` (`Rust Axum native handles`). Passing `NativeRegistry` or `HandleTable` from `shatter-rust/src/handler.rs` through `adapters.rs` into `executor.rs` would still not make frontend-process `Box<dyn Any + Send>` values visible to the compiled child binary that runs the Axum harness.

## Current Code Facts

- `shatter-rust/src/generators.rs::NativeRegistry::generate` calls a registered native generator, stores its returned `Box<dyn Any + Send>` in `HandleTable`, and returns this sentinel:

```json
{"__shatter_native": true, "handle": "h_0001"}
```

- `shatter-rust/src/handler.rs::generate_response` receives the generator file path and generator name from the `Generate` request, but native generation currently returns only the handle sentinel plus `generator_id`/`recipe` response fields.
- `shatter-core/src/input_gen.rs::prefetch_custom_values` stores the frontend's generated JSON value as the candidate input.
- `shatter-core/src/input_gen.rs::generate_inputs_with_custom` consumes only `GeneratedEntry.value`, so execute inputs keep the sentinel but lose any side-channel metadata unless the sentinel itself carries it.
- `shatter-cli/src/commands/build_frontend.rs` compiles configured Rust native generators into a custom frontend binary, not into per-function child harnesses.
- `shatter-rust/src/executor.rs` builds separate Rust harness crates under `.shatter` and runs them as child processes. Those child processes cannot access the frontend's `HandleTable`.
- `shatter-rust/src/executor.rs::generate_axum_harness` is one consumer that will need generated live values inside the harness process for `State<T>` and request extensions, but the subprocess replay problem is more general than Axum.

## Desired Behavior

Rust native generator outputs should be replayable inside subprocess harnesses. The execute input JSON should contain enough metadata for the harness builder to include the correct generator source and call the generator inside the child process with the recorded recipe.

For this increment:

- Preserve native generator replay metadata in the generated input value itself, including at least:
  - generator file path
  - generator name
  - recipe
  - a marker that distinguishes replayable Rust native values from ordinary JSON
- Keep existing JSON generator behavior unchanged where the value is already JSON-serializable.
- Teach Rust harness generation/build plumbing to copy or include the referenced `.rs` generator module(s) when an execute input contains replayable Rust native values.
- In the child harness, call the generator function with the recipe and downcast the returned `GeneratorResult.value` to the expected concrete Rust type.
- Report clear errors for missing generator source, missing replay metadata, generator panic/failure, or wrong downcast type.

This issue should not require a new config format. It can reuse the existing configured generator file path and generator name already present on `Generate` requests.

## In Scope

- Rust native generator sentinel/replay metadata shape.
- Core input-generation preservation of replay metadata through execute inputs.
- Rust subprocess harness generation/build support for replaying configured `.rs` native generators.
- Focused Rust regression coverage using a simple generated native type in a child harness process.

## Out of Scope

- Full `str-40s2` Axum `State<T>` / request-extension injection.
- Pickpackit-specific generator files or database setup.
- Changing the existing Rust native generator function signature.
- Designing a new generator configuration format.
- Making frontend-process `HandleTable` values cross process boundaries directly.

## Acceptance Checks

- Add a Rust regression proving a generated native value is materialized inside a subprocess harness from replay metadata, not read from the frontend-process `HandleTable`.
- The regression covers a wrong-type downcast or missing replay metadata and asserts a clear Shatter error.
- Existing Rust native generator unit tests continue to pass.
- Existing Rust executor crate-bridge tests continue to pass.
- `cargo test --manifest-path shatter-rust/Cargo.toml native` and the focused executor regression pass.
acceptance_criteria: Rust native generator replay metadata is preserved into execute inputs; subprocess harnesses materialize generated native values from replay metadata inside the child process; missing metadata/wrong type report clear errors; focused Rust native/executor regressions pass.

title: Rust Axum native handles
description:
Title: Rust Axum native handles
Tracker: Beads
Repo: shatter
Type: feature
Priority: 1
Labels: rust,axum,pickpackit,generators

# Rust Axum native handles

## Problem

After `str-027y` landed on `main`, Shatter no longer lets unsupported crate-bridge wrappers poison supported Pickpackit handlers. A targeted Pickpackit handler scan then improved enough for `api/src/handlers/health.rs::health` to execute, but almost every stateful API handler still fails because the Rust execution path cannot turn configured native-generator handles into typed live values for Axum extractors.

Post-`str-027y` targeted handler scan evidence:

- `attempted_functions`: 75
- `completed_functions`: 1
- `failed_functions`: 74
- `skipped_functions_count`: 0
- `unsupported_functions`: 0
- the completed function was `api/src/handlers/health.rs::health`

Representative blocked handler shapes include:

- `workspaces(State(state): State<AppState>, current: CurrentAccount)`
- `list_people(State(state): State<AppState>, current: CurrentAccount, Path(workspace_id): Path<Uuid>)`
- `create_person(State(state): State<AppState>, current: CurrentAccount, Path(workspace_id): Path<Uuid>, Json(payload): Json<CreatePersonRequest>)`
- `session(current: CurrentAccount)`

These are ordinary Axum request handlers. Their missing inputs are not JSON values: `AppState` contains a live `PgPool`, auth runtime, account service, assistant provider, and storage client; `CurrentAccount` is normally recovered from request extensions by its `FromRequestParts` implementation.

## Smallest Reproduction

Use Shatter `main` at or after commit `db64b4c5` (`bd sync: close str-027y`) and an isolated Pickpackit validation worktree. Build Shatter first:

```bash
cd ~/project/shatter
cargo build --release --bin shatter
cargo build --release --manifest-path shatter-rust/Cargo.toml
```

Then run a targeted Pickpackit handler scan:

```bash
cd /home/ketan/.local/share/worktrees/pickpackit/shatter-str-l5ru-validation/api
PATH=$HOME/project/shatter/target/release:$HOME/project/shatter/shatter-rust/target/release:$PATH \
  shatter scan src/handlers --require-rust \
  --parallelism 2 --parallelism-max 2 \
  --timeout-total 1800 --timeout-per-fn 60 --timeout-explore 60 \
  --progress --color never \
  -o /tmp/shatter-pickpackit-axum-native-handles/scan-handlers.json \
  -o /tmp/shatter-pickpackit-axum-native-handles/scan-handlers.md
```

Expected current result before this issue is fixed: `health` completes, while handlers such as `workspaces`, `list_people`, `list_luggage`, `list_catalog_kits`, and `session` do not complete because their extractor arguments require `AppState` or `CurrentAccount`.

## Current Code Facts

- Shatter config already has generator mappings:
  - `defaults.generators` maps a type name to a generator file.
  - `defaults.param_generators` maps a parameter name to a generator file.
  - `shatter-core/src/input_gen.rs::resolve_value_sources` chooses `param_generators` by exact parameter name first, then `generators` by `type_name`.
- `shatter-cli/src/commands/build_frontend.rs` already supports Rust native generators through `shatter build-frontend rust`.
  - It collects `.rs` generator files from the config.
  - It copies them into a temporary custom Rust frontend crate.
  - It registers each generator under the exact config key with `registry.register("<name>", Box::new(<module>::<name>))`.
- Rust native generator functions are expected to have the registered function shape used by `NativeRegistry`:

```rust
fn Name(recipe: Option<serde_json::Value>) -> shatter_rust::generators::GeneratorResult
```

- `shatter-rust/src/generators.rs::NativeRegistry::generate` stores the returned live `Box<dyn Any + Send>` in a `HandleTable` and returns this JSON sentinel to core:

```json
{"__shatter_native": true, "handle": "h_0001"}
```

- `shatter-core/src/input_gen.rs::prefetch_custom_values` preserves the generated sentinel as the candidate input value.
- `shatter-rust/src/handler.rs::handle_execute` does not pass `self.native_registry` or its `HandleTable` into `execute_adapter_owned` or the normal executor path.
- `shatter-rust/src/executor.rs::generate_axum_harness` currently handles `State<T>` by generating code equivalent to:

```rust
let state_value = input_obj.get("state").cloned().unwrap_or(Value::Object(serde_json::Map::new()));
let app = app.with_state(serde_json::from_value(state_value).unwrap_or_default());
```

- That JSON/default state path cannot work for Pickpackit's `AppState`.
- `shatter-rust/src/executor.rs` also has a crate-bridge guard that reports Axum `State<T>` as `not constructible without an app-state generator`, but the available generator output cannot currently reach the Axum harness as a typed value.
- Pickpackit `api/src/app.rs` defines `pub struct AppState` with fields `pool: PgPool`, `auth: AuthRuntime`, `accounts: AccountService`, `assistant: Arc<dyn AssistantProvider>`, and `storage: StorageClient`.
- Pickpackit `api/src/auth/current.rs` defines `pub struct CurrentAccount` and `pub fn test(id: Uuid) -> Self`.
- Pickpackit `api/src/handlers/test_support.rs` shows the project already has test-only construction patterns:
  - `shared_pool() -> PgPool`
  - `state(pool: PgPool) -> AppState`
  - `seed_workspace(pool: &PgPool, prefix: &str, with_member: bool) -> (Uuid, Uuid)`
- Pickpackit tests call handlers directly with values shaped like `workspaces(State(st), CurrentAccount::test(owner_account))`.

## Desired Behavior

The Rust frontend should resolve configured Rust native-generator sentinels during Axum handler execution and use the typed live values in the synthetic Axum request path.

For this focused increment:

- A generator mapped to the state type can provide the live value used by `.with_state(...)`.
- A generator mapped to a request-part extractor type such as `CurrentAccount` can provide a live value inserted into request extensions before `Router::oneshot`.
- Existing JSON synthesis for `Path<T>`, `Query<T>`, and `Json<T>` remains unchanged.
- If a needed native handle is missing, stale, or has the wrong concrete type, Shatter reports a clear unsupported/runtime error instead of compiling a harness that silently falls back to `Default`.

The intended `CurrentAccount` path is request-extension insertion. Shatter should not reimplement or bypass `FromRequestParts`; Axum should still run the handler's normal extraction code, and that extractor should find the generated value in `request.extensions()`.

## In Scope

- Rust frontend execution plumbing so the Axum adapter can access native-generator handles at execute time.
- Axum harness generation changes for exactly:
  - generated `State<T>` live values
  - generated request extension values for custom `FromRequestParts` extractors
- Focused regression coverage in Shatter using a small Rust Axum fixture with:
  - one state type generated natively
  - one custom request-part extractor generated natively and recovered from extensions
  - `Path<T>` and `Json<T>` in the same handler call
- Pickpackit validation using a custom Rust frontend and generator config, if generator files/config are already present or can be added as build/usage infrastructure only.

## Out of Scope

- Designing a new generator configuration format.
- Changing the existing Rust native generator function signature.
- Rewriting Pickpackit handler feature code.
- Mocking SQLx/database behavior generally.
- Running Pickpackit handlers that genuinely require Postgres when the test database is unavailable.
- Supporting every possible Axum extractor. This issue is only for `State<T>` and request-extension values needed by Pickpackit.
- Claiming the whole Pickpackit corpus reaches 90% after this issue alone.

## Acceptance Checks

- Add a Shatter Rust regression fixture where an Axum handler with `State<AppStateLike>`, a custom `FromRequestParts` extractor, `Path<T>`, and `Json<T>` executes successfully using configured native-generator handles.
- The regression asserts that the custom extractor is satisfied through request extensions, not by directly calling the handler with prebuilt arguments.
- Missing or wrong-type native handles for `State<T>` / extension values produce a clear Shatter error.
- Existing Rust frontend crate-bridge and Axum tests pass.
- With Pickpackit's test Postgres available and build/usage-only generator config in place, at least one non-`health` stateful handler such as `workspaces` or `list_people` completes and emits parseable Shatter JSON output.
acceptance_criteria: Axum State<T> and request-extension native generator handles execute through the Rust handler path; focused regression covers State, custom FromRequestParts, Path, and Json; missing/wrong handles report clear errors; targeted Pickpackit validation completes at least one non-health stateful handler when Postgres/generators are available.
notes:
Blocked by str-2jf8: frontend-process native handles cannot be used directly by subprocess harnesses; generator replay metadata must land first.
Unblocked after str-2jf8 landed and closed on main at 489735e2deec039581b07c639e01d25bb5b4d35b.

title: Rust crate-bridge wrapper poisoning
description:
# Rust crate-bridge wrapper poisoning

## Problem

A Rust scan of Pickpackit fails every selected API function because the Rust crate-bridge harness emits wrappers for functions whose parameters or return values cannot satisfy the serde bounds used by the generated harness. One unsupported function then poisons the shared crate-bridge build for otherwise runnable functions in the same crate.

Baseline reproduction from issue `str-l5ru`:

```bash
cd /home/ketan/.local/share/worktrees/pickpackit/shatter-str-l5ru-validation
PATH=/home/ketan/.local/share/worktrees/shatter/str-l5ru-pickpackit-baseline/target/release:/home/ketan/.local/share/worktrees/shatter/str-l5ru-pickpackit-baseline/shatter-rust/target/release:$PATH \
  shatter scan api/src --require-rust --parallelism 4 --parallelism-max 4 \
  --timeout-total 3600 --timeout-per-fn 60 --timeout-explore 60 \
  --progress --color never \
  -o /tmp/shatter-pickpackit-baseline/scan-api.json \
  -o /tmp/shatter-pickpackit-baseline/scan-api.md
```

Observed summary from `/tmp/shatter-pickpackit-baseline/scan-api.json`:

- `attempted_functions`: 92
- `completed_functions`: 0
- `failed_functions`: 92
- `skipped_functions_count`: 10
- `unsupported_functions`: 10

The first failure, `api/src/app.rs::build_router`, fails during crate-shadow compilation because the generated wrapper calls `serde_json::from_value` for non-`DeserializeOwned` values and `serde_json::to_value` for non-`Serialize` return values:

```text
error[E0277]: the trait bound `config::Config: serde::de::DeserializeOwned` is not satisfied
error[E0277]: the trait bound `Pool<Postgres>: serde::de::DeserializeOwned` is not satisfied
error[E0277]: the trait bound `Router: serde::Serialize` is not satisfied
error[E0277]: the trait bound `CorsLayer: serde::Serialize` is not satisfied
```

The dry-run plan selected `build_router` even though its signature is not executable by the current JSON harness:

```json
{
  "name": "build_router",
  "params": [
    {"name": "config", "type": "Config"},
    {"name": "pool", "type": "PgPool"}
  ],
  "return_type": "Router"
}
```

The same scan also selected simple functions such as `api/src/handlers/health.rs::health`, whose source is:

```rust
pub async fn health() -> Json<Value> {
    Json(json!({ "ok": true, "service": "pickpackit-api" }))
}
```

`health` should not be blocked by `build_router`, but it failed after the poisoned shared harness build timed out. Near the end of the baseline run, repeated failed builds also filled `/home` via generated `api/.shatter/cache/harness/rust/crate-bridge/...` state; those no-space failures are a secondary symptom of retrying/retaining poisoned harnesses.

## Desired Behavior

The Rust frontend should not let unsupported crate-bridge wrappers poison a shared harness. Functions whose params or return values cannot be driven through the current JSON harness should be classified as unsupported before crate-shadow compilation, omitted from generated crate-bridge wrappers, or isolated so supported functions can still build and execute.

## Acceptance Checks

- Add regression coverage with a Rust crate containing at least:
  - one unsupported exported function with a non-`DeserializeOwned` parameter or non-`Serialize` return type
  - one supported exported function in the same crate
- The unsupported function is reported as unsupported/skipped with a clear serde-bound or non-JSON-harness reason, not as a build failure.
- The supported function in the same crate still compiles and executes successfully in the same scan/explore path.
- A targeted Pickpackit reproduction such as `api/src/handlers/health.rs::health` no longer fails because unrelated wrappers like `build_router` cannot compile.
- The fix avoids unbounded per-function crate-shadow growth from repeatedly compiling known-unsupported wrappers.

## In Scope

- Rust frontend executability filtering for crate-bridge targets.
- Crate-bridge wrapper generation and harness build input selection.
- Regression tests in Shatter for mixed supported/unsupported functions in one Rust crate.

## Out of Scope

- Adding Pickpackit-specific generators or modifying Pickpackit feature code.
- Teaching Shatter to synthesize live database pools, routers, Axum state, or storage clients.
- Solving all Rust handler exploration gaps after unsupported wrappers are correctly isolated.
acceptance_criteria: Unsupported Rust crate-bridge functions with non-JSON-harness params or returns are skipped/isolated instead of poisoning shared harness builds; a supported function in the same crate still executes; regression coverage proves mixed supported/unsupported crate behavior.

title: Template type factory
description:
Title: Template type factory
Tracker: Beads
Repo: shatter
Type: feature
Priority: 1
Labels: go,planner,zolem

## Problem

Shatter's latest Zolem scan still has 20 skips caused by `parse.Node`. Zolem does not directly accept or store `parse.Node` values in its public fixture APIs; the skipped type leaks out because Shatter structurally descends into `*text/template.Template` internals.

This makes a common standard-library runtime object look like an impossible user-defined struct. The useful value to synthesize is a parsed `*template.Template`, not a raw `parse.Node`.

## Current Code Facts

- `internal/fixture/fixture.go` defines `type Fixture struct { ... templateBody *template.Template ... }`.
- `Fixture.SetResponseTemplate` calls `parseResponseTemplate(string(body))` and stores the returned template in `f.templateBody`.
- `internal/fixture/template.go` defines `parseResponseTemplate(body string) (*template.Template, error)`.
- `parseResponseTemplate` constructs templates with:
  - `template.New("response.json.tmpl")`
  - `Option("missingkey=error")`
  - `Funcs(template.FuncMap{"json": templateJSON})`
  - `.Parse(body)`
- A repository search found no direct `parse.Node` references in Zolem's `cmd` or `internal` source. The scan evidence points to `text/template` internals, not a Zolem API that should receive arbitrary parse tree nodes.
- Shatter's existing terminology for constructed live values is `generators`, `native generators`, and `runtime values`. If the user-facing concept becomes "type factories", this issue should map that term onto the existing runtime-value/generator implementation instead of adding unrelated naming in one place.

## Expected Outcome

Shatter treats `*text/template.Template` as a first-party specialized runtime value for Go. The planner should not recursively require arbitrary `parse.Node` construction when it encounters a `*template.Template` field or parameter.

For initial support, a deterministic parsed template such as a simple JSON body is sufficient. Zolem-specific template functions are not required for generic standard-library support, but the design should allow repo-local native generators to override or customize templates when a project needs custom funcs.

## Proposed Scope

- Add a Go runtime-value/type-factory path for `*text/template.Template`.
- Generate the value through `template.New(...).Parse(...)` rather than through structural construction of `template.Template` internals.
- Treat `text/template/parse.Node` inside a `*template.Template` as owned internal state once the specialized template value is chosen.
- Add a focused regression fixture with a struct containing `*template.Template` so the planner demonstrates it no longer tries to synthesize `parse.Node`.

## In Scope

- First-party Shatter Go handling for `*text/template.Template`.
- Tests proving Shatter avoids `parse.Node` skips for template-backed structs.
- Documentation or naming updates only where needed to explain this as a specialized runtime value/type factory.

## Out of Scope

- Building arbitrary parse trees for `text/template/parse.Node`.
- Zolem-specific template function maps as the default first-party behavior.
- General support for every standard-library type with unexported internals.

## Acceptance Checks

- A Go regression test with a `*template.Template` field or parameter plans/explores without a `parse.Node` unsupported-type skip.
- The generated template is created through the public `text/template` API and can execute successfully in the regression test.
- A Shatter scan of Zolem shows the `parse.Node` skip bucket below the current 20 occurrences.
- Existing Go runtime-value/native-generator tests continue to pass.
acceptance_criteria: Template-backed Go fixture plans without parse.Node skips; generated template uses public text/template API; Zolem parse.Node bucket drops below 20 when scanned.
close_reason: Completed template runtime value support; Zolem dry-run parse.Node skip count is 0.

title: JSON interface candidates
description:
Title: JSON interface candidates
Tracker: Beads
Repo: shatter
Type: feature
Priority: 1
Labels: go,planner,zolem

## Problem

Shatter's latest Zolem scan still has 17 raw `interface{}` / erased-interface skip occurrences. These are not all cases where a domain-specific concrete type is missing. Several are ordinary Go helper patterns where the callee accepts `interface{}` / `any` and immediately narrows it through JSON encoding usage.

Examples from Zolem include `cmd/zolem/local_admin.go:534` `writeJSON(w http.ResponseWriter, status int, v any)` and `cmd/zolemc/main.go:666` `writeJSONObject(w io.Writer, v any)`. Both pass `v` to JSON encoding and do not require a repo-specific domain object.

Today Shatter treats the erased type as impossible to synthesize. For these encode-style patterns, Shatter should behave more like it does for dynamically typed inputs: try a small, deterministic set of plausible concrete values, observe which paths are meaningful, and keep the search bounded.

## Current Code Facts

- Zolem has empty-interface JSON helper patterns such as parameters named `v` or `out` that are used with `encoding/json`.
- `cmd/zolem/local_admin.go:534` defines `writeJSON(w http.ResponseWriter, status int, v any)` and calls `json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(v)`.
- `cmd/zolemc/main.go:666` defines `writeJSONObject(w io.Writer, v any)` and calls `json.Marshal(v)`.
- `cmd/zolem/local_admin.go:517` defines `decodeRequestJSON(req *http.Request, v any)` and calls `json.NewDecoder(req.Body).Decode(v)`, but decode-style pointer destination support is out of scope for this first issue.
- `cmd/zolemc/main.go:586` defines `getJSON(ctx context.Context, p string, out any)` and `doJSON(..., in, out any)`, but client-side decode/input-output narrowing is out of scope for this first issue.
- Zolem also has non-empty domain interfaces such as `response.Generator`, `Recorder`, `Selector`, `specFetcher`, `localServer`, and provider generator interfaces. Those require implementor discovery or repo-specific values and should not be conflated with empty-interface candidate generation.
- Recent Shatter improvements removed `context.Context`, `http.ResponseWriter`, and partial-adapter skips from the latest Zolem scan, leaving `interface{}` as one of the largest remaining generic skip classes.

The repo-local Zolem scan command is:

```bash
SHATTER_BIN=${HOME}/project/shatter/target/release/shatter make shatter
```

That target runs `scripts/shatter-full-scan.sh`, which writes `shatter-report/full-scan.md` and `shatter-report/full-scan.json`.

## Expected Outcome

Shatter adds bounded candidate generation for Go `interface{}` / `any` parameters when the surrounding code gives enough JSON-encoding narrowing context.

For this first increment, support encode-style JSON usage only:

- If an `interface{}` value is passed directly to `json.Marshal` or `json.NewEncoder(...).Encode`, try representative JSON-serializable values such as `map[string]any`, `[]any`, `string`, `float64`, `bool`, and `nil`.
- Keep the candidate set small and deterministic.
- Record the concrete candidate in the generated input/behavior evidence using Shatter's existing input display or behavior-map convention. Do not invent a new output schema unless the existing convention has no place for it.

## Proposed Scope

- Add planner/analyzer support for context-sensitive candidate sets for empty-interface Go values passed to JSON encoders.
- Add regression fixtures for `json.Marshal(v)` and `json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(v)` helper shapes using `interface{}` / `any`.
- Keep decode-style pointer destination support and non-empty interface implementor discovery as separate concerns.

## In Scope

- Empty-interface (`interface{}` / `any`) candidates for parameters.
- JSON encode usage as the first narrowing source.
- Bounded deterministic exploration and behavior-map reporting of the selected concrete candidate.

## Out of Scope

- Full dynamic-language-style search over arbitrary Go values.
- `json.Unmarshal`, `json.NewDecoder(...).Decode`, or `out any` pointer-destination synthesis.
- Empty-interface struct fields unless they are already handled by the same parameter path without extra design.
- Exhaustive solving for all interface implementations.
- Repo-specific Zolem generator configuration.
- Non-empty interface fixes for `response.Generator`, `Recorder`, `Selector`, or provider interfaces unless they fall out of existing Shatter implementor discovery.

## Acceptance Checks

- A Go regression fixture for `json.Marshal(v interface{})` is attempted with JSON-serializable concrete candidates instead of skipped as unsupported.
- A Go regression fixture for `json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(v interface{})` is attempted with JSON-serializable concrete candidates instead of skipped as unsupported.
- Candidate generation is bounded and deterministic; the test asserts the candidate family or concrete candidate through Shatter's existing generated-input or behavior evidence.
- Existing Go planner/analyzer tests pass.
- If Zolem is available, `SHATTER_BIN=${HOME}/project/shatter/target/release/shatter make shatter` shows fewer raw `interface{}` skips than the current 17 without a broad increase in failed functions.
acceptance_criteria: Go JSON encode interface fixtures are attempted with bounded candidates instead of skipped; planner tests pass; optional Zolem scan reduces raw interface skips.

title: Pickpackit corpus baseline
description:
# Pickpackit corpus baseline

## Problem

Shatter needs an evidence-based baseline against `~/project/pickpackit` before individual Shatter gaps can be fixed. The user goal is for Shatter to successfully execute and test at least 90% of Pickpackit feature code, producing well-formed behavior maps and other outputs with a broad spread of inputs that exercise many branches, without non-trivial Pickpackit feature-code changes.

Pickpackit currently has Shatter configuration at `~/project/pickpackit/shatter.config.json` selecting:

- `web/src/**/*.ts`
- `web/src/**/*.tsx`
- `api/src/**/*.rs`

The config excludes tests/specs, `node_modules`, `api/src/bin/**`, `api/src/**/*test*`, and `target/**`. Pickpackit `Makefile` already exposes `shatter-dry-run`, `shatter-scan-web`, `shatter-scan-api`, and `shatter-scan` targets.

## Desired Outcome

Run current Shatter against the whole configured Pickpackit feature corpus from isolated worktrees, record target counts and outcomes, and identify concrete Shatter defects or missing capabilities blocking the 90% success goal.

For this baseline, the success denominator is every discovered feature-code function from the configured Pickpackit Shatter corpus:

- included source files from `~/project/pickpackit/shatter.config.json`
