The Kapow report used a mild overall status like partial-success even when the completed denominator represented a tiny fraction of the selected source set. That status is too weak for a broad-run report with thousands of failed/runtime_failed/unsupported functions.

Add an explicit validity/reliability layer separate from process exit code and per-target status. Examples:
- high: source set stable, artifact references valid, meaningful denominator representation.
- degraded: many failed/unsupported spans but artifacts are internally valid.
- low: completed-function denominator is below a configured source representation threshold.
- stale-source-set: source files changed during/after run.
- invalid-artifacts: TSV/JSON references missing artifacts.

The report should surface specific reasons and recommended next actions. Exit code policy can remain configurable, but machine-readable output must not require human inference to know a report is unreliable.
acceptance_criteria: Broad-run reports include a report_validity/reliability field with values such as high, degraded, low, stale-source-set. The CLI markdown and JSON explain why validity is degraded. Kapow-style runs with tiny completed denominators or stale source snapshots are not labeled plain partial-success without warnings.
close_reason: Landed in merge 08cb6c77. Adds report_validity layer with reason codes.

title: Artifact references valid
description:
Kapow reruns showed source-code-status.tsv emits paths to JSON spec files for partial/failed rows even when those JSON files do not exist. Failed rows can also reference markdown files that are absent. This breaks downstream analysis and makes report consumers chase stale paths.

Required behavior:
- TSV/JSON path fields are a contract: non-empty means the file exists.
- Partial/failed rows with no spec JSON should leave spec empty/null and include a reason such as spec_not_produced_due_to_build_failed.
- Failed rows with only logs should not invent markdown paths.
- Report finalization validates all path references.
- Extra artifacts under shatter-artifacts/files that are not referenced by the current manifest/TSV are either removed or reported as stale extras.

This issue covers artifact integrity only; source-set churn detection is tracked separately by the run manifest issue.
acceptance_criteria: Every non-empty artifact path emitted in TSV/JSON/markdown exists at report finalization time. Missing artifacts are represented as empty/null path fields plus explicit unavailable_reason, never as dangling paths. A post-run invariant test fails if any TSV path is missing or any file artifact has no current TSV row.
close_reason: Artifact-reference contract on explore summary landed as 1d86b6c3. UnavailableReason enum added, validator enforces (Some path AND file exists) XOR (None AND unavailable_reason). Stale extras reported (not deleted). Note: TSV/markdown variants in original issue text live in external kapow wrapper; this implementation enforces the contract on summary.json + per-target JSON. When str-jeen.16 introduces a real TSV manifest, the same enum + validator extends to it.

title: Run manifest source snapshot
description:
Kapow validation exposed that long Shatter runs can become stale while still exiting 0. During the final rerun, concurrent work changed the source set: the TSV included deleted/renamed files (MobileStateSheet*, StateSelector*) and missed newly added files. The report looked current but was no longer a valid denominator for main.

Shatter needs a run-start source snapshot and an end-of-run validation step. The snapshot should include:
- repository root and current working directory
- git commit or dirty-tree marker
- scope/config hash
- selected source paths
- source file size/hash or mtime+size
- frontend availability and dependency/environment preflight state

If paths are added, removed, or modified during the run, the final report must say so and mark validity low/stale instead of normal partial-success. The TSV should include manifest metadata or a link to manifest JSON.
acceptance_criteria: Each run writes a manifest containing git commit, scope hash, source path list, and per-file content hashes or mtimes. At run end Shatter verifies the source set is unchanged; if changed, the report status is stale-source-set and lists added/removed/changed paths. Long-run tests simulate files added/removed during exploration.
close_reason: Run-manifest source snapshot + end-of-run validation landed on main as 4dac093f. New shatter-core/src/run_manifest.rs; scan_orchestrator emits manifest.json and reports stale_source_set with source_diff on drift.

title: Failed spans count uncovered
description:
Kapow showed most functions failed before completion, but those functions disappeared from the line denominator. This lets Shatter report coverage over the successful sliver and hides the real risk.

Failed and unsupported spans need to count against coverage. If Shatter discovers a function span, then build_failed/runtime_failed/unsupported/timed_out should be reflected as uncovered or separately failed-covered span lines, not omitted.

Suggested buckets:
- covered_completed_lines
- uncovered_completed_lines
- build_failed_function_lines
- runtime_failed_function_lines
- timed_out_function_lines
- unsupported_function_lines
- no_target_file_lines
- undiscovered_source_lines

The report should retain completed-function coverage but also show "represented source percentage" and "unrepresented due to failure/unsupported" so broad-run coverage cannot look healthier than it is.
acceptance_criteria: Any discovered function span that fails, times out, or is unsupported contributes to an uncovered/failed/unsupported denominator bucket. Coverage summaries include line-weighted counts for completed, failed, unsupported, timed out, no-target, and undiscovered source. Tests cover mixed completed/build_failed/runtime_failed/unsupported files.
notes: Decomposed into str-jeen.41 (span outcome buckets), str-jeen.43 (no-target line buckets), str-jeen.44 (source representation metrics), and str-jeen.42 (mixed outcome bucket regression). Follow-on blockers moved to children: str-jeen.5 now waits on str-jeen.44, str-jeen.6 now waits on str-jeen.41.
close_reason: Decomposed into str-jeen.41, str-jeen.43, str-jeen.44, and str-jeen.42.

title: Coverage denominator semantics
description:
Kapow broad-run validation showed a report like 467/1606 lines even though the selected source set had roughly 197k physical lines and roughly 100k obvious non-test/demo/story lines. The 1606 denominator is only completed-function span lines, because failed/runtime_failed/unsupported functions are omitted from the denominator.

This makes broad-run coverage structurally misleading: the report answers "coverage of functions Shatter fully completed" while users read it as "coverage of this repository".

Required design:
- selected_source_files and selected_source_lines: every file in the run source set.
- productionish_source_lines or classified_source_lines: selected lines after test/demo/generated/type/config classification, when available.
- discovered_function_span_lines: all function spans Shatter discovered, regardless of outcome.
- attempted_function_span_lines: all function spans Shatter attempted to execute.
- completed_function_span_lines: only functions whose exploration completed.
- covered_lines within each denominator where meaningful.

The report must make the completed-only metric visually subordinate to whole-source and attempted-span metrics.
acceptance_criteria: Reports expose at least four named denominators: selected source lines, discovered/attempted function span lines, completed-function span lines, and whole-source explored lines. The markdown/JSON explicitly labels completed-function coverage as a subset metric. A Kapow-style fixture with many failed targets must not report a tiny denominator as source coverage.
notes: Decomposed into str-jeen.17 (whole-source), str-jeen.18 (span outcome split), str-jeen.19 (markdown layout), str-jeen.20 (kapow regression). Closing in favor of children.

title: Epic: Honest broad-run coverage
description: Kapow broad-run validation showed Shatter can complete with exit 0 while reporting a wildly misleading coverage denominator. The retained runs selected roughly 100k obvious production-ish lines / ~197k physical source lines, but the report surfaced only completed-function span coverage such as 467/1606 lines. Most discovered functions failed, were unsupported, or were dropped from the coverage denominator, making the report look like coverage of a tiny successful subset rather than coverage of the codebase. This epic tracks the reporting, artifact-integrity, source-set, and frontend reliability work required before broad-run coverage can be trusted.
acceptance_criteria: Broad-run reports distinguish whole source-set coverage, attempted function span coverage, completed-function coverage, failed/unsupported span impact, and report validity. TSV/JSON/markdown artifact references are internally consistent. Source churn during long runs is detected. Kapow-style validation produces an actionable denominator and failure-impact breakdown rather than a misleading 1606-line denominator.
notes: Tracker modeling repair: this epic was appearing in bd ready because parent-child links do not block readiness and Beads only supports all-children gates at creation time. Keep the epic in_progress while unfinished children remain; work should be claimed from child issues.

title: Rust frontend availability
description: Kapow validation attempted two Rust targets and both failed with: shatter-rust frontend not found: install it on PATH or build with cargo build --manifest-path shatter-rust/Cargo.toml. Shatter should either make Rust frontend availability part of setup/discovery or report Rust as unsupported/unavailable before attempting targets.
acceptance_criteria: Rust target discovery checks frontend availability before execution. If available, representative Kapow Rust files execute through explore. If unavailable, the summary reports a clear frontend-unavailable status instead of generic failure, with setup/build instructions.
close_reason: Implemented frontend availability precheck. Shatter now checks Rust frontend availability during target discovery in run_explore and run_scan; fails fast with structured install hint when unavailable; mixed-language scans skip Rust files and continue with available languages. Added unit + integration tests. Landed on main as merge commit.

title: TS referenced tsconfig paths
description: Kapow validation produced many TypeScript runtime failures because Shatter selected web/tsconfig.json, which is references-only and lacks compilerOptions.paths, instead of resolving to a config such as tsconfig.app.json that contains baseUrl and @/* paths. Representative error: tsconfig-paths adapter requires compilerOptions.paths in /home/ketan/project/kapow/web/tsconfig.json.
acceptance_criteria: TypeScript exploration handles project-reference tsconfig layouts by selecting or resolving a config with compilerOptions.paths/baseUrl when needed. Representative Kapow web files no longer fail with tsconfig-paths adapter errors. Tests cover a references-only root tsconfig pointing at an app config with paths.

title: No-target coverage gaps
description: Kapow validation showed extremely low useful coverage: 1026 files attempted, 561 'No targets discovered', 430 files with runtime_failed entries, and only 31 files with coverage tables. Shatter needs to classify no-target and runtime-failed outcomes so the coverage denominator and actionable gaps are visible.
acceptance_criteria: Explore summaries classify no-target files by reason where possible, aggregate runtime_failed functions by root cause, and report executable coverage denominator separately from discovered/attempted files. Tests cover no-target and runtime_failed aggregation.
close_reason: Landed on main. ExploreSummary now splits failed→build_failed/runtime_failed/timed_out and skipped→unsupported/skipped_by_policy with produced_coverage and no_target_reason; run-wide footer surfaces breakdown when non-completed buckets are non-zero. Tests cover bucket aggregation, no-target classification, breakdown rendering (suppress-on-happy-path), and serde back-compat. Walkthrough/E2E/smoke green. Finer error-class clustering within runtime_failed left for follow-up.

title: Timeout per function
description: Kapow validation exposed that explore timeout is currently applied at file/job granularity, causing large files with many targets to hit a single 30s wall-clock limit. Timeout control should be fine-grained per discovered function/target so one slow function does not discard the rest of the file. Observed in /home/ketan/project/kapow with tools/kapow files timing out at file level.
acceptance_criteria: Explore can enforce timeout per function/target, not only per file. Slow functions are reported individually as timeout while other functions in the same file can complete. Reports make the timeout scope explicit.
close_reason: Landed on main. ObservationOutput.timed_out plumbed end-to-end through both parallel explorer paths (random explore_function + concolic orchestrator's TerminationReason::TimeoutExplore via From<ExploreResult>). New classify_outcome_status helper downgrades Ok+timed_out to status=failed with explicit 'function timed out after Xs (per-function budget)' reason that round-trips into the TimedOut bucket added in str-oo31. 5 new tests cover both paths plus the CLI helper. E2E + walkthrough green. Out-of-scope follow-ups will be filed: kapow-side wrapper script change, --timeout flag doc-rename.

title: Partial counted success
description: Kapow validation showed summary accounting that groups partial files together with successful files. The run reported 1009 succeeded targets, but source-code-status.tsv showed only 31 success and 978 partial. This makes coverage and run health look much better than they are.
acceptance_criteria: Summary output reports success and partial as separate top-level counts. Any derived succeeded/failed totals are named unambiguously and do not count partial as full success. Tests cover mixed success/partial/failed/timeout status aggregation.
close_reason: Bug actually lives in kapow's scripts/shatter-source-set.sh, not in shatter. Filed in kapow as kapow-8zkl. Shatter does not emit success/partial counts; if a shatter-side authoritative status report is desired, that's a separate epic.

title: Go mixed package harness
description: Kapow validation still has Go runtime failures after the frontend update. Representative logs show generated harness/package conflicts such as 'found packages shattertarget (admissions.go) and main (admissions_heuristic_test.go)' in tools/kapow and similar failures in api/cmd/server, plus some instrumentation rewrite syntax errors. This appears distinct from the previously tracked internal-package visibility problem: the frontend rewrites or stages the target as shattertarget while other package main files remain in the compile context.
acceptance_criteria: Go explore can run files in directories containing package main tests or sibling files without mixed-package failures. Generated harness/staging avoids pulling unrelated package main files into the shattertarget package context. Regression tests include a package with target source plus _test.go siblings.
close_reason: Fixed: stage rewritten _test.go siblings into overlay so Go's directory loader sees consistent package declarations after main → shattertarget rename. Added integration regression test reproducing the kapow 'found packages shattertarget (X.go) and main (Y_test.go)' error. Landed on main as merge of str-x0sv-go-mixed-package.

title: Launcher harness must be synthesized inside target Go module
description:
Discovered by str-hy9b.J3 fourth-pass kapow validation (docs/validation/2026-04-go-frontend-kapow-rerun.md).

The Go launcher generator emits a synthetic shatter-launcher-<hash> package OUTSIDE the target's module tree. Go's internal/ visibility rule then blocks the launcher's main.go from importing any kapow/.../internal/... package, so any function whose call chain touches an internal package fails launcher build with:

  main.go:7:2: use of internal package github.com/ketang/kapow/<...>/internal/<...> not allowed

This accounts for 336 of the 546 explore-stage errors in the kapow rerun (61.5%). It is the single largest category and gates the substantive v1→v2 comparison required by str-hy9b.J3. Internal/ visibility cannot be bypassed by build flags; the structural fix is to synthesize the launcher INSIDE the target module — typically as a tmp package under <module-root>/.shatter-launchers/<hash>/ — so the launcher and the target share the module-internal scope.

Acceptance: scan against ~/project/kapow with the same --exclude set as the J3 fourth-pass reproduction reduces internal-package errors from 336 to <50 (residual cases are valid cross-module access blocked by the language). At least one previously-failing function with an internal-package call chain (e.g. `AcceptInvite`, `AddBookmark`) successfully explores.

Tests: integration test that scans a synthetic Go module containing a function whose body imports a sibling internal/ package; assert the launcher builds and the function explores. Add a regression case that scans a function whose body does NOT touch internal/ to ensure the launcher inlining doesn't break the simple case.

Implementation notes:
- Cleanup story: launchers under <module-root>/.shatter-launchers/ must be torn down after scan; document a cleanup hook.
- The .gitignore-style filter in user repos may need updating; document.
- Conflict story: if the user already has .shatter-launchers/, refuse with a clear error or use a UUID-suffixed dir.

Blocks: str-hy9b.J3 substantive numbers (issue is closed, but a follow-up rerun is the validation gate).

title: Parallelism floor and ceiling clamp
description: Clamp scan/explore default --parallelism to [4, 16] regardless of nproc. Currently defaults to std::thread::available_parallelism(), which on 32-core boxes spawns 32 frontends. For Go scans this multiplies into ~32 concurrent 'go build' invocations and OOMs the host. Apply a global floor=4 (avoid sub-saturation on small machines) and ceiling=16 (avoid melt on large machines). Touches shatter-cli/src/commands/scan.rs:553-560 and shatter-cli/src/commands/explore.rs:1644 (any other call site of available_parallelism for parallelism defaults). User-overridable floor/ceiling are tracked separately.
close_reason: Implemented clamp to [4, 16] in resolve_parallelism (shatter-cli/src/helpers.rs); wired into scan and explore. End-to-end verified: --parallelism 32 -> 16 with warn, --parallelism 1 -> 4 with warn, in-range pass-through, auto-detect clamps to 16 on 32-CPU host. Commit 80e15ee5 on str-hy9b.J3-kapow-rerun.

title: extract_function_source panics when start_line > file length
description:
Discovered during str-hy9b.J3 kapow re-run validation (post str-s3s4/gq7c/dcqc).

shatter-core/src/fingerprint.rs:258-268 extract_function_source clamps end_line to lines.len() but does not clamp start_line. When a frontend reports start_line > file line count, the slice index lines[start..end] panics:

  thread 'main' panicked at shatter-core/src/fingerprint.rs:267:13:
  range start index 148 out of range for slice of length 43

Triggered by some Go file in kapow during scan (panic happens inside the scan after all generated files were soft-skipped). Likely cause: a stale analyzer cache, an off-by-N line-number bug in the Go analyzer, or files being re-read with different line counts than when analyzed.

Fix (immediate): clamp start to end. If start >= end, return empty string. extract_function_source returns Result<String, io::Error> — could also surface a richer error, but empty string matches "function not present at this location" semantics and avoids breaking callers.

Test: unit test calling extract_function_source with start_line past end-of-file; assert it returns Ok(empty) (or an Err) rather than panicking.

Independent of Go frontend: the panic is in shatter-core. Even if the frontend bug that generates bad line numbers is fixed, the core helper should not panic.
close_reason: Clamped start to end. Landed in commit 9b619710.

title: Build-tag-gated files trigger ParseError aborting batch analyze
description:
Discovered during str-hy9b.J3 kapow re-run (fourth regression after str-s3s4, str-gq7c, str-dcqc).

Files with //go:build constraints that don't match the analyzer's default tags (e.g. //go:build ui in kapow's api/ui/embed.go) are listed in pkg.GoFiles but not in pkg.Syntax, because go/packages excludes them when their tags are inactive. The shatter-go analyzer then errors with:

  frontend error (ParseError): target file "..." not found in loaded package syntax

This aborts batch_analyze instead of soft-skipping (str-s3s4 fix only handles NotSupported, not ParseError).

Fix preferences:
1. Detect build-tag-gated files in the analyzer (parse the build constraint, check against current tags) and return NotSupported with a clear short_reason. This piggybacks on the str-s3s4 soft-skip path.
2. Alternatively, broaden batch_analyze.rs's soft-skip handling to include ParseError when its message matches 'not found in loaded package syntax'. Less surgical but immediately effective.

Reproduction: shatter scan ~/project/kapow --language go [usual J3 excludes].

Distinct from str-jl9r (P3 feature: actually honor build tags during analysis). This bug is just about not aborting when one is encountered.
close_reason: Soft-skip build-tag-excluded Go files. Analyzer detects via go/build.Default.MatchFile, returns typed BuildTagExcludedError, handler maps to ErrNotSupported so batch_analyze soft-skips. Landed: acf3ec82.

title: HTTP adapters emit invalid TypeKind 'primitive' (str)
description:
Discovered during str-hy9b.J3 kapow re-run (third regression after str-s3s4 and str-gq7c).

shatter-go/protocol/{gin_adapter.go, nethttp_adapter.go, nethttp_recognizer.go} emit synthetic_params with TypeInfo{Kind: "primitive", Label: "string"} for adapter-recognized HTTP handler functions (method/path/body fields). The Rust core's TypeInfo enum has no "primitive" variant; valid variants are int, float, str, bool, array, object, union, nullable, complex, opaque, unknown.

Result: any Go file recognized by an HTTP/Gin adapter produces analyze JSON that the Rust core cannot deserialize, aborting batch_analyze. Triggered by api/internal/handler/log_config.go in kapow.

Fix: change Kind from "primitive" to "str" in all three files (the Label field is already "string", which is correct as a Go-side label hint). serde will tolerate the extra Label field on the Str variant unless deny_unknown_fields is set.

Tests: add round-trip tests in protocol_test.go covering an adapter-recognized HTTP handler analyze response, asserting it deserializes cleanly on the Rust side.
close_reason: Fixed: Kind:primitive → Kind:str in 3 adapter files; tests assert valid kinds. Landed in commit 712cdee2.

title: Go frontend buildUnOp emits binary-op names for unsupported unary tokens
description:
REGRESSION discovered during str-hy9b.J3 kapow validation. shatter-go/protocol/analyzer.go:1297-1314 buildUnOp handles only token.SUB (neg) and token.XOR (bitwise_not) explicitly, then falls through to tokenToOp() — which is the BINARY op map. For any other unary operator the function emits a binary-op name as the unary variant, producing a payload Rust core cannot deserialize.

Concrete failure: `&x` (address-of) produces a SymExpr{Kind:"un_op", Op:"bitwise_and"} because token.AND maps to bitwise_and in tokenToOp. Rust expects unary variants in {not, neg, bitwise_not, type_of, typeof}. Scan aborts with:
  Error: batch analyze failed: ... unknown variant `bitwise_and`, expected one of `not`, `neg`, `bitwise_not`, `type_of`, `typeof`

Affected operators (any UnaryExpr where Op is not SUB or XOR): &x address-of, *x deref, +x positive, <-x receive, !x logical-not.

Note: !x (token.NOT) currently goes through tokenToOp which returns "not" — accidentally correct. All others are broken.

Fix (preferred): in buildUnOp, switch on expr.Op explicitly. For SUB → neg, XOR → bitwise_not, NOT → not. For unsupported ops (AND/MUL/ADD/ARROW), return SymExpr{Kind:"unknown"} rather than fabricating a fake op name. Adding new unary variants to the protocol is a larger change and should be a separate decision.

Tests: add cases to symextract_test.go and analyzer_test.go for &x, *x, +x asserting kind=unknown (not un_op with a binary-op name). E2E: scan a small Go target containing &x and assert it does not abort.

Reproduces on every Go file in ~/project/kapow that uses & for address-of (which is most of them). This is why kapow validation cannot complete — the regression blocks any non-trivial scan.

title: Scan aborts on NotSupported from frontend (str-1giz regression)
description:
REGRESSION introduced by str-1giz. The Go frontend now returns NotSupported for generated files at the analyze handler. shatter-core/src/batch_analyze.rs:174-198 treats ANY ResponseResult::Error (including NotSupported) as fatal, aborting the whole batch.

Repro: shatter scan ~/project/kapow --language go aborts at first generated file (e.g. api/graph/generated/generated.go) with:
  Error: batch analyze failed: frontend error for <path>: frontend error (NotSupported): generated files are skipped by default

Discovered while running str-hy9b.J3 kapow validation. Pre-existing _test.go skip works only because file-listing filters _test.go BEFORE batch_analyze; generated-file detection happens INSIDE the frontend handler, so the file reaches batch_analyze and the NotSupported is fatal.

Fix options:
1. (preferred) batch_analyze.rs: treat NotSupported as a soft skip — log warning, omit from registry, continue. Symmetric with how the scan orchestrator already tolerates per-file failures elsewhere.
2. Move generated-file detection out of the handler into the file-discovery / scan-orchestrator layer (so files are filtered before frontends see them).

Add scan-level integration test that includes a generated file in the input set and asserts the scan completes successfully with the file omitted from results.

Tests/gates: cargo test --test e2e_concolic, smoke, gauntlet on kapow.
Owner-recommended: pair with the original str-1giz author or whoever owns batch_analyze.rs.

title: Internal-method spike fixture
description: Add examples/go/internal-method/ with module example.com/spike, internal/svc/svc.go declaring *Service, New(), and (*Service).DoIt(x int) int with one branch. Test asserts: analyzer discovers (*Service).DoIt as method; planner emits plan with ReceiverKind new_service; build succeeds (no internal visibility error); launcher runs and emits completed outcome. Spec: docs/specs/2026-04-17-go-frontend-redesign-v2.md §D7.
acceptance_criteria: Test passes via cargo test --test e2e_concolic.

title: Legal-anchor resolver
description: loader.LegalAnchor(targetPkg) returning the deepest internal/ segment's parent (or module root). Reject cross-module anchors. Generated launcher path: <anchor>/shatter_launcher_<targetIDHash>. Suffix-disambiguate on collision. Vendored deps deferred (H5). Spec: docs/specs/2026-04-17-go-frontend-redesign-v2.md §D1.
acceptance_criteria: Test matrix passes: acme/foo→acme; acme/foo/internal/bar→acme/foo; acme/internal/foo/bar→acme; acme/foo/internal/bar/internal/baz→acme/foo/internal/bar; acme→acme. Collision case produces unique anchor.

title: Overlay manifest generator
description: New shatter-go/overlay/ with Manifest{Replace map[string]string}, Builder.Add(inTreePath,realPath), Builder.AddGenerated(realFile,anchor,basename), Builder.Write() returning manifest path. Manifest under <workspace>/overlays/<planID>.json. Assert real files exist at write time. Spec: docs/specs/2026-04-17-go-frontend-redesign-v2.md §D2.
acceptance_criteria: Manifest with one wrapper + one launcher builds successfully under 'go build -overlay <manifest>' against trivial fixture.

title: Adopt go/packages loader
description: Add golang.org/x/tools/go/packages dep. New shatter-go/loader/ wrapping packages.Load with NeedTypes|NeedSyntax|NeedTypesInfo|NeedDeps|NeedFiles|NeedImports|NeedName|NeedModule. Cache results in workspace. Provide LoadPackage and LoadFile. Standalone-file mode materializes synthetic module. Spec: docs/specs/2026-04-17-go-frontend-redesign-v2.md §C1.
acceptance_criteria: Loader returns package with non-empty Types/Syntax/TypesInfo for repo packages and standalone files. Round-trip caching works.

title: Artifact workspace layout
description: New shatter-go/workspace/ module with Workspace type owning the layout (analysis/, plans/, generated/, overlays/, binaries/, cache/build, cache/loader, runs/). Resolver: env var → repo override → user-data default. metadata.json on init. Wire into Go frontend entry point. Spec: docs/specs/2026-04-17-go-frontend-redesign-v2.md §B1.
acceptance_criteria: Unit tests cover env-override and default-path resolution. metadata.json created on first use, read on subsequent runs.

title: Outcome protocol types
description: Add OutcomeStatus enum (completed, completed_with_findings, unsupported, build_failed, runtime_failed, timed_out, skipped_by_policy) and InvocationOutcome object to protocol/schema.json. Regenerate bindings into shatter-core, shatter-go/protocol, shatter-ts, shatter-rust. Update protocol/GOVERNANCE.md and parity-matrix.yaml. Spec: docs/specs/2026-04-17-go-frontend-redesign-v2.md §Phase A / A1.
acceptance_criteria: Round-trip serialization tests pass in all three frontends. Schema validation passes. npx task parity accepts new capability declaration.

title: Demo explore bounds
description: Update walkthrough and gauntlet broad explore steps to use explicit execution bounds instead of relying on unbounded natural convergence. Keep the demos representative while ensuring they complete reliably after recent explore scheduler changes.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- walkthrough and gauntlet broad explore steps have explicit bounds.
- the affected demo steps complete reliably on a normal local build.
- script output still demonstrates exploration behavior usefully.

title: BatchScheduler enqueue API
description:
Add a public BatchScheduler::enqueue(task_index, budget) method that pushes a new entry into the queue mid-flight, with rank 0 and zero batches completed. Foundational piece for str-b2my.10 (concurrent discovery + execution in shatter explore), which needs to grow the work-item set after the scheduler is constructed.

Caller is responsible for assigning a unique task_index that matches its parallel work-item Vec. The scheduler treats the new entry exactly like a freshly-constructed entry — eligible for the next next_batch() pick subject to FIFO tie-break against the existing queue.

No CLI or behavior change; pure API addition with unit tests.
acceptance_criteria:
- BatchScheduler::enqueue(task_index, budget) is public and documented
- Enqueueing mid-flight (between next_batch and record_outcome on other tasks) does not corrupt in_flight tracking
- Enqueued entries respect FIFO tie-break (rank 0 → ordered after existing rank-0 entries)
- Enqueueing after the queue has fully drained still works (re-activates the scheduler)
- is_complete() correctly accounts for newly enqueued entries
- Unit tests cover enqueue mid-flight, enqueue after drain, enqueue interleaved with record_outcome
close_reason: Merged into main as 18af8396. BatchScheduler::enqueue API added with 8 unit tests + 2 proptests covering mid-flight enqueue, post-drain reactivation, FIFO tie-break, and duplicate-index panics. Foundation for str-b2my.10.

title: Fix clippy 1.93 errors in shatter-cli
description:
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings fails with 6 errors in shatter-cli. str-kyhy previously fixed 1.93 clippy breakage on shatter-core but did not reach shatter-cli (likely because clippy short-circuited).

Errors:
- shatter-cli/src/commands/explore.rs:2863 — clippy::type_complexity on Vec<Vec<(u32, Vec<(u32, DiscoveryMethod)>)>>
- shatter-cli/src/commands/explore.rs:2876 — clippy::useless_vec (vec![0usize, 0usize] -> array)
- shatter-cli/src/commands/nondeterminism.rs:443 — clippy::cloned_ref_to_slice_refs (&[decl.clone()] -> slice::from_ref(&decl))
- shatter-cli/src/telemetry_flush.rs:312 — clippy::assertions_on_constants
- shatter-cli/src/telemetry_flush.rs:313 — clippy::assertions_on_constants
- shatter-cli/src/telemetry_flush.rs:318 — clippy::assertions_on_constants

Fix each at the root, no blanket allows. Verification: cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings must pass.

title: Fix rustfmt 1.93 drift across shatter-core and shatter-cli
description:
rustfmt 1.8.0 (bundled with rustc 1.93.1) reformats ~105 files in shatter-core and shatter-cli that have never been run through the new defaults. cargo fmt --all -- --check currently fails across most of the Rust tree. No rustfmt.toml exists, so the drift is from stock defaults.

Fix: run cargo fmt --all and commit the result. Pure mechanical change, no semantic edits.

Verification: cargo fmt --all -- --check must pass cleanly.

title: Explore writes fingerprinted maps
description: explore.rs run_explore calls cache.store(&bmap) at the two artifact-persistence sites (~lines 1966 and 2040) without setting bmap.fingerprint. After str-bo4z.1 added store_with_fingerprint and is_fresh drop-on-stale, these call sites produce legacy-None entries that get dropped on every subsequent run. The cache is functional but ineffective for explore CLI artifacts until both call sites migrate to store_with_fingerprint using the deep fingerprint already available via AssemblyOpts.deep_fingerprints (~line 2010).
acceptance_criteria: Both explore.rs cache.store call sites in run_explore use store_with_fingerprint; behavior maps persisted via the explore CLI survive across runs when function bodies are unchanged.

title: Persist fn scheduler state
description:
Persist per-function scheduler metadata separately from canonical behavior maps and stored inputs.

Acceptance:
- Scheduler metadata is stored per function.
- Scheduler metadata is advisory and reconstructible rather than canonical.
close_reason: Landed via merge d059f339. SchedulerStateCache persistence layer + scan_orchestrator wiring with proptest coverage.

title: Explore reuses retained seeds
description:
Feed retained significant inputs back into explore through the existing seed and candidate-input paths.

Acceptance:
- No separate replay subsystem is introduced.
- Changed functions prioritize rerunning retained significant values through existing local seed loading.

title: Body changes reset fn scheduler
description:
Reset function-specific scheduler memory when a function body changes.

Acceptance:
- Function-body changes clear cooldown, attempt history, and related scheduler hints for that function.
- Changed functions return to the queue as effectively unexplored.

title: Stored inputs survive changes
description:
Preserve compatible stored inputs across function-body changes so they can be reused as seeds.

Acceptance:
- Same-signature stored inputs remain reusable.
- Only obvious additive and subtractive arity cases are adapted; ambiguous changes are rejected.

title: Body changes drop stale maps
description:
Invalidate cached behavior maps when a function body changes.

Acceptance:
- Function-body changes invalidate only that function's behavior map.
- Unrelated functions keep their cached maps.
close_reason: Landed via merge f720944f (branch str-bo4z.1-invalidate-stale-maps). Tracker status was stale.

title: Explore resumes broad runs
description:
Automatically resume broad explore runs from durable artifacts and scheduler memory when available.

Acceptance:
- Interrupted broad runs continue without an explicit resume command.
- Missing scheduler metadata only degrades prioritization quality, not recoverability.
close_reason: Landed via merge 6ccd07ed. Auto-resume broad explore runs from durable artifacts and resume-state sidecars, with deep fingerprint staleness detection.

title: Explore leases one worker per fn
description:
Ensure one explored function is allocated to only one execution worker at a time.

Acceptance:
- No two workers execute batches for the same function concurrently.
- Targets discovered for an active function wait until that function is released and requeued.

title: Explore discovers while executing
description:
Run target discovery concurrently with execution so exploration can begin before full repo discovery completes.

**Depends on str-b2my.17** (BatchScheduler enqueue API) for the foundational scheduler change.

Acceptance:
- Execution can start before full discovery completes.
- Newly discovered functions can enter the queue while workers are active.

Implementation outline (post-str-b2my.17):
- Hoist work_items / accumulators / scheduler out of the per-target loop in shatter-cli/src/commands/explore.rs
- Spawn a tokio discovery task that walks parsed[1..], runs analyze + builds work items, sends them via mpsc channel
- Batch loop drains the channel each iteration and calls BatchScheduler::enqueue for new entries
- Loop terminates only when scheduler is_complete AND discovery channel is closed AND drain returned nothing
- E2E test: multi-target broad explore completes correctly with second target's functions present

title: Explore reranks after each batch
description:
Release function batches after each batch and rerank globally before picking more work.

Acceptance:
- Functions with uncovered work re-enter the global queue after each batch.
- The same function may be selected again if it still ranks highest.

title: Explore batches one function
description:
Execute broad-scope explore work as fixed-size batches against one function at a time.

Acceptance:
- Each batch explores one function only.
- Batch size is internal and not part of the main user-facing CLI.

title: Explore prints periodic summaries
description:
Print periodic human-readable progress summaries during explore runs.

Acceptance:
- Summaries print every 15 seconds by default for bounded and unbounded runs.
- --quiet suppresses periodic summaries.

title: Explore adds clear stop flags
description:
Add clear bounded-run controls for explore using concrete units instead of vague effort semantics.

Acceptance:
- Explore supports --time-limit, --coverage-threshold, and --max-executions.
- Help text explains each flag in clear user-facing units.

title: Explore defaults to unbounded
description:
Make unbounded execution the default behavior of shatter explore when no stop condition is provided.

Acceptance:
- Explore runs until interrupted when no limit flags are set.
- Bounded runs remain available through explicit stop flags.

title: Epic: Explore artifact durability
description: Make explore artifacts durable and reusable across runs by adding precise invalidation, stored-input survival, and bounded representative retention.

title: Epic: Continuous explore runs
description: Make shatter explore usable as a long-running, interruption-safe exploration loop with repo-wide scheduling, periodic progress reporting, resumable execution, and parallel workers.

title: Go standalone smoke deserialization
description:
# Frontend / Protocol Issue

## Protocol-Visible Behavior

- Does this change the wire format or semantics of any protocol message? (yes)
- If yes, which commands or fields are affected: Go frontend response payload consumed by the CLI/core pipeline during standalone smoke exploration
- Is this change backward-compatible? (no)

## Parity Impact

Which parallel code paths are affected? Check all that apply and confirm the parallel path is updated too.

- [ ] `buildSymExpr` (instrumentor.ts) — update `buildSymExprWithFlow` to handle the same node type
- [ ] `buildSymExprWithFlow` (instrumentor.ts) — update `buildSymExpr` to handle the same node type
- [ ] Random explorer (`shatter-core/src/explorer.rs`) — verify concolic orchestrator (`orchestrator.rs`) gets the same change
- [ ] Concolic orchestrator (`shatter-core/src/orchestrator.rs`) — verify random explorer (`explorer.rs`) gets the same change
- [ ] CLI wiring in `main.rs` — verify both `--concolic` and default explorer paths receive the new config or flag

## Default Behavior

- Is new functionality enabled by default? (yes)
- If no, what flag or config option enables it: N/A

## Required Test Updates

- [ ] Unit tests added or updated
- [ ] Property tests (proptest / fast-check / rapid) added for any new public function
- [ ] E2E concolic tests verified (`cargo test --test e2e_concolic`)
- [ ] Conformance tests verified (`npx task conformance`) — required if protocol-visible
- [ ] Walkthrough updated (`demo/walkthrough.sh`) if the compact demo narrative changes
- [ ] Gauntlet updated (`demo/gauntlet.sh`) or equivalent targeted coverage added for non-demo CLI changes

## Contract Updates

- Are runtime contracts (`#[requires]` / `#[ensures]`) needed? (yes)
- If yes, identify the trust boundary that justifies them (Z3 FFI / subprocess JSON / cross-collection index): subprocess JSON from Go frontend into Rust deserialization
- Confirm all three qualifying criteria are met: trust boundary, type gap, silent corruption (see `shatter-core/CLAUDE.md`)

## Notes

Observed during verification on April 8, 2026:
- `SHATTER_EXAMPLES_DIR=/home/ketan/project/shatter/examples npx task smoke`
- TypeScript smoke case passed
- Go library smoke case passed
- Go standalone smoke case failed for `examples/standalone/go/smoke-main.go:SmokeClassify`
- Failure artifact: `shatter-artifacts/explore-results/home_ketan_project_shatter_examples_standalone_go_smoke-main.go/00012_SmokeClassify.json`
- Error: `frontend error: failed to deserialize response: invalid value: integer -24576, expected u64`

This looks like a protocol/type mismatch on a negative integer crossing the Go frontend to Rust boundary. The bug is end-to-end user visible because the documented smoke pipeline fails even though the rest of the command launches successfully.
acceptance_criteria: - The Go standalone smoke case explores successfully through the smoke task.\n- Negative integer values from the Go frontend no longer fail protocol deserialization at the CLI/core boundary.\n- A regression test covers the standalone Go SmokeClassify path.
notes: Verified on 2026-04-09 from main commit 120ef02: SHATTER_EXAMPLES_DIR=/home/ketan/project/shatter/examples npx task smoke now passes all three smoke cases, including examples/standalone/go/smoke-main.go:SmokeClassify. The reported deserialization error did not reproduce on current main.

title: Examples checkout refresh crash
description:
The reusable examples checkout helper crashes before smoke or E2E setup can run when the default checkout already exists.

Observed during verification on April 8, 2026:
- `npx task smoke` fails immediately in `scripts/examples_checkout.py`
- Crash site: `refresh_checkout()` calls `checkout_dir.join(\".git\")`, but `pathlib.Path` has no `join` method
- Current traceback points at `scripts/examples_checkout.py:42`

User-visible impact:
- The documented smoke flow is broken by default when `/tmp/shatter-examples-main` already exists
- This also blocks the default examples resolution path for other commands that depend on the helper

Notes:
- A local `SHATTER_EXAMPLES_DIR` override can bypass the helper, but that is only a workaround and should not be required for the documented path.
acceptance_criteria: - scripts/examples_checkout.py refreshes an existing checkout without crashing.\n- npx task smoke can resolve examples without requiring SHATTER_EXAMPLES_DIR override.\n- A regression test covers the existing reusable checkout path.

title: Review plugin replacement plan
description: origin/main removed plugins/shatter-agent-review in commit d2fecec. The pushed branch str-wepv.4-run-interpret contains multi-target run/interpret work for the removed plugin, but it cannot land until the project decides whether to restore that plugin, move the workflow to a new location, or retire the downstream review plugin scope.
acceptance_criteria: The repo has a documented decision for the downstream review workflow: restore, relocate, or retire it; and any blocked child issues are updated accordingly.
close_reason: Decision: retire. Plugin moved to a separate project; Shatter retires the downstream review scope. str-wepv epic and all children closed.

title: Scan --since path join
description:
scan --since <ref> <subdir> can duplicate the subdirectory prefix when SCM returns repo-root-relative changed files and the scan root is a nested path.

Current repro on main:
cargo run -p shatter-cli -- scan --since HEAD~1 examples/standalone/ts

Current failure:
File not found: .../examples/standalone/ts/standalone/ts/22-opaque-predicate.ts

Expected behavior:
Changed files under the requested root are discovered exactly once and analyzed successfully.

Acceptance criteria:
- Add a regression test for nested-root --since.
- Verify the repro command above succeeds.
- Verify demo/gauntlet.sh step 32 no longer fails for this reason.

title: Plugin run and interpret
description: Implement the 'run shatter and interpret results' skill so it discovers all integrated targets in a repo, invokes their project-defined 'shatter' wrapper commands, reports failures immediately while continuing with remaining targets, and summarizes results per target.
acceptance_criteria: The run skill executes all integrated targets by default; skips non-integrated targets with a clear 'not integrated' status; reports per-target failures as soon as they happen; continues unless interrupted; and ends with a per-target and overall summary.
close_reason: Retired with str-wepv epic; plugin moved to a separate project.

title: Plugin target discovery
description: Implement repo analysis for the Shatter skills plugin so it can inspect documentation and build infrastructure, identify contained supported-language project roots, and determine likely integration surfaces for each target.
acceptance_criteria: The plugin identifies supported-language targets across a repo; detects likely native command surfaces such as package.json, justfile, and Makefile; excludes obviously vendored/generated targets; and asks for confirmation before proceeding when more than four targets are found.
close_reason: Retired with str-wepv epic; plugin moved to a separate project.

title: Plugin init integration
description: Implement the 'initialize shatter in a project' skill so it reads repo docs and workflow files, runs shatter init where needed, and adds one project-native wrapper command named 'shatter' per target when the edit is simple and unambiguous.
acceptance_criteria: The init skill preserves each target's native conventions; runs shatter init at the appropriate scope; adds exactly one broad-analysis wrapper command per integrated target; and only auto-edits straightforward, unambiguous integration surfaces.
close_reason: Retired with str-wepv epic; plugin moved to a separate project.

title: Plugin proposal fallback
description: Implement the init skill's proposal-only fallback so ambiguous integration cases are handled safely. When the plugin cannot determine a clear integration path, it should produce a concrete proposed change instead of editing files blindly.
acceptance_criteria: Ambiguous targets are not auto-edited; the skill explains why integration is unclear; proposed commands or patches are concrete enough for user review; and skipped targets are reported explicitly.

title: Shatter skills plugin
description: Create a skills plugin that can integrate Shatter into real projects by analyzing repo documentation and build infrastructure, preserving native workflow conventions, and running Shatter through project-defined wrapper commands. V1 scope is limited to initialization/integration and execution/result interpretation; removal and persistent integration ledgers are out of scope.
acceptance_criteria: Child issues define target discovery, integration, proposal fallback, and run/interpret behavior

title: TS bundle extract race
description: Full cargo test can fail in shatter-cli helper tests when multiple tests call the embedded TypeScript frontend extractor against a fresh cache root in parallel. The extractor writes to a fixed .tmp path and races on rename, producing 'No such file or directory' for frontend-<hash>.js.tmp -> frontend-<hash>.js. This reproduces on clean origin/main and is independent of invocation-model metadata.
acceptance_criteria: full cargo test no longer fails because of embedded frontend extraction races

description:

## Notes

Extend analysis metadata so frontends can report when a target requires an adapter-owned invocation model instead of a plain direct call. The model must remain generic and opaque to core, while still giving the CLI and persisted artifacts enough structure to explain why a target is treated as a hook, handler, or runtime-driven scenario.
acceptance_criteria:
- FunctionAnalysis can represent direct-call and adapter-owned invocation models.
- Synthetic parameters and scenario-facing metadata are preserved through protocol transport.
- Core rendering remains generic and does not introduce framework-specific enums.

title: TS adapter substrate
description: Add the TypeScript-side adapter registry, resolver/runtime/invocation hook points, transparent adapters, and fixture coverage needed to host framework-specific execution extensions.

title: Core adapter substrate
description:
Build the framework-agnostic substrate in shatter-core for opaque adapter descriptors, hint transport, invocation metadata, and selection policy.

This epic must not introduce framework-specific enums or hard-coded React, browser, Go, or Rust logic into core.

title: Execution adapter framework
description:
Umbrella epic for the long-term execution-adapter subsystem described in docs/execution-adapters.md.

This epic tracks the generic core substrate, language-frontend adapter registries, recognizers, execution-plan composition, and the first framework-specific proving grounds in TypeScript, Go, and Rust.

Scope rule: framework semantics stay in language frontends; shatter-core owns only opaque adapter descriptors, generic invocation metadata, hint surfacing, and policy evaluation.

title: Merge YAML genetic defaults
description: merge_configs() currently drops defaults.genetic when multiple .shatter/config.yaml layers are combined, even though scan already reads genetic settings from merged YAML defaults. Fix the layered merge so subtree-local genetic settings behave consistently under hierarchical discovery.
acceptance_criteria: Layered .shatter/config.yaml files preserve defaults.genetic; a nearer config can override the inherited genetic block; regression tests cover hierarchical merge behavior

title: Merge YAML mock defaults
description: merge_configs() currently drops defaults.mocks when multiple .shatter/config.yaml layers are combined. That makes subtree-local mock overrides unreliable even though the YAML schema already supports defaults.mocks. Fix the merge so far configs provide defaults and nearer configs override specific keys.
acceptance_criteria: Layered .shatter/config.yaml files preserve defaults.mocks; far configs contribute unspecified symbols; nearer configs override duplicate symbols; regression tests cover at least one far-plus-near merge case

title: YAML owns per-fn budgets
description: Move the persistent per-function scan budget knobs out of shatter.config.json and into hierarchical .shatter/config.yaml defaults. Scope is limited to max_iterations and timeout_per_fn, which already map cleanly to YAML defaults.max_iterations and defaults.timeout semantics. The change should preserve CLI flag precedence while making subtree-local overrides possible in monorepos.
acceptance_criteria: Scan uses hierarchical .shatter/config.yaml for max_iterations and per-function timeout defaults when CLI flags are absent; shatter.config.json no longer owns those two fields; docs and examples show the new source of truth

title: Merge YAML exploration defaults
description: merge_configs() currently drops defaults.exploration when multiple .shatter/config.yaml layers are combined. That breaks hierarchical control of adaptive strategy settings even though the schema and resolver support exploration config at YAML defaults and function scope. Fix the merge behavior and lock it down with tests.
acceptance_criteria: Layered .shatter/config.yaml files preserve defaults.exploration; a nearer config can override inherited exploration settings; regression tests cover hierarchical merge behavior

title: Scan shows live progress
description: Add consistent live progress output to shatter scan so users can see which functions are processing, skipped, or failed during long scans.
acceptance_criteria: Scan emits progress continuously during execution; progress includes completed, skipped, and failed functions; timing or completion counts are included; structured progress output is available for tooling.

title: Scan keeps completed reports
description: Persist per-function scan results as they complete so long scans retain useful output even when interrupted before the final aggregate report is written.
acceptance_criteria: Completed function scan outputs are written incrementally; skipped and failed functions are recorded incrementally; partial scan artifacts remain usable after interruption; users can inspect completed work without waiting for the final report.

title: Scan writes partial summary
description: Maintain one summary artifact for a running or interrupted scan that shows overall status and points to completed artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: A summary artifact exists during scan execution; it records run status such as running, completed, failed, or interrupted; it lists completed, failed, and skipped work with artifact references; a user can inspect one file to understand partial scan state.

title: Resume interrupted scan
description: Allow scan to continue from saved partial results instead of restarting from scratch after interruption.
acceptance_criteria: Interrupted scan can skip already completed functions; pending or failed functions can be retried; incompatible resume state is detected clearly; resumed scan still produces correct final aggregate output.

title: Explore shows live progress
description: Add user-visible progress output to shatter explore so long runs show liveness and current activity before final reports are written.
acceptance_criteria: Explore emits visible progress before completion; progress identifies the current target or function; failures are reported immediately; structured progress output is available for tool consumers.

title: Explore keeps completed results
description: Persist per-function explore results as each function completes so finished work survives interruption and can be inspected without rerunning the whole target set.
acceptance_criteria: Each completed function writes a durable artifact; failed functions write a durable failure artifact; interruption after partial completion leaves usable artifacts for finished functions; artifact naming is deterministic and collision-safe.

title: Explore builds from saved results
description: Assemble final explore reports and spec output from persisted per-function artifacts instead of relying only on in-memory accumulation.
acceptance_criteria: Completed runs still produce the expected final outputs; final assembly reads saved function artifacts; a run that finished artifacts but failed before final assembly can still be finalized afterward.

title: Z3 build avoids gh-release
description:
# Summary
Fresh Rust builds that touch `shatter-cli` can fail in `z3-sys` because `shatter-core` enables the `z3` crate's `gh-release` feature, forcing build-time GitHub release downloads even when system Z3 is already installed.

## Reproduction
- On a machine with system Z3 available via `pkg-config`, run `cargo run -p shatter-cli -- explore ...` or `npx task smoke` from a clean or newly rebuilt worktree.
- Observe `z3-sys` panic from `build.rs` with `Could not find release for z3-4.16.0`.
- Confirm `pkg-config --modversion z3` succeeds locally.

## Acceptance
- Fresh Rust builds of `shatter-cli` do not depend on live GitHub release fetches for Z3 when a local Z3 install is available.
- `cargo build -p shatter-cli` succeeds in the affected environment.
- The smoke path no longer fails in `z3-sys` due to the gh-release fetch path.

title: Go harness uses module deps
description:
## Summary

`shatter explore` fails during Go harness preparation when the target package imports other packages from the same module. The temporary harness module does not inherit the real project's `go.mod` / `go.sum`, so ordinary intra-module imports fail to resolve and the package becomes completely unexplorable.

## Protocol-Visible Behavior

- Does this change the wire format or semantics of any protocol message? no
- If yes, which commands or fields are affected:
- Is this change backward-compatible? N/A

## Parity Impact

- [ ] `buildSymExpr` (instrumentor.ts) — update `buildSymExprWithFlow` to handle the same node type
- [ ] `buildSymExprWithFlow` (instrumentor.ts) — update `buildSymExpr` to handle the same node type
- [ ] Random explorer (`shatter-core/src/explorer.rs`) — verify concolic orchestrator (`orchestrator.rs`) gets the same change
- [ ] Concolic orchestrator (`shatter-core/src/orchestrator.rs`) — verify random explorer (`explorer.rs`) gets the same change
- [ ] CLI wiring in `main.rs` — verify both `--concolic` and default explorer paths receive the new config or flag

## Default Behavior

- Is new functionality enabled by default? yes
- If no, what flag or config option enables it:

## Required Test Updates

- [ ] Unit tests added or updated
- [ ] Property tests (proptest / fast-check / rapid) added for any new public function
- [ ] E2E concolic tests verified (`cargo test --test e2e_concolic`)
- [ ] Conformance tests verified (`npx task conformance`) — required if protocol-visible

- [ ] Gauntlet updated (`demo/gauntlet.sh`) or equivalent targeted coverage added for non-demo CLI changes

## Contract Updates

- Are runtime contracts (`#[requires]` / `#[ensures]`) needed? no
- If yes, identify the trust boundary that justifies them (Z3 FFI / subprocess JSON / cross-collection index):
- Confirm all three qualifying criteria are met: trust boundary, type gap, silent corruption (see `shatter-core/CLAUDE.md`)

## Steps to Reproduce

Target package: a Go package inside a module that imports another package from the same module.

Example from `github.com/ketang/kapow/api`:

```bash
shatter explore api/internal/search/access.go --max-iterations 5
```

Observed failure:

```text
build failed: exit status 1
suggest.go:9:2: no required module provides package github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/db
```

`--analyze-only` succeeds; the failure is specific to the prepare / instrumentation / build phase.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Exploring a Go package with intra-module imports succeeds without requiring manual refactors or `go get` in a temporary harness.
- The fix preserves package-wide builds so functions in otherwise-valid files are not blocked by unrelated sibling imports.
- Regression coverage exercises a module-local import case end-to-end.

title: Scan fails on 0% explored targets
description:
# Summary
`shatter scan` exits 0 and writes a report even when every attempted function was skipped before execution, producing a false-positive successful scan.

## Problem
A target with attempted functions but 0 explored functions should not be counted as OK. Current behavior masks broken build or instrumentation environments and can mislead CI.

## Reproduction
- Scan a Go target with a stale `go.mod` so every function fails during instrumentation or build.
- Observe exit code 0 and a written report despite 0% exploration.

## Acceptance
- If `functions_attempted > 0` and `functions_explored == 0`, scan exits non-zero.
- The final status clearly indicates scan failure rather than success.
- Report-writing behavior is consistent with the failure status and covered by tests.
