title: Curate gauntlet scan FAIL allowlist
description: Triage current FAIL rows in standalone TS scan after str-jeen.57 made gauntlet fail on FAIL/error(s). Then add an allowlist mechanism so the gauntlet passes for known/expected FAILs but fails on new ones.
acceptance_criteria: Each current FAIL has a documented disposition; allowlist file format defined; gauntlet exits 0 only when no unexpected FAIL/error rows appear.

title: Gauntlet artifact leakage
description:
`demo/gauntlet.sh` claims it uses temporary directories so the run does not pollute repo-local state, but the May 8, 2026 run wrote large ignored artifacts under the checkout.

Evidence:
- The script comment says: `Use temporary directories so the gauntlet never pollutes repo-local state`.
- The gauntlet output repeatedly logged artifact writes under `/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-artifacts/...`, including `explore-results` and `scan-results`.
- After the run, `git status --ignored --short shatter-artifacts` showed `!! shatter-artifacts/`.
- `du -sh shatter-artifacts` reported `3.7G`.
- `shatter cache clear` at Step 60 reported `0 files, 0 B freed`, so the gauntlet cleanup step did not cover these artifacts.

The tracked worktree stayed clean, but repeated gauntlet runs can consume substantial disk and make local state misleading.
acceptance_criteria:
- Gauntlet-created explore/scan artifacts are written under a temporary run directory by default, or the gauntlet cleanup step removes only the artifacts it created.
- A completed gauntlet run leaves no new repo-local ignored `shatter-artifacts/` data unless the user explicitly opts into retaining it.
- The final gauntlet summary reports where retained artifacts live and distinguishes temporary HTML reports from repo-local diagnostic artifacts.

title: Gauntlet step count drift
description:
`demo/gauntlet.sh` reports an inaccurate step inventory. The run on May 8, 2026 printed `Step N/62`, but the actual transcript skipped Step 13, used Step 22 twice, and ended at Step 60 before the summary.

Evidence from `/tmp/shatter-gauntlet-20260508.log`:
- `Step 12/62 · Scan Rust Examples` is followed by `Step 14/62 · Run: Analyze Only`.
- `Step 22/62` appears twice: `Execution Timeout` and `Go Execution Timeout`.
- The last executable step is `Step 60/62 · Cache Clear`; no Step 61 or Step 62 appears.

This does not directly break CLI behavior, but it makes the gauntlet less auditable. Humans and agents cannot tell whether two steps were omitted, intentionally removed, or accidentally skipped.
acceptance_criteria:
- The gauntlet derives the total step count from the actual registered steps or has a test that keeps `TOTAL` and step numbers in sync.
- A full gauntlet run prints a contiguous sequence from 1 through the declared total with no duplicates or gaps.
- The final summary reports the actual completed step count and fails if any registered step was skipped unexpectedly.

title: Refute available to Kapow agents
description:
Agents working on Kapow validation need Refute integrated into the local workflow and documented as an available capability.

Refute sources:
- Local checkout: ~/project/refute
- GitHub: https://github.com/shatterproof-ai/refute

Scope this as an agent-facing integration, not a rewrite of Refute. The implementation should make it clear how an agent in a Kapow work session discovers the tool, invokes it, and interprets its output for Shatter/Kapow validation work.
acceptance_criteria:
- Agents working in ~/project/kapow can invoke Refute through a documented command or wrapper without manually rediscovering the repo path.
- The integration records any prerequisites, expected environment variables, and failure modes needed for agent use.
- A smoke check demonstrates Refute can run from a Kapow-oriented agent workflow and produces actionable output or a clearly documented unsupported-state message.

title: Rust execute handles all input types
description: rust-execute-partial: Rust executor returns not_supported for NonExecutable inputs requiring dynamic dispatch. Expand to handle all input types TS/Go handle.

title: Go frontend captures full side-effect set
description: Go executor currently captures only console_output and global_state_change. Implement thrown_error capture (panic/recover wrapping) and the remaining kinds (global_mutation, file_write, network_request, environment_read) where feasible. Tracked as divergence go-side-effects-partial in protocol/parity-matrix.yaml.

title: TS frontend implements teardown command
description: TypeScript currently does not advertise the teardown capability or implement a teardown handler. Implement teardown in shatter-ts/src/handlers.ts and add 'teardown' to SUPPORTED_CAPABILITIES. Tracked as divergence ts-missing-teardown in protocol/parity-matrix.yaml.

title: Go frontend command_capabilities drift: get_invocation_plan missing from registry.yaml
description:
The Go frontend declares `get_invocation_plan` in its handshake response but `protocol/registry.yaml > frontends.go.command_capabilities` omits it. `task parity` (specifically `protocol/conformance/run_golden_tests.py`) reports:

```
FAIL: handshake/go: capability mismatch vs registry.yaml
  registry:   [analyze, ..., generate, instrument, prepare, setup, teardown]
  frontend:   [analyze, ..., generate, get_invocation_plan, instrument, prepare, setup, teardown]
```

Reproduces on clean `origin/main` at `feb498a1` (str-jeen.54). Not introduced by str-1hlk.5 — that branch did not modify `frontends:` and reproduces the same failure on main without its changes applied.

## Likely cause
Either the Go frontend started declaring `get_invocation_plan` without a corresponding registry update, or the parity tightening in str-1hlk.10 surfaced a pre-existing gap. Fix is one-line: add `get_invocation_plan` to `frontends.go.command_capabilities` in `protocol/registry.yaml` (and re-record the golden file if needed).

## Acceptance
- `task parity` exits 0 on a clean main checkout.
- `task conformance` and `task golden-test` still pass.

## Related
- str-1hlk.10 (parity tightening, recently landed)
- str-1hlk.5 (IDL field model — discovered this gap while running gates)

## Side note
`task check-fast` also fails on main with `shatter-go/protocol` test package timing out at 600s (`gocommand.runCmdContext` blocked in `Cmd.Wait`). This is the str-jeen.52 flake family — separate issue, not folded into this one.

title: Dry-run output config clash
description:
Zolem setup check currently fails against the rebuilt Shatter binary because `scan --dry-run` rejects JSON output requested by project configuration or CLI defaults.

Observed failure from `./scripts/test-shatter-setup.sh` in Zolem:
`scan --dry-run does not support --format json (and writes no JSON to -o files). Re-run without --dry-run for the JSON scan report, or drop --format json / the .json -o file to keep the dry-run plan.`

This may also need a small Zolem script/config follow-up once Shatter's intended dry-run contract is settled, but Shatter should make dry-run setup checks robust and easy to use.
acceptance_criteria:
- `scan --dry-run` can run in a project whose normal scan config includes JSON report output, either by ignoring report outputs during dry-run or by providing an explicit documented override.
- CLI diagnostics identify the exact flag/config field that must change when dry-run output remains invalid.
- Add a CLI/config regression fixture using a Go target project with JSON scan output configured; assert dry-run succeeds or fails with the new explicit remediation path.
- Zolem `./scripts/test-shatter-setup.sh` passes against current Shatter, or the Shatter issue documents the precise Zolem script change needed as a separate follow-up.

title: Error-only completion status
description:
Zolem broad scan reported 112 completed functions, but 60 of those completed functions had only throwing discovered inputs. The markdown function table showed only 6 PASS rows, 26 WARN rows, and 80 FAIL rows.

This makes broad-run success and coverage hard to interpret: functions with only wrapper/runtime invocation errors are mixed with functions where Shatter actually exercised target behavior. The report should separate behavioral exploration from error-only invocation completion.
acceptance_criteria:
- JSON and markdown report separate counts for functions with at least one non-throwing input versus functions whose discovered inputs all throw.
- Coverage and completion summaries distinguish completed-with-behavior from completed-with-invocation-errors.
- Add a target-language regression fixture, preferably Go, with one function that explores successfully and one function that can only produce invocation errors; assert the report exposes the two outcomes separately.
- Function tables or machine-readable output allow consumers to filter scan failures, invocation failures, and successful behavioral exploration separately.
- Zolem rerun makes the 52 non-throwing completed functions and 60 error-only completed functions visible without custom JSON post-processing.

title: Status TSV projection
description:
Generate a stable TSV projection from the authoritative run-status.json for broad-run spreadsheet and shell analysis.

Scope:
- Produce a documented TSV derived from run-status.json, not independently aggregated wrapper state.
- Define stable column names for run identity, file identity, file status, per-file counters, target identity/outcome where applicable, artifact path fields, validity, and failure taxonomy.
- Add parity tests that prove TSV rows are a projection of the JSON source and do not invent markdown-scraped success/partial/failed state.

Out of scope: adding new status semantics beyond those already present in run-status.json.
acceptance_criteria:
A status TSV artifact is emitted from run-status.json with documented stable columns.
Tests assert TSV values match the JSON source for representative file and target outcomes.
Downstream broad-run analysis can consume JSON or TSV without parsing markdown prose.
The TSV writer uses one canonical ordered column definition for both header emission and row serialization. Tests assert the exact header order, that every row has the same column count as the header, and that values round-trip against run-status.json by column name. Any reorder, rename, or removal requires a TSV schema version bump; additive columns are appended.

title: Status rollup metrics
description:
Populate the authoritative status export with the existing broad-run rollup metrics that currently force consumers to understand multiple report surfaces.

Scope:
- Include denominator metrics, source-set classification totals, report validity/reliability and reason codes, frontend availability/preflight results, and line-weighted failure impact.
- Reuse the existing denominator, validity, source-bucket, preflight, and failure-impact structures where possible.
- Include coverage budget gate results once str-jeen.8 lands, without duplicating the gate implementation in this issue.

Out of scope: defining new threshold CLI/config behavior; that remains str-jeen.8.
acceptance_criteria:
run-status.json includes rollups for source denominators, source buckets, validity/reason codes, frontend preflight, and line-weighted failure impact.
The rollups agree with the existing JSON/markdown report data in regression tests.
The schema has a reserved/optional gate_decisions field that can be populated by str-jeen.8.

title: Status per-target rows
description:
Add target-level status rows to the authoritative status export so downstream analysis can inspect exact target outcomes and artifact contracts.

Scope:
- For each discovered or attempted target, include stable target identity, source file, source span, frontend/language, outcome, failure/unavailable reason, and validity impact.
- Include artifact paths using the existing existence contract: non-empty paths must exist, unavailable artifacts carry structured unavailable_reason.
- Keep target outcome semantics aligned with existing scan/explore report status values.

Out of scope: file-level rollup totals and TSV projection.
acceptance_criteria:
run-status.json includes a targets[] section with structured rows for discovered/attempted targets.
Every non-empty artifact path in target rows is validated or paired with an unavailable_reason.
Tests cover completed, failed, unsupported, skipped/unavailable, and missing-artifact prevention cases.

title: Status per-file rows
description:
Add file-level status rows to the authoritative status export so automation can understand run health without scraping markdown or external wrapper outputs.

Scope:
- For each selected source file, include path, language/frontend, source bucket, selected line count, discovered target count, attempted target count, completed target count, failed/unsupported counts, and file-level status.
- Use existing source-set classification and denominator accounting rather than introducing new classification semantics.
- Preserve a clear distinction between no-target, unsupported, skipped/unavailable frontend, failed, partial, and completed file states.

Out of scope: target-level artifact paths and TSV projection.
acceptance_criteria:
run-status.json includes a files[] section with one row per selected source file.
Rows expose source bucket, line count, target counts, and file-level status.
Tests cover completed, failed/partial, unsupported/no-target, and unavailable-frontend file rows.

title: Status export skeleton
description:
Emit the first authoritative machine-readable run status artifact without trying to populate every downstream analysis field in the first slice.

Scope:
- Write a versioned run-status.json artifact for scan/explore broad-run output.
- Include run identity, command/config identity, generated_at timestamp, manifest path/hash, source snapshot identity, and links to existing summary/report artifacts.
- Document the initial schema version and compatibility expectation for future fields.

Out of scope: per-file rows, per-target rows, TSV projection, and gate decisions.
acceptance_criteria:
run-status.json is emitted with a stable schema_version and run identity fields.
The artifact links to manifest/source snapshot and existing report artifacts.
Tests assert the skeleton fields exist for a minimal successful run.

title: Replace 'npx task' in non-doc files (scripts, configs)
description:
Companion to str-60p0 (which handled .md files only). Remaining 'npx task' occurrences in non-doc files:

- protocol/parity-matrix.yaml:4 — comment '# CI task: npx task parity'
- scripts/perf_isolation_report.py:231 — user-facing string 'Run `npx task perf-isolation` first to collect data.'
- scripts/setup-hooks.sh:86 — actual invocation: npx task "${PUSH_TASK}" 2>&1 || exit 1
- shatter-go/.golangci.yml:4 — comment '# Runs via: npx task go:test'

setup-hooks.sh is the only behavioral change — if 'npx task' does not work (go-task is not an npm script), that script is already failing for users; replace with 'task'.

The other three are comment/string updates only.
close_reason: Landed on origin/main via swarm wave 1

title: Drift entries expire
description:
Require every allowed_divergences entry to carry owner, tracking issue, status, and resolution condition.

Protocol-Visible Behavior
- Does this change the wire format or semantics of any protocol message? no
- If yes, which commands or fields are affected: N/A
- Is this change backward-compatible? yes

Parity Impact
- Makes parity drift explicit, owned, and eventually removable.

Default Behavior
- Is new functionality enabled by default? yes

Required Test Updates
- Validator tests for divergence metadata.
- npx task parity.
acceptance_criteria:
Validator fails on divergence entries missing metadata.
Resolved drift entries are removed or fail after a grace period.
protocol/PARITY.md and parity-matrix.yaml no longer diverge silently.

title: Semantic protocol cases
description:
Add behavior-level conformance cases beyond structural shape.

Protocol-Visible Behavior
- Does this change the wire format or semantics of any protocol message? no
- If yes, which commands or fields are affected: tests only for current expected behavior
- Is this change backward-compatible? yes

Parity Impact
- Cross-frontend observable behavior for errors, planner responses, setup/teardown, and prepare lifecycle.

Default Behavior
- Is new functionality enabled by default? yes

Required Test Updates
- protocol/conformance/conformance_cases.yaml.
- npx task conformance.
acceptance_criteria:
Cases cover invalid prepare_id, unsupported command behavior, version mismatch, missing-file precedence, and planner response with runtime_value.
Known intentional differences are documented in known_drifts.
npx task conformance passes with all required frontends.

title: Conformance requires frontends
description:
Make CI fail when a declared frontend is missing from conformance. Local runs may still allow an explicit skip mode.

Protocol-Visible Behavior
- Does this change the wire format or semantics of any protocol message? no
- If yes, which commands or fields are affected: N/A
- Is this change backward-compatible? yes

Parity Impact
- Ensures conformance actually covers TypeScript, Go, Rust, and noop in CI.

Default Behavior
- Is new functionality enabled by default? yes in CI

Required Test Updates
- Harness tests or CI task coverage for missing frontend behavior.
- npx task conformance.
acceptance_criteria:
CI conformance requires TypeScript, Go, Rust, and noop.
Missing shatter-go/bin/shatter-go or equivalent build artifact fails in CI.
Local skip behavior is explicit, not silent.
close_reason: Landed on origin/main via swarm wave 1

title: Rust frontend codegen
description:
Generate or generated-check shatter-rust/src/protocol.rs shared wire enums/structs from the authoritative contract.

Protocol-Visible Behavior
- Does this change the wire format or semantics of any protocol message? no
- If yes, which commands or fields are affected: generated binding should preserve current wire shape
- Is this change backward-compatible? yes

Parity Impact
- Rust frontend protocol binding and round-trip tests.

Default Behavior
- Is new functionality enabled by default? yes

Required Test Updates
- Rust frontend protocol round-trip tests.
- cargo test for shatter-rust.
- npx task parity and npx task conformance.
acceptance_criteria:
Rust frontend protocol round-trip tests pass.
Command/status/error-code drift from core is caught by codegen check.
Rust frontend advertised capabilities still come from implementation, not blindly from the contract.

title: Go protocol codegen
description:
Generate Go protocol enums and shared request/response field declarations where practical, while preserving Go-specific handler implementation code.

Protocol-Visible Behavior
- Does this change the wire format or semantics of any protocol message? no
- If yes, which commands or fields are affected: generated binding should preserve current wire shape
- Is this change backward-compatible? yes

Parity Impact
- Go frontend protocol constants, planner enum values, and request/response structs or generated checks.

Default Behavior
- Is new functionality enabled by default? yes

Required Test Updates
- Go protocol/property tests.
- go test ./... in shatter-go.
- npx task parity and npx task conformance.
acceptance_criteria:
Error codes, command/status constants, and planner enum values are generated or checked from generated fixtures.
Go protocol/property tests pass.
runtime_value and future enum additions cannot exist only in Go.

title: TS protocol codegen
description:
Generate TypeScript protocol types from the authoritative contract and reduce shatter-ts/src/protocol.ts to generated exports plus any TS-only helpers.

Protocol-Visible Behavior
- Does this change the wire format or semantics of any protocol message? no
- If yes, which commands or fields are affected: generated binding should preserve current wire shape
- Is this change backward-compatible? yes

Parity Impact
- TypeScript frontend protocol binding and round-trip tests.

Default Behavior
- Is new functionality enabled by default? yes

Required Test Updates
- TS protocol tests.
- fast-check protocol round-trip coverage if existing surface changes.
- npx task parity and npx task conformance.
acceptance_criteria:
Command/status/error-code unions are generated.
TS protocol tests pass.
Manual TS protocol enum drift is no longer possible.

title: Go wrapper imports for Zolem
description: Zolem full Shatter scans fail during Go wrapper compilation because generated wrappers reference qualified parameter types such as context.Context, io.Writer, and package-local imported types without importing their packages. The same scan also shows stale zero-argument constructor calls for constructors whose signatures now require parameters, causing every attempted Zolem function to fail before execution.
acceptance_criteria: Zolem's make shatter no longer fails from generated Go wrapper compile errors for missing imports or wrong constructor arity.
notes: Implemented on branch str-iylc-go-wrapper-imports-zolem: wrapper discovery hashes now include per-target import metadata so stale generated wrappers are invalidated, and wrapper target import collection no longer records result-only selector packages that the generated source never names. Verified go test ./wrapper ./planner ./protocol, cargo build --release, and Zolem make shatter with the rebuilt binary.

title: Scan ID qualified-key review
description: Review compute_scan_id after qualified_id is exposed on the wire. Decide whether scan_id derivation should include the same stable qualified key so checkpoint and resume behavior remains robust when duplicate names or receiver-qualified methods are present.
acceptance_criteria: The scan_id derivation decision is documented in code or tests. If behavior changes, checkpoint/resume regression tests cover duplicate named functions. If behavior stays path-based, tests or notes explain why the existing key remains stable enough.

title: Explore CLI report conflates branch_count with unique_paths
description:
shatter-core/src/report.rs:404-405 sets branch_count and branches_covered both from exploration.unique_paths, conflating path enumeration with branch coverage. Affects the standalone explore CLI's final report (frontend-agnostic). The scan path is unaffected — it goes through scan_orchestrator which populates branch_count from analysis.branches.len(). Discovered while fixing str-qo1.11.

Acceptance: explore CLI final report uses analyzer-derived branch counts, not unique_paths. branch_count and branches_covered are distinct, accurate values for all frontends.

title: Overlay smoke test fails with buildvcs probe
description: shatter-go/overlay/TestBuilder_GoBuildOverlaySmokeTest invokes `go build -overlay` directly (not via BuildLauncher) and fails with `error obtaining VCS status: exit status 128` when an ancestor of the test tempdir contains a broken .git. Same root cause as str-qo1.15 but on a separate code path the str-qo1.15 fix did not cover (test uses raw exec, not BuildLauncher). Fix: pass -buildvcs=false in the test's build args. Trivial.

title: Scan qualified function IDs follow-up: receiver-disambiguated IDs and wire exposure
description:
Follow-up to str-fuhw (Scan qualified function IDs). The initial fix in str-fuhw-qualified-ids switches scan-internal maps (file_map, analysis_map, scan_set, selected_set, by_name) to qualified IDs of form "<file>::<name>" and adds a regression fixture for two files each defining Write. Deferred items captured here:

1. **Same-file different-receiver Go methods.** "<file>::<name>" does not disambiguate two methods named Write on different receivers in the same file. Needs either receiver-decorated names emitted by shatter-go, or a richer qualified-ID format like "<file>::<receiver>::<name>" (or "<file>::<name>@<start_line>"). Crosses into shatter-go frontend scope, which str-fuhw was constrained not to touch.

2. **Wire-format qualified_id exposure.** Add a qualified_id field to ScanReport / FunctionReport JSON so downstream consumers (CI rerun wrappers, dashboards) can reliably distinguish duplicate-named functions across files. Overlaps with str-tzbr (CLI JSON stdout contract) and should land after that contract is finalized.

3. **compute_scan_id robustness review.** Currently path-based (safe for the str-fuhw bug). Re-evaluate whether qualified IDs should also feed into scan_id derivation once the wire change in (2) lands, to keep checkpoint resume keys stable across refactors.

4. **Rename FunctionResult.function_name semantics.** Today it stores a bare name for backward-compatible display. Once (2) is on the wire, consider promoting function_name to qualified_id and adding a separate display_name. This is a wire schema change and must be coordinated with report.rs, batch_state.rs, html_templates.rs, progress events, and any external consumers.

Acceptance for this follow-up: items 1 and 2 land at minimum; 3 and 4 may be split out further.
close_reason: All child follow-ups str-fuhw.1.1 through str-fuhw.1.4 are closed and landed on main; latest landing 66b495090f7244d4bac793231d19020e5abc05b3

title: Go harness disables buildvcs
description:
With a fresh Go workspace, focused Zolem exploration failed before meaningful harness compilation with:

```
error obtaining VCS status: exit status 128
    Use -buildvcs=false to disable VCS stamping.
```

Repro shape:

```
SHATTER_GO_WORKSPACE_ROOT=/tmp/shatter-zolem-fresh-workspace \
  shatter explore --project-dir /home/ketan/project/zolem --no-cache \
  internal/response/faker.go:tokenizeWords
```

Go's default `-buildvcs=auto` tries to stamp binaries with repository metadata when the main package/module/current directory appear to be in a VCS checkout. Shatter's generated overlay/launcher builds are disposable test harnesses, often built from generated workspace paths against real module paths, so VCS stamping adds no value and can fail before exploration.
acceptance_criteria: Go frontend harness and launcher builds disable VCS stamping or otherwise avoid VCS probes for disposable generated binaries. A regression builds an overlay-based generated launcher from a real module checkout without requiring git status to succeed. Zolem focused explore in a fresh SHATTER_GO_WORKSPACE_ROOT no longer fails with `error obtaining VCS status`.
close_reason: Fixed in 9255a983; merged to main. Added -buildvcs=false to BuildLauncher and setup loader go build invocations. Two regression tests in shatter-go/launcher/launcher_buildvcs_test.go cover both white-box (fake-go shim) and black-box (broken ancestor .git) reproduction.

title: Stale flags untracked funcs
description: A spec generated for only Refute `detectServerKey` was checked with `shatter stale selector.go spec.json`. The command returned exit 1 and marked the other functions in the file (`ForFile`, `prefersTSMorph`, `prefersOpenRewrite`, `detectLanguageID`) as stale even though they were never present in the targeted spec. Missing/untracked functions should not be conflated with stale changed functions.
acceptance_criteria: `shatter stale` distinguishes fresh, stale, removed, and untracked/not-in-spec functions. When a spec contains one targeted function from a multi-function file, unchanged sibling functions are reported as untracked or omitted, not stale, and exit-code policy reflects only actual stale/removed tracked functions unless a strict full-file mode is requested.
close_reason: Fixed in 492f9a36 / merge 76669d27..main. Added IncrementalPlan.untracked bucket; shatter stale exits 0 unless tracked drift. --strict opts in to legacy full-file behavior. Regression tests in spec.rs cover the bead repro.

title: Concurrent frontend spawn busy
description: Launching multiple Go-frontend Shatter commands against Refute concurrently caused `failed to spawn Go frontend: failed to spawn frontend: Text file busy (os error 26)`. Agent workflows often run independent probes in parallel, so the frontend build/spawn path needs locking, immutable binaries, or clear serialization.
acceptance_criteria: Two independent Shatter Go commands can start concurrently without `Text file busy`, or the CLI serializes/locks the shared frontend build artifact and reports a clear wait. Regression test or integration script launches two small Go probes concurrently and asserts both complete or one waits cleanly without spawn failure.
close_reason: Fixed in 5b79bb3a: serialize Go frontend extraction with unique tmp paths

title: Quiet mode hides reports
description: Focused Refute exploration with `--stdout --format text --quiet` exited 0 but printed no final exploration report, while artifacts on disk contained completed results. `--quiet` is documented as reducing log verbosity, not suppressing the command result. This makes quiet mode unusable for scripts that want clean final output.
acceptance_criteria: Quiet mode suppresses info/progress logging but still emits the requested command result to stdout or the requested output path. Regression tests cover focused explore and scan with `--quiet --stdout --format text/json` and assert the final report is present.
close_reason: Fixed in a7f7d10a; merged. Removed log_enabled!(Info) gates from 5 report-emission sites in explore.rs; added matching gate to emit_explore_progress' eprintln. 4 regression tests in shatter-cli/tests/quiet_report_emission.rs cover explore+scan × text+json under --quiet --stdout (json explore remains rejected per str-tzbr contract).

title: Scan artifact side effects
description: A bounded Refute scan was invoked with explicit `/tmp` report outputs plus `--no-cache --no-seeds`, but Shatter still wrote scan artifacts under `/home/ketan/project/refute/shatter-artifacts/...` and used the Go analysis cache, logging cache misses on the dry run and cache hits on the following real run. This makes it hard to run Shatter as a clean external audit tool and makes `--dry-run`/`--no-cache` semantics surprising.
acceptance_criteria: Document and enforce side-effect policy for scan/explore. With explicit external output paths plus cache/seeds disabled, scan does not write project-local artifacts unless an artifact directory is explicitly requested. `--no-cache` suppresses Go analysis cache reads/writes or the help text clearly distinguishes behavior-map cache from frontend analysis cache. Regression test runs scan against a fixture and asserts no project-local artifact/cache directories are created.
close_reason: Fixed in 57ad1acb: clean external-audit mode for scan suppresses project-local artifacts when -o + --no-cache + --no-seeds

title: preflight_failed wire status
description: Add preflight_failed to protocol error codes and OutcomeStatus enum so env-preflight failures bucket distinctly from not_supported. Follow-on from str-jeen.26 which used not_supported as a stopgap. Touches protocol/registry.yaml, OutcomeStatus, all three frontend bindings, parity matrix.
acceptance_criteria: preflight_failed exists as a first-class error code/outcome status. str-jeen.26 stopgap converted to use it. Parity matrix updated.
close_reason: Landed in merge 671ee933. preflight_failed first-class on wire (registry + OutcomeStatus + ErrorCode); TS emits, Go/Rust declare-only with explicit divergence row in parity-matrix.yaml. str-jeen.26 stopgap converted. Latent file_not_found vs preflight ordering bug in TS analyze fixed as part of the conversion. Per-frontend CLAUDE.mds updated per parity contract.

title: Markdown source-set summary
description: Markdown run report includes a source-set bucket table. Feeds the productionish_source_lines denominator referenced by str-jeen.1a.
acceptance_criteria: Markdown shows file/line counts per bucket. productionish_source_lines is exposed in JSON and referenced in markdown.
close_reason: Source Set Summary table + productionish_source_lines exposed in JSON; SCAN_REPORT_SCHEMA_VERSION bumped 2→3 with #[serde(default)] back-compat. Landed in ec18fa44.

title: Classifier cross-language fixtures
description: Test coverage for the classifier across Go, TS/TSX, Rust, config, generated, and test paths.
acceptance_criteria: Fixtures for each language plus config/generated/test classes. Tests assert correct bucket assignment.
close_reason: Cross-language classifier fixtures landed (10 tests, Go/TS/TSX/Rust/generated/test/declaration/policy-excluded/config buckets). Note: config-as-ProductionIsh contract pinned by these tests will be revised by str-jeen.47 (Unsupported file-type bucket). Merged in d58a4ed6919e2477f0e54793c125ba49786b8d61.

title: Source-set classifier core
description: Per-file source-set classifier runs before target discovery. Adds source_bucket field to JSON: production_ish, test_spec, generated, declaration_only, fixture_sample, policy_excluded.
acceptance_criteria: Each selected file has source_bucket in JSON. Path-based classifier covers the six buckets. Schema documented.

title: Go bug audit + cross-link
description: Tracker pass: review closed Go frontend bugs against current Kapow run; reopen or cross-link any whose failure modes still occur.
acceptance_criteria: Each closed Go bug has been re-checked. Reopened or linked entries documented in str-jeen.12f notes.
notes: Audit complete. Reviewed closed Go frontend bugs against fourth-pass kapow run (docs/validation/2026-04-go-frontend-kapow-rerun.md). Confirmed-fixed: str-gq7c, str-dcqc, str-jdz8 (plus str-a4c/6a7/iqnk/ws3k/x426/xkb/yqih no longer surfacing). Still-occurring failure modes covered by open str-b7zh: cross-linked str-3f2o, str-jeen.33, str-jeen.36 (in addition to str-jeen.32 already linked); pre-existing str-x0sv↔str-jeen.35 retained. No reopens. Full audit appended to str-jeen.12 notes.

title: Authoritative status export
description:
Some earlier confusion came from wrapper scripts aggregating Shatter outputs differently from the core tool, and from report consumers scraping markdown/TSV paths. Shatter should provide one authoritative status export for broad-run analysis.

The export should include:
- run manifest identity and source snapshot
- per-file status and per-target statuses
- artifact paths with existence contract
- denominator metrics and source-set classification
- failure taxonomy and line-weighted impact
- report validity/reliability
- threshold gate decisions
- frontend availability/preflight results

Markdown can be derived from this structure, but automation should not need to parse human report prose to understand run health.
acceptance_criteria: Shatter emits an authoritative machine-readable run status JSON/TSV with source-set manifest, per-file/per-target status, artifact paths, denominator metrics, validity status, failure taxonomy, and threshold gate results. Downstream scripts no longer need to infer success/partial/failed by scraping markdown.
notes:
Decomposed on 2026-05-06 into focused children:
- str-jeen.16.1 Status export skeleton
- str-jeen.16.2 Status per-file rows
- str-jeen.16.3 Status per-target rows
- str-jeen.16.4 Status rollup metrics
- str-jeen.16.5 Status TSV projection

Ordering: skeleton blocks rows/rollups/TSV; TSV waits for file rows, target rows, and rollup metrics. This issue is now an epic/rollup for those children. Coverage gate implementation remains str-jeen.8; status rollup should expose gate decisions after that lands rather than reimplementing gates.
close_reason: all child issues closed; final status TSV landing c12e45b343bb440a0a61d8dc434c146e86a1bcb9 on main

title: Source-set line classifier
description:
The Kapow analysis had to use ad hoc path regexes to estimate obvious non-test/demo/story lines (~100k) versus total physical selected lines (~197k). Shatter should own this classification instead of making users compute it after the fact.

Add a source-set classifier that operates before target discovery and records why each file/line bucket is in scope. The classifier does not decide coverage by itself; it gives context for denominators and no-target reasons.

Suggested buckets:
- production-ish executable source
- test/spec/story/demo/e2e source
- generated/schema/scalar source
- declaration/type-only/config constants source
- fixture/sample/docs source
- policy-excluded or scope-excluded source

Reports should make it easy to say: "Shatter selected N files / X physical lines; Y lines are production-ish; completed exploration represents Z lines."
acceptance_criteria: Reports classify selected source lines/files into production-ish, test/spec/story/demo, generated/schema, declaration/type-only/config, fixture/sample, and excluded-policy buckets. The classifier is exposed in JSON and summarized in markdown. Tests cover representative Go, TS/TSX, Rust, config, generated, and test paths.
notes: Decomposed into str-jeen.37 (classifier core), .38 (cross-language fixtures), .39 (markdown summary).

title: Broad-run validation corpus
description:
Kapow is a valuable real-world stress test, but it is large, mutable, and subject to concurrent source churn. Shatter needs a stable broad-run validation corpus that captures the failure classes discovered in Kapow while remaining suitable for regression testing.

Fixture/corpus should include representative modules for:
- large source set where completed denominator is tiny unless failed spans count
- source files added/removed during a simulated run
- dangling artifact path prevention
- Go internal packages, generated harness import references, mixed package _test.go siblings, and rewrite syntax edge cases
- TS private helpers not exported, TSX/type-only syntax, project aliases, missing dependency preflight, browser globals
- unavailable Rust frontend in a mixed-language run
- no-target files by reason category

This can be a synthetic repo under tests/fixtures or a scripted mini-repo generated during tests. The goal is to make broad-run reliability testable without depending on a moving external Kapow checkout.
acceptance_criteria: A repeatable broad-run validation corpus or fixture suite exists that exercises Kapow-discovered failure classes without requiring a live mutable Kapow checkout. CI or a documented local gate compares denominator, artifact integrity, stale-source detection, and failure-class counts against expected thresholds.
notes: Implemented under branch str-jeen.14 (3 commits: 17445c98 fixtures+manifest, a14370ef gate+tests, 03e66c7b Taskfile+docs). 13/13 fixtures pass under cargo-debug shatter; gate verified to exit 1 on threshold regression; 27 unit/property tests pass; test-standard clean. Awaiting team-lead landing.
close_reason: Landed in merge 0c929389. New corpus at tests/broad-run-corpus/ + scripts/broad_run_validation_gate.py + Taskfile entries broad-run-validation / broad-run-validation-tests. Documented local gate, not wired into mandatory CI per task scope. Threshold ratchet table in docs/validation/broad-run-corpus.md surfaces which open str-jeen P1 beads should tighten which thresholds when they land.

title: Coverage budget gates
description:
Broad-run users need a way to fail CI or local validation when Shatter produces a technically completed but low-validity report.

Add optional gates such as:
- --min-source-representation-percent
- --max-failed-span-percent
- --max-unsupported-span-percent
- --fail-on-stale-source-set
- --fail-on-missing-artifacts
- --fail-on-low-report-validity

Defaults should preserve current behavior unless the project config opts in. The report should show which gates were evaluated and why they passed/failed. This builds on the denominator, span accounting, artifact-contract, and validity-status issues.
acceptance_criteria: CLI/config supports thresholds for minimum source representation, maximum failed/unsupported span rate, stale source-set failure, and missing artifact failure. Tests cover pass/fail behavior and JSON output includes threshold decisions.

title: Concurrency semantics spec
description:
Define the concrete semantics for concurrent single-function exploration before implementation.

Decide and document:
- Whether `--seed` under parallelism requires exact candidate/output order or bounded reproducibility only.
- Function-level setup, execution-level setup, and teardown behavior across observer subprocesses.
- Candidate queue capacity, duplicate suppression, drain/shutdown behavior, and timeout behavior.
- Result ordering in `ObservationOutput` and progress counters under out-of-order completion.
- Whether the first implementation covers random exploration only, concolic exploration, or both.

This is a decision/spec issue, not implementation.
acceptance_criteria: Determinism, setup/teardown, queue shutdown, timeout, result ordering, and initial mode scope are documented. The spec identifies required regression fixtures for later child issues. str-frc child issues are adjusted if the chosen semantics change their scope.
close_reason: Concurrency semantics spec landed on main as 15507a55. See docs/specs/concurrent-single-function-exploration.md.

title: Bound GOMAXPROCS in go-frontend
description: Each spawned go-frontend invokes 'go build' on wrapper compilation; that toolchain run uses GOMAXPROCS=nproc by default, so N frontends * nproc threads exhausts CPU/memory on large hosts. Set GOMAXPROCS=2 (or min(2, nproc/parallelism)) in the env passed to embedded_go_frontend.rs spawn. Caps the inner toolchain without changing Shatter-level concurrency. Cleanest tactical fix for the kapow scan blowup.
close_reason: Implemented in shatter-cli/src/helpers.rs::frontend_config(): GOMAXPROCS=2 injected for Language::Go via new GO_FRONTEND_GOMAXPROCS const. Two unit tests added (frontend_config_go_caps_gomaxprocs, frontend_config_non_go_omits_gomaxprocs). Landed via merge 8ffe25be on main.

title: batch_analyze ParseError on build-tag-excluded files aborts scan
description:
Discovered during str-hy9b.J3 kapow re-run validation (post str-s3s4/gq7c/dcqc fixes).

Go frontend returns ParseError for files whose //go:build tag excludes them from the default build configuration. Example from kapow:

  Error: batch analyze failed: frontend error for /home/ketan/project/kapow/api/ui/embed.go:
    frontend error (ParseError): target file "/home/ketan/project/kapow/api/ui/embed.go" not found in loaded package syntax

api/ui/embed.go has `//go:build ui` so under default tags it is not part of the loaded package syntax. The analyzer hits the file via discovery, fails to find it in the loaded syntax, and emits ParseError.

shatter-core/src/batch_analyze.rs only soft-skips ErrorCode::NotSupported (added by str-s3s4). All other error codes — including ParseError — still abort the entire scan.

Options:
1. Go frontend: return NotSupported (not ParseError) when a file is excluded by build tags. Semantically accurate: this configuration does not support this file. Pairs with eventual str-jl9r (build-tag-aware analysis) which would still benefit from the soft-skip behavior for tags shatter doesn't enable.
2. Rust core: extend the soft-skip in batch_analyze to also tolerate ParseError (or better, a new BuildTagExcluded code). Riskier — ParseError covers real syntax errors that we DO want surfaced.

Recommend option 1 plus a focused test: scan a Go directory containing a //go:build tag excluded file and assert the scan completes with the file logged as skipped.

Triggered by 3 files in kapow (api/tools.go, api/ui/embed.go, api/ui/stub.go).
Workaround for J3: --exclude the offending files.
close_reason: Duplicate of str-8amu (same bug: Go ParseError on //go:build-excluded files aborts batch_analyze). str-8amu is higher priority (P1 vs P2) and the canonical issue going forward.

title: Go frontend reports function line numbers past end of file
description:
Discovered during str-hy9b.J3 kapow re-run.

A function in a kapow source file was reported by the Go analyzer with start_line=148, end_line=>=148, in a file that only had 43 lines. This caused a panic in shatter-core/src/fingerprint.rs::extract_function_source: 'range start index 148 out of range for slice of length 43'.

Defensive fix landed in shatter-core (clamp both indices to lines.len()), but the Go frontend should not be reporting line numbers past EOF in the first place. Likely root cause: the package-aware analyzer reporting positions from a sibling file in the package while the protocol layer attributes them to the requested target file.

Reproduce: run a kapow scan; the panic surfaces somewhere in the analyze stage. With the defensive fix the scan continues, but downstream consumers (fingerprint registry, source code excerpt UI) will silently get empty function bodies for those targets.

Fix: in shatter-go/protocol/analyzer.go, ensure StartLine/EndLine on FunctionAnalysis only reflect positions within the function's *own* file. Cross-check fset position file against the requested target file before recording line numbers.
close_reason: Skip declarations whose fset position file != requested target file in analyzer's file.Decls loop. Defensive cross-check via declarationBelongsToTargetFile (abs-path equality + single-file synthetic-module base-name fallback). Tests cover multi-file fixtures + direct helper assertion. Landed: 0727affa.

title: Wire generic instantiation planner into classify/receiver
description:
Follow-up to str-hy9b.F5. The planner module shatter-go/planner/generics.go was landed standalone. Wiring requires:

1. protocol/types.go — add TypeParams []TypeParamInfo{Name, Constraint} to DiscoveredTarget
2. protocol/discovered_target.go — populate TypeParams in BuildDiscoveredTarget by walking fn.Type.TypeParams.List; render constraint exprs (handle qualified idents like cmp.Ordered, interface literals, type-set unions)
3. planner/classify.go — replace blanket HasTypeParams → UnsupportedReasonGenericUnconstrained with per-typeparam dispatch via PlanGenericInstantiations
4. planner/receiver.go — same
5. planner/plan.go — emit one InvocationPlan per (instantiation × value-plan) cross product
6. Wrapper generator: emit Identity[string](v) instead of Identity(v) for generic calls. Needs new field on InvocationPlan (e.g. GenericTypeArgs []string) and threading through the launcher.

Item 6 is the real risk and likely wants its own design pass. Suggest decomposing further if it grows.

AC: real generic functions in the test corpus get plans (not just unsatisfied) and the resulting wrappers compile and run.

title: Define InvocationPlan protocol types (Rust + Go)
description: No InvocationPlan request/response struct exists in shatter-core/src/protocol.rs or shatter-go/protocol/types.go. Without wire types, Rust core cannot request a plan from the Go frontend even though Go declares invocation_plan: supported (protocol/parity-matrix.yaml:578-587). Define the request (e.g. get_invocation_plan) and response schema per str-hy9b.E1, regenerate bindings, add round-trip tests in both frontends. Filed from str-hy9b.H1 audit. Blocks the other three str-hy9b.H1 follow-ups (CLI flag, explorer wiring, orchestrator wiring).
notes: Reopened on 2026-04-23 during closure/beads reconciliation: verified this protocol work is not landed on the primary branch main. The issue was closed from local/expedition branch state (str-zbyp and str-hy9b), not from a verified main-branch landing.
close_reason: InvocationPlan protocol types defined under str-hy9b.E1 and wired in str-hy9b.H2/H5; --planner CLI flag added; both explorer and orchestrator paths wired. Completed on expedition base branch.

title: Wire Go planner into concolic orchestrator path
description: shatter-core/src/orchestrator.rs has no planner references. The concolic loop uses solver::solve_for_new_path (Z3) and drilling for input generation; ExploreConfig has no planner field. The concolic orchestrator must consume the Go planner artifact when the invocation_plan capability is declared, on parity with the random explorer. Filed from str-hy9b.H1 audit. Shatter has a documented parallel-paths rule (CLAUDE.md): features added to explorer.rs are routinely missing from orchestrator.rs, so both wirings must land together. Depends on str-hy9b.E1 and the protocol InvocationPlan wire format.
close_reason: Concolic orchestrator wired to consume InvocationPlan via str-hy9b-29 (str-yi9y). default_execute_plan propagates through ObserveStageOptions into orchestrator ExploreConfig. Completed on expedition base branch.

title: Wire Go planner into random explorer path
description: shatter-core/src/explorer.rs has no planner references. Input generation is hard-wired via ValueSource, generate_inputs_with_custom, and mock parameters. ExploreConfig has no planner field. If the Go frontend declares invocation_plan: supported (protocol/parity-matrix.yaml:578-587), the random explorer path must be able to request and consume that plan when dispatching invocations to the Go frontend. Filed from str-hy9b.H1 audit. Depends on str-hy9b.E1 (planner skeleton) and a protocol wire format for InvocationPlan.
close_reason: Random explorer wired to consume InvocationPlan via str-hy9b-29 (str-yi9y). default_execute_plan propagates through ObserveStageOptions into explorer ExploreConfig. Completed on expedition base branch.

title: Add --planner CLI flag wired into Explore command
description: No --planner CLI flag exists in shatter-cli/src/args.rs Explore command or shatter-cli/src/main.rs. Go declares invocation_plan: supported in protocol/parity-matrix.yaml:578-587, but there is no way for the user to request planner-driven invocation at the CLI. Add a --planner flag to the Explore command and thread it through to both explorer and orchestrator paths. Filed from str-hy9b.H1 audit.
close_reason: --planner CLI flag added to Explore command in str-hy9b-29, threaded through to both explorer and orchestrator paths. Completed on expedition base branch.

Cache analyzer output at <workspace>/analysis/<hash>.json keyed by SHA-256 of (target package GoFiles bytes, one-level import GoFiles bytes excluding stdlib, runtime.Version(), ProtocolVersion, functionName). Lite-load via packages.Load(NeedName|NeedFiles|NeedImports|NeedModule) skips parse+typecheck on hit. Acceptance test in analysis_cache_handler_test.go asserts hit on second call and miss on single-line source change via captured slog records. Property tests in analysis_cache_property_test.go cover determinism, byte-sensitivity, order-independence. Landed via merge 5d791b27. title: Explore report omits failed functions
description:
Observed while running `make shatter-explore` in the Kapow repo on 2026-04-16.

Kapow invokes `shatter explore <file> --spec -o <file>.md --spec-out <file>.json` once per eligible source file. In that run, 108 attempted files produced zero-byte markdown reports even though Shatter analyzed functions, wrote per-function explore artifacts, and recorded failures in `shatter-artifacts/explore-results/.../summary.json`.

Examples:
- `api/internal/auth/context.go`: empty markdown, but `summary.json` reports `total_functions: 5`, `completed: 0`, `failed: 1`, and the log shows `use of internal package ... not allowed`.
- `web/src/lib/auth/index.ts`: empty markdown, but `summary.json` reports two failed functions and the log shows `Function "..." not found in instrumented module exports`.
- `api/internal/annotation/service.go`: markdown is non-empty only because two functions completed; five failed functions are still omitted from the markdown even though they appear in the per-file summary.

The current implementation writes markdown from `acc.md_fragments.join(...)` in `shatter-cli/src/commands/explore.rs`. Fragments are only appended for functions that make it through result assembly, so discovered functions that fail before assembly disappear from the human-readable report entirely.

This violates the documented contract in `SPEC.md` that the markdown report is a human-readable summary of all explored functions, and it makes downstream wrappers like Kapow overcount empty artifacts as useful output.
acceptance_criteria:
- Per-file markdown report enumerates every analyzed function for the target file.
- Functions that fail, time out, or are skipped before result assembly still appear in markdown with status and a concise human-readable error message.
- Failure messages preserve the useful diagnostic cause without dumping full raw logs into the report.
- Successful functions continue to render their normal behavior/coverage sections.
- A zero-byte markdown file is only possible when analyze finds zero functions.
- Regression tests cover at least one file with all functions failing and one mixed-success file.

title: Loop templates derive from snapshots
description:
Build bounded symbolic-unroll templates from observed loop snapshots. Add a focused `symbolic_unroll.rs` that extracts supported per-iteration state updates from 2+ concrete iterations and generalizes them into a bounded symbolic formula over iteration count `k`.

Keep scope narrow:
- identifier locals only
- linear integer updates
- canonical counted loops plus one simple accumulator pattern
- deterministic rejection for unsupported shapes
acceptance_criteria: Template extraction succeeds for at least one counted-loop case and one accumulator-style case; unsupported loops are rejected deterministically without panics or partial formulas; named constants bound unroll depth and any template-size caps; core tests show extracted templates preserve observed iteration behavior on supported examples

title: Pre-execution constraint evaluator to skip predictable paths
description:
The orchestrator currently executes every candidate input in the worklist and only discovers after execution whether the path was already known. A ConstraintEvaluator can predict this before paying the IPC round-trip cost by substituting concrete values into known SymExpr constraint trees.

Architecture:
- ConstraintEvaluator takes a SymExpr and HashMap<String, ConcreteValue>, returns Option<bool> — Some(true/false) if fully evaluable, None if it hits an Unknown subexpression.
- Lives in the orchestrator's execution dispatch, not inside any specific strategy. Before any execute call, regardless of InputSource (Z3-solved, random, drilled, boundary, fuzzed), run the candidate through the evaluator.
- If evaluation matches a known path with full confidence (all constraints are Expr and evaluate to match a path in covered_paths), skip the execution.
- Conservative default: if any constraint is Unknown or non-deterministic, always execute.

Applies to all input sources: Z3-solved inputs (solution may satisfy a different known path), random/boundary inputs (many retrace known paths), drilled inputs, and future fuzz inputs.

Cost model: one shallow SymExpr tree walk per constraint per candidate. Even evaluating every candidate against every known path is far cheaper than one IPC round-trip.

Edge cases:
- Non-deterministic frontends (time-dependent branches, random state): prediction could be wrong. Execute if any constraint is flagged non-deterministic or Unknown.
- Skipped inputs count toward worklist drain but not toward execution counts or plateau counters.
- Evaluator bugs that incorrectly skip inputs are worse than wasted executions — conservative (execute on uncertainty) is correct.

Implementation: new module constraint_eval.rs in shatter-core. Integration point: orchestrator.rs execution dispatch, before the execute protocol call.
acceptance_criteria:
1. ConstraintEvaluator correctly evaluates SymExpr trees with concrete value substitution
2. Orchestrator skips execution for candidates that match known paths with full constraint confidence
3. Candidates with Unknown or partial constraints are always executed (no false negatives)
4. Skipped inputs do not count toward plateau counters or execution metrics
5. All existing InputSource types are filtered (Z3, Random, Drilled, Boundary)
6. Proptest coverage for evaluator correctness (arbitrary SymExpr + concrete values)

title: Gate ProgressEvent JSON by log level
description: Pre-existing ProgressEvent JSON lines (emit_explore_progress in shatter-cli/src/commands/explore.rs) still print at default INFO verbosity. Noted during str-cii2 walkthrough review. Gate behind --json / log level so humans do not see the raw JSON in normal runs.
acceptance_criteria: ProgressEvent JSON lines only emitted when user has opted into machine-consumable output (flag or log level).

title: Explore coverage/progress stats
description: Show what explore is doing while it runs, even when a function is still being re-enqueued and not finding new paths. Report cumulative per-function exploration totals, per-path/code-path effort, branch coverage, and MC/DC condition coverage when applicable. Make long-running broad explore runs understandable to humans and agents.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- Explore output reports cumulative exploration stats per function while the run is in progress or at function completion.
- Output includes branch coverage summary for each function.
- When MC/DC is enabled, output includes condition coverage / MC/DC summary.
- Output makes clear when a function continues running without new discoveries.

title: adapter_selection: disabled adapter re-enabled via dependency resolution
description:
Proptest regression surfaced during str-b2my.10 work: when an adapter is explicitly Disabled and another adapter requires it as a dependency, the required adapter is incorrectly re-added to active. Reproduction seed (stripped from proptest-regressions/adapter_selection.txt to keep str-b2my.10 scoped):

cc e5ef5036b1ea38792bec5f91b119e084aa65bc6c3f6317e09a6b4e4cf9457b9a

Shrunk input: profile = [ts/browser-dom Disabled, ts/react-hooks None], hints = [ts/react-hooks requires ts/browser-dom, ts/browser-dom Auto]. Expected: ts/browser-dom stays out of active. Actual: ts/browser-dom is active because ts/react-hooks's requirement re-adds it.

Panic site: shatter-core/src/adapter_selection.rs:701

title: Drop --emit-tests export
description: Remove or formally deprecate scan-time test generation. Shatter's primary regression artifact should be behavior maps, reports, and diffable discovery outputs, not generated framework-native tests. The current feature adds framework-specific complexity, creates a second source of truth, and projects an evolving behavior corpus into brittle snapshot code. Replace the product direction with artifact-first regression workflows and keep test generation only if explicitly retained as a low-investment export path.
acceptance_criteria:
A clear product decision is recorded: remove the flag entirely or deprecate it with a migration path.
Scan/report regression artifacts are identified as the primary replacement workflow.
Follow-on implementation work is split separately if needed (CLI removal, docs cleanup, export-module deletion, or deprecation warnings).

title: Orchestrator resume-from-state for explore batches
description: Currently orchestrator::explore() starts fresh each batch, rediscovering previously-covered paths until reaching novel territory. This produces O(batches) rediscovery per function in the str-b2my.6 round-robin batching loop. A resume-from-state API — persisting the worklist, covered_paths set, and input seeds across batches — would eliminate the tax and let EXPLORE_BATCH_ITERATIONS drop below 500 without hurting effective throughput.
acceptance_criteria: Batches 2..N of the same function in a broad-scope explore run make new progress from the start without rediscovering paths already covered in earlier batches.
close_reason: Landed via merge 8d51ad44. ExploreState carries covered_paths and discovery_inputs across batches, eliminating O(batches) rediscovery.

title: str-bo4z.1 follow-up: migrate explore.rs cache.store calls to store_with_fingerprint
description:
Follow-up from str-bo4z.1 (cache invalidation on body change).

shatter-cli/src/commands/explore.rs has two call sites that persist behavior maps without setting a fingerprint:
- explore.rs:1966 — cache.store(&bmap)
- explore.rs:2040 — cache.store(&bmap)

After str-bo4z.1, BehaviorMapCache::is_fresh drops any on-disk entry whose stored fingerprint is None or mismatches. Until these call sites migrate to the new cache.store_with_fingerprint(&bmap, fingerprint), explore-written entries will be dropped on every subsequent run (safe — just forces re-explore, no stale data served).

Fix: plumb the current deep fingerprint (already in AssemblyOpts at explore.rs:2010) through to both cache.store call sites and switch to store_with_fingerprint.

Coordinate with str-b2my.6 which also touches explore.rs.

Scope: shatter-cli/src/commands/explore.rs only.

Acceptance:
- Both call sites use cache.store_with_fingerprint
- E2E concolic tests still pass
- Manual verification: running explore twice in a row on an unchanged function serves from cache on the second run (is_fresh returns true)
close_reason: Duplicate of str-bo4z.11 (P1, parented under epic str-bo4z). Both cover the same explore.rs migration; keeping the better-linked one.

title: Behavior quotas split by mode
description:
Keep behavior-class quota accounting separate by coverage mode while leaving the stored-input corpus shared.

Acceptance:
- Branch-mode saturation does not block MC/DC-specific representatives.
- The shared stored-input corpus remains reusable across modes.
close_reason: Landed via merge 62ea758b. CoverageMode enum splits per-behavior quotas so branch saturation doesn't block MC/DC representatives.

title: Stored inputs keep distinct reps
description:
Use shallow JSON-based distinctness to retain diverse representatives within a behavior class.

Acceptance:
- Obvious near-duplicate representatives are not retained indefinitely.
- A more distinct new representative can evict a weaker retained representative.
close_reason: Landed via merge 165e0a92. Diversity-based eviction for per-behavior representatives in interesting_pool.rs.

title: Stored inputs cap per behavior
description:
Limit retained representative inputs per discovered behavior class.

Acceptance:
- At most 10 representatives are retained per behavior class.
- The first representative for a behavior class is preserved unless replaced by a more distinct retained set policy.

title: Scheduler state tracks uncovered
description:
Persist uncovered-target scheduler state for discovered functions.

Acceptance:
- Discovered uncovered targets receive zeroed attempt state immediately.
- Only uncovered targets are retained durably; covered targets age out on normal flush.
close_reason: Landed via merge 153f31f6. Uncovered branch tracking in scheduler state with exhaustion gate and flush policy in scan_orchestrator.rs.

title: Scheduler state splits by mode
description:
Partition scheduler state by active coverage mode so branch mode and MC/DC mode do not contaminate each other.

Acceptance:
- Cooldown and attempt history are kept separate for branch mode and MC/DC mode.
- Only one coverage mode is active in a single run.
close_reason: Landed via merge 521bacf5. Scheduler state partitioned by coverage mode; branch exhaustion doesn't contaminate MC/DC lookups.

title: Explore persists batch outcomes
description:
Return batch-level outcome summaries from workers so the global scheduler can update ranking using function-level evidence.

Acceptance:
- Worker summaries include executions run, coverage before and after, uncovered remaining, new classes, new retained inputs, and failures.
- Global scheduling does not depend on per-generator internals.

title: Explore auto-selects workers
description:
Choose execution worker count automatically by default while allowing explicit user override.

Acceptance:
- Automatic worker selection applies to explore generally, not just long runs.
- Discovery remains a separate single worker.
close_reason: Landed via merge 24800fe2. Renamed --jobs to --workers with hidden alias for backward compat.

title: Explore enqueues known targets
description:
Only schedule functions once concrete uncovered targets are known, and enqueue all known uncovered targets for each discovered function.

Acceptance:
- Functions do not enter execution as unknown speculative work.
- Discovery computes nesting depth for known uncovered targets up front.

title: Explore penalizes repeated misses
description:
Demote functions that repeatedly consume batches without producing useful progress.

Acceptance:
- Attempt penalty is applied at batch granularity, not per execution.
- Periodic summaries show numeric attempt penalties.

title: Explore cools recent functions
description:
Apply a recency cooldown to recently active functions so breadth-first progress is preserved across the repo.

Acceptance:
- Cooldown is tracked per function and decays by completed batches, not wall clock time.
- Periodic summaries show numeric cooldown scores.

title: Explore reports fallback state
description:
Report when normal frontier work is exhausted and explore is revisiting hard targets and mutating existing corpus.

Acceptance:
- Summaries explicitly describe fallback behavior when easy frontier work is exhausted.
- Fallback reporting does not imply a separate scheduler mode.

title: Explore shows active vs queued
description:
Show active and queued function work in periodic summaries.

Acceptance:
- Summaries show active and queued counts.
- Summaries show top named active and queued functions.

title: shatter-go: stack overflow in type analyzer — infinite recursion on struct types
description:
The Go analyzer crashes with a goroutine stack overflow (1 GB limit) when analyzing files with cyclical struct type references. Triggers on `api/internal/nl/prompt.go`.

```
runtime: goroutine stack exceeds 1000000000-byte limit
fatal error: stack overflow
```

The infinite loop occurs between `structTypeInfo` and `goTypeToTypeInfoWithContext` in `shatter-go/protocol/analyzer.go`. The cycle repeats:
- `goTypeToTypeInfoWithContext` → `structTypeInfo` (line 681)
- `structTypeInfo` → `goTypeToTypeInfoWithContext` (line 584/586)

**Root cause:** Type resolution doesn't guard against cycles in struct types, likely via `interface{}` fields, self-referencing `*go/types.Named`, or embedded types that refer back to themselves.

**Fix:** Add a visited-set (e.g., `map[types.Type]bool`) parameter to both functions to detect cycles. When a type is seen a second time, return a reference/stub instead of recursing. See stdlib's `go/types.NewMethodSet` for the pattern.

**Impact:** Only 1 of 137 targets hit this, but it's a hard crash. Any codebase with struct types that form reference cycles through interfaces or pointer indirection will trigger it.

See: /home/ketan/project/kapow/shatter-artifacts/bug-3-stack-overflow-type-recursion.md

title: shatter-go: deserialization error — missing field 'fields' (5 targets)
description:
The Go analyzer omits the `fields` key when a struct has no exported fields, or when the type is an alias/wrapper. The Rust deserializer expects `fields` unconditionally.

Affects 5 targets, including `api/internal/config/config.go` and `api/internal/fixture/fixture.go`. Error:
```
Error: analyze failed: failed to deserialize response: missing field `fields` at line 1 column XXXXX
```

**Root cause:** Same as bug 1 — Go analyzer omits optional fields that should default to empty collections.

**Fix:** Add `#[serde(default)]` to the `fields` field in the Rust analyzer response struct. A single pass through all collection-typed fields would likely fix both this bug and bug 1.

**Related:** Bug 1 (missing field 'args') has the same root cause.

See: /home/ketan/project/kapow/shatter-artifacts/bug-2-deserialization-missing-fields.md

title: shatter-go: deserialization error — missing field 'args' (19 targets)
description:
The Go analyzer emits function descriptors where some functions have zero parameters, and the `args` field is either omitted or set to null instead of `[]`. The Rust frontend cannot deserialize these responses.

Affects 19 targets across api/ (14) and tools/ (5) modules. All fail with:
```
Error: analyze failed: failed to deserialize response: missing field `args` at line 1 column XXXXX
```

**Root cause:** Go analyzer omits `args` field for zero-parameter functions.

**Fix:** Add `#[serde(default)]` to the `args` field in the Rust analyzer response struct, or ensure the Go analyzer always emits `"args": []`.

**Related:** Bug 2 (missing field 'fields') has the same root cause — both are optional fields that the Go analyzer sometimes omits.

See: /home/ketan/project/kapow/shatter-artifacts/bug-1-deserialization-missing-args.md

title: Embedded frontend cache writes
description:
Embedded frontend extraction currently fails when the default cache location is unwritable, which causes helper tests and npx task smoke to fail with read-only filesystem errors.

Repro in a constrained environment:
- cargo test fails in shatter-cli helper tests with failed to write frontend bundle / failed to write go frontend binary
- npx task smoke fails before exploration for the same reason

Expected behavior:
Frontend extraction uses a writable cache directory or returns a deterministic, testable fallback when the default location is not writable.

Acceptance criteria:
- Add a regression test that exercises unwritable default cache behavior.
- Helper tests pass under the constrained setup.
- npx task smoke no longer fails for this cache-write reason.

title: Project root Go fixture stale
description: The full cargo test sweep on 2026-04-05 exposed shatter-core/src/project.rs::detects_go_project_via_go_mod using examples/go/01-simple-branch.go, but examples/go no longer exists in this repo. The test now walks up to the repo root and incorrectly detects TypeScript via ./package.json. Update the fixture path or test setup to reflect the current Go project layout without depending on removed examples.

title: Examples external checkout
description: Remove the examples git submodule and make Shatter rely on the separate https://github.com/shatterproof-ai/examples repository via external checkouts under /tmp. Update scripts, tasks, tests, Docker demo flows, and documentation so they no longer assume examples/ exists inside this repo. Use latest main/HEAD from the external repo rather than a pinned submodule SHA, with SHATTER_EXAMPLES_DIR as an override for advanced/local use.
acceptance_criteria: The repo no longer tracks examples as a submodule; walkthrough/gauntlet/task/test flows resolve examples from an external checkout under /tmp by default; Docker/demo/perf paths no longer mount or read repo-local examples/; contributor and CI docs no longer instruct submodule init or recurse-submodules; SHATTER_EXAMPLES_DIR override is documented and supported where needed.

title: Axum handler adapter
description:
# Frontend / Protocol Issue

## Protocol-Visible Behavior

- Does this change the wire format or semantics of any protocol message? (yes)
- If yes, which commands or fields are affected: Invocation and observation behavior for Axum handlers
- Is this change backward-compatible? (yes)

## Parity Impact

Which parallel code paths are affected? Check all that apply and confirm the parallel path is updated too.

- [ ] `buildSymExpr` (instrumentor.ts) — update `buildSymExprWithFlow` to handle the same node type
- [ ] `buildSymExprWithFlow` (instrumentor.ts) — update `buildSymExpr` to handle the same node type
- [ ] Random explorer (`shatter-core/src/explorer.rs`) — verify concolic orchestrator (`orchestrator.rs`) gets the same change
- [ ] Concolic orchestrator (`shatter-core/src/orchestrator.rs`) — verify random explorer (`explorer.rs`) gets the same change
- [ ] CLI wiring in `main.rs` — verify both `--concolic` and default explorer paths receive the new config or flag

## Default Behavior

- Is new functionality enabled by default? (no)
- If no, what flag or config option enables it: Enabled when the Axum handler adapter is selected

## Required Test Updates

- [ ] Unit tests added or updated
- [ ] Property tests (proptest / fast-check / rapid) added for any new public function
- [ ] E2E concolic tests verified (`cargo test --test e2e_concolic`)
- [ ] Conformance tests verified (`npx task conformance`) — required if protocol-visible
- [ ] Walkthrough updated (`demo/walkthrough.sh`) if the compact demo narrative changes
- [ ] Gauntlet updated (`demo/gauntlet.sh`) or equivalent targeted coverage added for non-demo CLI changes

## Contract Updates

- Are runtime contracts (`#[requires]` / `#[ensures]`) needed? (no)
- If yes, identify the trust boundary that justifies them: N/A
- Confirm all three qualifying criteria are met: N/A

## Notes

Implement a Rust adapter that executes Axum handlers against synthetic request, state, and extractor inputs, then normalizes the resulting response behavior in HTTP terms.
acceptance_criteria:
- Axum handlers run against a synthetic request/state/extractor harness.
- Exploration results record normalized HTTP response behavior instead of raw extractor structs.
- Axum support composes with async runtime handling without moving framework semantics into core.
